1.1: Blessed is the man who does not walk in the counsel of the wicked, nor stand in the way of sinners, nor sit in the seat of scoffers.
1.2: But his delight is in the instruction of the LORD, and on his instruction he meditates day and night.
1.3: He is like a tree planted by streams of water that yields its fruit in its season, and its leaf does not wither. In all that he does, he prospers.
1.4: The wicked are not so, but are like chaff that the wind drives away.
1.5: Therefore the wicked shall not stand in the judgment, nor sinners in the assembly of the righteous.
1.6: For the LORD watches over the way of the righteous, but the way of the wicked will perish.

2.1: Why do the nations rage, and the peoples plot in vain?
2.2: The kings of the earth rise up, and the rulers conspire together against the LORD and against his Anointed One.
2.3: "Let us tear apart their bonds and throw off their cords from us."
2.4: He who sits in the heavens laughs; the Lord scoffs at them.
2.5: Then he will speak to them in his wrath and terrify them in his burning anger:
2.6: "But I have installed my King on Zion, my holy hill."
2.7: I will proclaim the LORD's decree: he said to me, 'You are my Son; today I have begotten you.'
2.8: Ask me, and I will give the nations as your inheritance and the ends of the earth as your possession.
2.9: You shall rule them with an iron rod and shatter them like a clay vessel.
2.10: Now then, be wise, O kings; take warning, O rulers of the earth.
2.11: Serve the LORD with reverent fear, and celebrate with trembling.
2.12: Pay homage to the Son, lest he be angry and you perish from his path, for his wrath can ignite quickly. Blessed are all who take refuge in him.

3.1: A Psalm of David, when he fled from Absalom his son. O LORD, how many are my foes! Many are rising against me.
3.2: Many are saying of me, 'There is no salvation for him in God.' Selah
3.3: But you, O LORD, are a shield around me, my glory, and the one who lifts my head.
3.4: I cried out to the LORD, and he answered me from his holy hill. Selah
3.5: I lay down and slept; I awoke again, for the LORD sustained me.
3.6: I will not fear the tens of thousands who have surrounded me on every side.
3.7: Rise up, O LORD! Save me, O my God! For you strike all my enemies on the cheek; you shatter the teeth of the wicked.
3.8: Salvation belongs to the LORD; your blessing be upon your people. Selah

4.1: To the choirmaster: with stringed instruments. A Psalm of David. Answer me when I call, O God of my righteousness! You have freed me when I was in distress. Be gracious to me and hear my prayer.
4.2: O men, how long will you turn my honor to shame? How long will you love what is worthless and pursue lies? Selah
4.3: But know that the LORD has set apart the faithful for himself; the LORD hears when I call to him.
4.4: Tremble with awe and do not sin; search your hearts in silence on your beds. Selah
4.5: Offer right sacrifices, and put your trust in the LORD.
4.6: Many say, 'Who will show us any good?' Let the light of your face shine on us, O LORD!
4.7: You have placed more joy in my heart than they have when their grain and wine overflow.
4.8: In peace I will lie down and sleep, for you alone, O LORD, make me dwell in safety.

5.1: To the choirmaster: for the flutes. A Psalm of David. Give ear to my words, O LORD; consider my groaning.
5.2: Attend to the sound of my cry, my King and my God, for to you I pray.
5.3: O LORD, in the morning you hear my voice; in the morning I lay my prayer before you and wait.
5.4: For you are not a God who takes pleasure in wickedness; evil cannot dwell with you.
5.5: The arrogant cannot stand in your sight; you hate all who practice evil.
5.6: You destroy those who speak falsehood; the LORD abhors the violent and the deceitful.
5.7: But I, through the abundance of your steadfast love, will come into your house. I will bow down toward your holy temple in reverence of you.
5.8: Lead me, O LORD, in your righteousness because of those who lie in wait for me; make your way level before me.
5.9: For there is nothing reliable in their mouth; their inward part is ruin; their throat is an open grave; they flatter with their tongue.
5.10: Hold them guilty, O God; let them fall by their own schemes; drive them away for the multitude of their rebellions, for they have defied you.
5.11: But let all who take refuge in you rejoice; let them shout for joy forever. Spread your protection over them, and let those who love your name exult in you.
5.12: For you, O LORD, bless the righteous; you surround him with favor as with a shield.

6.1: To the choirmaster: with stringed instruments, according to the Sheminith. A Psalm of David. O LORD, do not rebuke me in your anger, nor discipline me in your fury.
6.2: Be gracious to me, O LORD, for I am weak; heal me, O LORD, for my bones are in agony.
6.3: My whole being is deeply troubled. But you, O LORD—how long?
6.4: Turn back, O LORD, rescue my life; save me for the sake of your steadfast love.
6.5: For among the dead no one remembers you; in Sheol no one praises you.
6.6: I am exhausted with my moaning; every night I drench my bed with tears; I soak my couch with my weeping.
6.7: My eye has grown dim from grief; it wastes away because of all my foes.
6.8: Depart from me, all you who do evil, for the LORD has heard the sound of my weeping.
6.9: The LORD has heard my supplication; the LORD receives my prayer.
6.10: All my enemies shall be ashamed and greatly disturbed; they shall turn back and be disgraced in an instant.

7.1: A Shiggaion of David, sung to the LORD concerning Cush the Benjaminite. O LORD my God, in you I take refuge; save me and deliver me from all my pursuers,
7.2: lest they tear my soul apart like a lion, mauling it with no one to rescue.
7.3: O LORD my God, if I have done this — if there is guilt on my hands —
7.4: if I have repaid evil to one who was at peace with me, or stripped my enemy without cause —
7.5: then let the enemy pursue and overtake me, trample my life into the ground, and lay my honor in the dust. Selah
7.6: Rise up, O LORD, in your anger; rouse yourself against the rage of my enemies. Bestir yourself — you have decreed judgment!
7.7: Let the assembly of the peoples gather around you, and take your seat on high above them.
7.8: The LORD judges the peoples; vindicate me, O LORD, according to my righteousness and the integrity that is in me.
7.9: Let the wickedness of the wicked come to an end and establish the righteous, for the righteous God searches hearts and minds.
7.10: My shield is God, the Most High, who saves those who are upright in heart.
7.11: God is a righteous judge, a God who expresses indignation every day.
7.12: If a man does not repent, God will sharpen his sword; he has bent his bow and strung it.
7.13: He has prepared deadly weapons against the persecutors; his arrows will be fiery shafts.
7.14: Look: the wicked person conceives evil, becomes pregnant with mischief, and gives birth to deception.
7.15: He dug a pit, made it deep, and fell into the very pit he made.
7.16: His mischief comes back on his own head; his violence falls down on his own skull.
7.17: I will give thanks to the LORD for his righteousness, and sing praise to the name of the LORD Most High.

8.1: A Psalm of David, for the choir director, to be played on the Gittith. O LORD our Lord, how majestic is your name throughout all the earth! You have placed your glory above the heavens.
8.2: From the mouths of children and nursing infants you have ordained praise, because of your enemies, to silence the foe and the avenger.
8.3: When I look at your heavens, the work of your fingers — the moon and the stars that you have established —
8.4: what is humanity that you give it a thought? What is a human being that you attend to him?
8.5: Yet you have made him just a little lower than the heavenly beings, and crowned him with glory and honor.
8.6: You have given him dominion over the works of your hands; you have placed everything under his feet:
8.7: all sheep and cattle, and also the wild animals,
8.8: the birds of the sky and the fish of the sea, whatever moves along the ocean's currents.
8.9: O LORD our Lord, how majestic is your name throughout all the earth!

9.1: A Psalm of David, for the choir director, set to 'Muth-labben.' I will give thanks to you, LORD, with my whole heart; I will tell of all your wonderful works.
9.2: I will rejoice and be glad in you; I will sing praise to your name, O Most High.
9.3: When my enemies turn back, they stumble and perish at your presence.
9.4: For you upheld my right and my cause; you sat on the throne, judging with righteousness.
9.5: You rebuked the nations and destroyed the wicked; you blotted out their name forever and ever.
9.6: The enemy has come to an end in perpetual ruin; you uprooted their cities; their very memory has perished.
9.7: But the LORD sits enthroned forever; he has set his throne for judgment.
9.8: He judges the world with righteousness; he rules the peoples with equity.
9.9: The LORD is a refuge for the oppressed, a stronghold in times of trouble.
9.10: Those who know your name will trust in you, for you, LORD, have never abandoned those who seek you.
9.11: Sing praises to the LORD who dwells in Zion; declare his deeds among the nations!
9.12: For the one who avenges bloodshed is mindful of them; he does not forget the cry of the humble.
9.13: Be gracious to me, O LORD! See the suffering I endure from those who hate me — you who lift me back from the very gates of death —
9.14: so that I may declare all your praises in the gates of daughter Zion, and rejoice in your salvation.
9.15: The nations have sunk in the pit they made; their own foot was caught in the net they concealed.
9.16: The LORD has made himself known by executing judgment; the wicked are ensnared by the work of their own hands. Higgaion. Selah
9.17: The wicked will return to Sheol, all the nations that forget God.
9.18: For the needy will not always be forgotten, nor will the hope of the poor perish forever.
9.19: Rise up, O LORD! Let not man have his way; let the nations be judged in your presence!
9.20: Strike them with fear, O LORD! Let the nations know they are only human. Selah

10.1: Why, O LORD, do you stand at a distance? Why do you hide yourself in times of trouble?
10.2: In his arrogance the wicked person persecutes the poor; let him be caught in the very schemes he has devised.
10.3: For the wicked person boasts of the cravings of his heart, and the greedy curses and spurns the LORD.
10.4: In his arrogance the wicked person does not seek God; in all his thinking, there is no place for God.
10.5: His ways always succeed; your judgments are too high for him to perceive; he sneers at all his enemies.
10.6: He says in his heart, 'I will never be shaken; through all generations I will face no trouble.'
10.7: His mouth is full of cursing, deceit, and fraud; under his tongue are trouble and wickedness.
10.8: He lurks in ambush near the villages; in hiding places he kills the innocent. His eyes are set to watch for the helpless.
10.9: He crouches like a lion in his lair; he lies in wait to catch the poor; he catches the poor by dragging them into his net.
10.10: He crouches and springs, and the helpless fall by his strength.
10.11: He says in his heart, 'God has forgotten; he has hidden his face; he will never notice.'
10.12: Rise up, O LORD! O God, lift up your hand! Do not forget the afflicted!
10.13: Why does the wicked person despise God and say in his heart, 'You won't call me to account'?
10.14: But you do see! You observe trouble and grief and take it into your hands. The helpless person entrusts himself to you; you have always been the helper of the fatherless.
10.15: Break the arm of the wicked and the evildoer; call his wickedness to account until there is none left.
10.16: The LORD is King for ever and ever; the nations will vanish from his land.
10.17: LORD, you have heard the desire of the humble; you will strengthen their heart; you will turn your ear
10.18: to vindicate the fatherless and the oppressed, so that mortal man will no longer strike terror.

11.1: A Psalm of David, for the choir director. In the LORD I take refuge. How can you say to me, 'Fly to your mountain like a bird'?
11.2: For look — the wicked string the bow; they have set their arrows on the string to shoot in the darkness at the upright in heart.
11.3: If the foundations are destroyed, what can the righteous do?
11.4: The LORD is in his holy temple; the LORD's throne is in heaven. His eyes observe; his eyelids examine the children of mankind.
11.5: The LORD tests the righteous, but his soul hates the wicked and the one who loves violence.
11.6: Let him rain down coals on the wicked; fire and sulfur and a scorching wind will be the lot assigned to their cup.
11.7: For the LORD is righteous; he loves righteous deeds; the upright will see his face.

12.1: A Psalm of David, for the choir director, to be played on the Sheminith — the eight-stringed instrument. Help us, LORD! For the godly are gone; the faithful have vanished from among humanity.
12.2: Everyone lies to his neighbor; they speak with flattering lips and a divided heart.
12.3: May the LORD cut off all flattering lips and every tongue that boasts,
12.4: those who say, 'With our words we will prevail; our lips are our own — who can lord it over us?'
12.5: 'Because the poor are oppressed and the needy groan, I will now arise,' says the LORD. 'I will set them in the safety they long for.'
12.6: The words of the LORD are pure words, like silver refined in a furnace in the ground, purified seven times.
12.7: You, O LORD, will keep them; you will protect us from this generation forever.
12.8: The wicked parade on every side when what is vile is honored among humanity.

13.1: For the director of music. A Psalm of David. How long, LORD? Will you forget me forever? How long will you hide your face from me?
13.2: How long must I wrestle with my thoughts, bearing sorrow in my heart every day? How long will my enemy triumph over me?
13.3: Look at me and answer me, O LORD my God; give light to my eyes, lest I fall into the sleep of death,
13.4: lest my enemy say, 'I have overcome him,' and my foes rejoice when I am brought down.
13.5: But I have trusted in your steadfast love; my heart will rejoice in your salvation.
13.6: I will sing to the LORD, for he has been generous to me.

14.1: For the director of music. A Psalm of David. The fool says in his heart, 'There is no God.' They are corrupt; they have done vile deeds; there is no one who does good.
14.2: The LORD looks down from heaven on all people to see if there are any who understand, who seek God.
14.3: All have turned aside; all have become corrupt together; there is no one who does good, not even one.
14.4: Do none of the workers of evil understand—those who devour my people as they eat bread, and who do not call on the LORD?
14.5: There they were filled with fear, for God is with the righteous generation.
14.6: You would shame the plans of the poor, but the LORD is their refuge.
14.7: Oh, that salvation for Israel would come from Zion! When the LORD restores the fortunes of his people, Jacob will rejoice and Israel will be glad.

15.1: A Psalm of David. LORD, who may dwell in your sanctuary? Who may live on your holy mountain?
15.2: The one who walks blamelessly, does what is right, and speaks truth from the heart;
15.3: who does not slander with the tongue, does no harm to a neighbor, and takes up no reproach against a friend;
15.4: who despises the contemptible but honors those who fear the LORD; who keeps an oath even when it costs them and does not go back on it;
15.5: who does not lend money at interest or take a bribe against the innocent. Whoever does these things will never be shaken.

16.1: A Miktam of David. Keep me safe, O God, for in you I take shelter.
16.2: I said to the LORD, 'You are my Lord; I have no good apart from you.'
16.3: As for the holy ones who are in the land, they are the noble—in them is all my delight.
16.4: The sorrows of those who chase other gods will multiply; I will not pour out their libations of blood or take their names on my lips.
16.5: The LORD is my allotted portion and my cup; you secure my future.
16.6: The boundary lines have fallen for me in pleasant places; truly I have a beautiful inheritance.
16.7: I will praise the LORD who counsels me; even at night my inmost self instructs me.
16.8: I have set the LORD always before me; because he is at my right hand, I will not be shaken.
16.9: Therefore my heart is glad and my spirit rejoices; my body also rests secure.
16.10: For you will not leave my soul in Sheol, or let your Holy One see decay.
16.11: You will make known to me the path of life; in your presence is complete joy; at your right hand are pleasures that last forever.

17.1: A Prayer of David. Hear a just cause, O LORD; listen to my cry; give ear to my prayer from lips without deceit.
17.2: Let my vindication come from you; let your eyes behold what is just.
17.3: You have tested my heart; you have examined me through the night; you have tried me and found nothing; I have resolved that my mouth will not sin.
17.4: Regarding human deeds, by the word of your lips I have avoided the ways of the violent.
17.5: My steps have kept to your paths; my feet have not stumbled.
17.6: I call on you, for you will answer me, O God; turn your ear to me; hear my words.
17.7: Display your wonderful steadfast love, O Savior of those who seek refuge from their enemies by your right hand.
17.8: Guard me as the apple of your eye; hide me in the shadow of your wings,
17.9: from the wicked who assail me, from my deadly enemies who encircle me.
17.10: Their hearts are closed to compassion; their mouths speak with arrogance.
17.11: Now they surround our every step; they watch for a chance to bring us to the ground.
17.12: He is like a lion eager for prey, like a young lion crouching in ambush.
17.13: Arise, LORD! Confront him! Bring him down! Rescue me from the wicked by your sword,
17.14: by your hand, LORD, from those whose portion is in this present life. May they be filled with what you store up for them; may their children be satisfied and leave their surplus to their children's children.
17.15: As for me, I will behold your face in righteousness; when I awake I will be satisfied with your likeness.

18.1: For the director of music. Of David the servant of the LORD, who sang to the LORD the words of this song when the LORD rescued him from all his enemies and from Saul. He said: I love you, LORD, my strength.
18.2: The LORD is my rock, my fortress, and my rescuer; my God is my rock in whom I take refuge—my shield, the horn of my salvation, my stronghold.
18.3: I called to the LORD, who is worthy of all praise, and I am saved from my enemies.
18.4: The cords of death wrapped around me; the floods of chaos overwhelmed me;
18.5: the ropes of Sheol entangled me; the snares of death were set before me.
18.6: In my distress I called to the LORD; I cried to my God for help. From his temple he heard my voice, and my cry before him came into his ears.
18.7: The earth shook and trembled; the foundations of the mountains quaked and shook, for he was angry.
18.8: Smoke poured from his nostrils and consuming fire from his mouth; blazing coals ignited before him.
18.9: He bowed the heavens and came down; dark cloud was under his feet.
18.10: He mounted a cherub and flew; he soared on the wings of the wind.
18.11: He made darkness his shelter, his canopy of dark clouds heavy with rain.
18.12: From the brightness of his presence his clouds burst forth with hailstones and coals of fire.
18.13: The LORD thundered from heaven; the Most High raised his voice amid hailstones and coals of fire.
18.14: He shot his arrows and scattered them; he hurled lightning bolts and threw them into confusion.
18.15: The channels of the sea were exposed and the foundations of the world were laid bare at your rebuke, O LORD, at the blast of the breath of your nostrils.
18.16: He reached down from on high and took hold of me; he pulled me out of deep waters.
18.17: He rescued me from my powerful enemy, from those who hated me—for they were stronger than I was.
18.18: They confronted me in the day of my disaster, but the LORD was my support.
18.19: He brought me out into an open place; he rescued me, because he was pleased with me.
18.20: The LORD rewarded me according to my righteousness; he repaid me according to the cleanness of my hands.
18.21: For I have kept to the LORD's ways and have not turned away from my God in rebellion.
18.22: All his rules were set before me, and I did not set aside his statutes.
18.23: I was blameless before him and kept myself from wrongdoing.
18.24: So the LORD rewarded me according to my righteousness, according to the cleanness of my hands in his eyes.
18.25: To the faithful you show yourself faithful; to the blameless you show yourself blameless;
18.26: to the pure you show yourself pure; but to the devious you show yourself cunning.
18.27: For you save an afflicted people, but proud eyes you humble.
18.28: For you light my lamp; the LORD my God brightens my darkness.
18.29: For by your help I can charge against a raiding party; by my God I can scale a wall.
18.30: This God—his way is perfect; the LORD's word is refined and true; he is a shield to all who shelter in him.
18.31: For who is God besides the LORD? And who is a rock except our God?
18.32: It is God who arms me with strength and makes my way perfect—
18.33: he makes my feet like the feet of a deer and sets me securely on the heights,
18.34: he trains my hands for battle; my arms can bend a bow of bronze.
18.35: You have given me the shield of your salvation; your right hand held me up, and your gentleness made me great.
18.36: You gave wide room for my steps beneath me, and my feet did not slip.
18.37: I pursued my enemies and overtook them and did not turn back until they were finished.
18.38: I struck them down so that they could not rise; they fell under my feet.
18.39: For you armed me with strength for the battle; you brought down under me those who rose against me.
18.40: You made my enemies turn their backs in retreat, and I destroyed those who hated me.
18.41: They cried for help, but there was no one to save them—they even cried to the LORD, but he did not answer them.
18.42: I ground them fine as windblown dust; I threw them out like mud from the streets.
18.43: You saved me from conflicts with my own people; you made me head of the nations; people I had not known served me.
18.44: As soon as they heard of me, they obeyed; foreigners submitted to me.
18.45: Foreigners lost their courage and came out of their fortresses trembling.
18.46: The LORD lives! Blessed be my Rock! Exalted be the God of my salvation—
18.47: the God who gives me justice and subdues peoples under me,
18.48: who rescued me from my enemies—you lifted me above those who rose against me, you saved me from the violent man.
18.49: Therefore I will give you thanks, O LORD, among the nations, and sing praises to your name.
18.50: He gives great salvation to his king and shows steadfast love to his anointed—to David and his descendants forever.

19.1: To the choirmaster. A Psalm of David. The heavens declare the glory of God, and the expanse proclaims his handiwork.
19.2: Day after day pours out its speech, and night after night reveals knowledge.
19.3: There is no speech, there are no words—their voice cannot be heard.
19.4: Yet their proclamation goes out through all the earth, and their message to the ends of the world. In the heavens he has pitched a tent for the sun,
19.5: which rises like a bridegroom leaving his chamber, and rejoices like a champion eager to run his course.
19.6: Its rising is from one end of the heavens, and its circuit reaches the other end; nothing is hidden from its heat.
19.7: The instruction of the LORD is perfect, reviving the soul; the testimony of the LORD is trustworthy, making the simple wise.
19.8: The precepts of the LORD are right, bringing joy to the heart; the commandment of the LORD is radiant, enlightening the eyes.
19.9: The fear of the LORD is pure, enduring forever; the judgments of the LORD are true and altogether righteous.
19.10: They are more desirable than gold, than great quantities of finest gold; and sweeter than honey, than honey dripping from the comb.
19.11: Moreover, your servant is warned by them; in keeping them there is great reward.
19.12: Who can understand his own errors? Cleanse me from sins I cannot see.
19.13: Keep your servant also from willful sins; let them not have mastery over me. Then I will be blameless and clean of great rebellion.
19.14: May the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart be pleasing in your sight, O LORD, my rock and my redeemer.

20.1: To the choirmaster. A Psalm of David. May the LORD answer you in your day of trouble! May the name of the God of Jacob set you on high!
20.2: May he send you help from his sanctuary and support you from Zion!
20.3: May he remember all your offerings and accept your burnt sacrifices! Selah
20.4: May he grant you your heart's desire and fulfill all your plans!
20.5: May we shout for joy at your victory, and in the name of our God raise our banners! May the LORD fulfill all your requests!
20.6: Now I know that the LORD saves his anointed one; he answers him from his holy heaven with the saving power of his right hand.
20.7: Some boast in chariots and some in horses, but we will boast in the name of the LORD our God.
20.8: They collapse and fall, but we rise and stand firm.
20.9: LORD, give victory to the king! Answer us when we call.

21.1: To the choirmaster. A Psalm of David. The king rejoices in your strength, LORD, and in your salvation how greatly he exults!
21.2: You have given him his heart's desire and have not withheld what his lips requested. Selah
21.3: For you meet him with blessings of goodness and set a crown of finest gold upon his head.
21.4: He asked you for life, and you gave it to him—length of days forever and ever.
21.5: Through your salvation his glory is great; you have bestowed splendor and majesty upon him.
21.6: For you make him most blessed forever; you fill him with gladness in your presence.
21.7: For the king trusts in the LORD, and through the steadfast love of the Most High he will not be shaken.
21.8: Your hand will reach all your enemies; your right hand will seize those who hate you.
21.9: You will make them like a burning oven in the time of your anger; the LORD in his wrath will swallow them up, and fire will devour them.
21.10: You will destroy their offspring from the earth and their descendants from among mankind.
21.11: For they intended evil against you and devised a plot they cannot carry out.
21.12: You will put them to flight as you aim your bow at their faces.
21.13: Rise in your strength, O LORD! We will sing and praise your mighty deeds.

22.1: To the choirmaster: according to the Doe of the Dawn. A Psalm of David. My God, my God, why have you forsaken me? Why are you so far from saving me, so far from my cries of anguish?
22.2: O my God, I cry out by day, but you do not answer; by night I find no relief.
22.3: But you are holy, enthroned on the praises of Israel.
22.4: In you our ancestors put their trust; they trusted, and you rescued them.
22.5: They cried to you and were delivered; they trusted in you and were not put to shame.
22.6: But I am a worm and not a man, scorned by everyone and despised by the people.
22.7: All who see me mock me; they sneer and shake their heads:
22.8: "He trusted in the LORD; let the LORD rescue him. Let him deliver him, since he delights in him!"
22.9: Yet you drew me out of the womb; you made me trust in you at my mother's breasts.
22.10: Upon you I was cast from birth; from my mother's womb you have been my God.
22.11: Do not be far from me, for trouble is near and there is no one to help.
22.12: Many bulls surround me; powerful bulls of Bashan encircle me;
22.13: they open wide their jaws against me like a roaring, ravening lion.
22.14: I am poured out like water, and all my bones are disjointed; my heart has turned to wax, melting within me.
22.15: My strength is dried up like a fragment of pottery; my tongue sticks to the roof of my mouth; you lay me in the dust of death.
22.16: For dogs surround me; a mob of evildoers encircles me; they have pierced my hands and feet —
22.17: I can count all my bones; they stare and gloat over me.
22.18: They divide my garments among themselves and cast lots for my clothing.
22.19: But you, LORD, do not stay far away! You are my strength; come quickly to help me!
22.20: Rescue my life from the sword, my precious soul from the clutch of the dog!
22.21: Rescue me from the lion's jaws! You have answered me from the horns of the wild oxen.
22.22: I will proclaim your name to my brothers; in the midst of the congregation I will praise you.
22.23: You who fear the LORD, praise him! All descendants of Jacob, honor him! Stand in awe of him, all descendants of Israel!
22.24: For he has not despised or scorned the suffering of the afflicted one; he has not hidden his face from him but listened when he cried out to him.
22.25: My praise in the great congregation comes from you; I will fulfill my vows before those who fear him.
22.26: The humble will eat and be satisfied; those who seek the LORD will praise him — may your hearts live forever!
22.27: All the ends of the earth will remember and turn to the LORD, and all the families of the nations will bow down before him.
22.28: For dominion belongs to the LORD, and he rules over the nations.
22.29: All the prosperous of the earth will feast and worship; before him all who go down to the dust will bow — even those who cannot preserve their own life.
22.30: Descendants will serve him; the Lord will be declared to the coming generation.
22.31: They will come and proclaim his saving acts to a people not yet born, declaring that he has done it.

23.1: A Psalm of David. The LORD is my shepherd; I shall lack nothing.
23.2: He makes me lie down in green pastures; he leads me beside quiet waters.
23.3: He restores my inner life; he guides me along right paths for the sake of his name.
23.4: Even though I walk through the valley of deepest darkness, I will fear no evil, for you are with me; your rod and your staff, they comfort me.
23.5: You set a table for me in full view of my enemies; you anoint my head with oil; my cup overflows.
23.6: Surely goodness and steadfast love will pursue me all the days of my life, and I will live in the house of the LORD forever.

24.1: A Psalm of David. The earth belongs to the LORD and all its fullness; the world and all who live in it.
24.2: For he founded it on the seas and established it above the rivers.
24.3: Who may ascend the mountain of the LORD? Who may stand in his holy dwelling?
24.4: The one with clean hands and a pure heart, who does not set his mind on worthless things or make deceitful promises.
24.5: He will receive blessing from the LORD and vindication from the God who saves him.
24.6: Such is the generation of those who seek him, who seek the face of the God of Jacob. Selah
24.7: Lift up your heads, you gates! Rise up, you ancient doors, so the King of glory may enter!
24.8: Who is this King of glory? The LORD, strong and mighty, the LORD, powerful in battle!
24.9: Lift up your heads, you gates! Rise up, you ancient doors, that the King of glory may enter!
24.10: Who is this King of glory? The LORD of Armies — he is the King of glory! Selah

31.1: For the director of music. A Psalm of David. In you, LORD, I take refuge; let me never be put to shame; rescue me by your righteousness.
31.2: Turn your ear to me; quickly rescue me. Be my rock of refuge, a strong fortress to save me.
31.3: For you are my rock and my fortress; lead me and guide me for your name's sake.
31.4: Free me from the trap they have secretly set for me, for you are my refuge.
31.5: Into your hand I commit my spirit; you have redeemed me, LORD, faithful God.
31.6: I despise those who cling to worthless idols; but I trust in the LORD.
31.7: I will be glad and rejoice in your steadfast love, for you have seen my distress and known the anguish of my soul;
31.8: you did not hand me over to the enemy; you set my feet in a wide-open place.
31.9: Have mercy on me, LORD, for I am in trouble; my eye wastes with grief—my soul and my body too.
31.10: For my life is exhausted by grief and my years by sighing; my strength fails because of my iniquity, and my bones waste away.
31.11: Among all my enemies I am an object of scorn, and especially to my neighbors; those who know me are afraid—when people see me in the street they flee.
31.12: I am as forgotten as a dead man; I have become like a shattered clay vessel.
31.13: I hear the rumors of the many—terror surrounds me—as they plot together against me, conspiring to take my life.
31.14: But I trusted in you, LORD; I said, 'You are my God.'
31.15: My times are in your hand; rescue me from my enemies and from those who pursue me.
31.16: Make your face shine on your servant; save me through your steadfast love.
31.17: LORD, let me not be put to shame, for I have called on you; let the wicked be ashamed—let them go silently down to Sheol.
31.18: Let the lying lips be struck silent—lips that speak against the righteous with arrogance and contempt.
31.19: How great is your goodness, which you have stored up for those who fear you and displayed for those who take shelter in you, in full view of everyone!
31.20: In the shelter of your presence you hide them from human plots; you conceal them in your tent from the quarreling of tongues.
31.21: Praise the LORD, for he displayed his wonderful steadfast love to me when I was like a man in a besieged city.
31.22: I said in my panic, 'I am cut off from your sight.' Yet you heard my cries for mercy when I called to you.
31.23: Love the LORD, all you his faithful people! The LORD protects the faithful and pays back the proud in full.
31.24: Be strong, and let your heart be courageous, all you who hope in the LORD!

32.1: A Maskil of David. Blessed is the one whose transgression is forgiven, whose sin is covered.
32.2: Blessed is the man to whom the LORD does not reckon iniquity, and in whose spirit there is no deceit.
32.3: When I kept silent about my sin, my bones wasted away through my constant groaning.
32.4: Day and night your hand pressed down on me; my strength dried up like moisture in the summer heat. Selah
32.5: I made my sin known to you and did not hide my iniquity; I said, 'I will confess my transgressions to the LORD,' and you forgave the guilt of my sin. Selah
32.6: Therefore let every faithful person pray to you while you may still be found; when the great floodwaters surge, they will not reach them.
32.7: You are my hiding place; you guard me from trouble; you surround me with songs of rescue. Selah
32.8: I will instruct you and show you the way you should walk; I will guide you with my eye on you.
32.9: Do not be like a horse or mule that lacks understanding and must be controlled with bit and bridle, or it will not come near you.
32.10: The wicked have many sorrows, but steadfast love surrounds those who trust in the LORD.
32.11: Be glad in the LORD and rejoice, you righteous people! Shout for joy, all who are upright in heart!

33.1: Shout for joy in the LORD, you righteous! Praise is beautiful from those who are upright.
33.2: Praise the LORD with the lyre; make music to him with the ten-stringed harp.
33.3: Sing him a new song; play with skill and shout for joy.
33.4: For the word of the LORD is right and true; all his works are done in faithfulness.
33.5: He loves righteousness and justice; the earth is full of the LORD's steadfast love.
33.6: The heavens were made by the word of the LORD, and all their host by the breath of his mouth.
33.7: He gathers the sea's waters like a heap; he stores the deep in reservoirs.
33.8: Let all the earth fear the LORD; let all who live in the world stand in awe of him.
33.9: For he spoke, and it came to be; he commanded, and it stood firm.
33.10: The LORD thwarts the purposes of the nations; he brings the plans of the peoples to nothing.
33.11: The counsel of the LORD stands forever, the purposes of his heart through all generations.
33.12: Blessed is the nation whose God is the LORD—the people he has chosen as his own possession.
33.13: The LORD looks down from heaven; he observes all humanity;
33.14: from his dwelling place he surveys all who live on the earth,
33.15: he who forms the hearts of all and considers everything they do.
33.16: No king is saved by the size of his army; no warrior is rescued by his own great strength.
33.17: A horse is a vain hope for victory; its great power cannot bring rescue.
33.18: The eye of the LORD is on those who fear him, on those who hope in his steadfast love,
33.19: to rescue them from death and to keep them alive in famine.
33.20: We wait for the LORD; he is our help and our shield.
33.21: Our hearts are glad in him, for we trust in his holy name.
33.22: Let your steadfast love, LORD, rest on us, as we have placed our hope in you.

25.1: A Psalm of David. To you, LORD, I lift up my soul.
25.2: O my God, in you I trust; do not let me be put to shame; do not let my enemies triumph over me.
25.3: Indeed, no one who waits for you will be put to shame; those who betray without reason will be the ones shamed.
25.4: Make your ways known to me, O LORD; teach me your paths.
25.5: Lead me in your faithfulness and teach me, for you are the God of my salvation; I wait for you all day long.
25.6: Remember your compassion, O LORD, and your steadfast love, for they have been from everlasting.
25.7: Do not hold against me the sins of my youth or my rebellions; according to your steadfast love, remember me—for your goodness' sake, O LORD.
25.8: The LORD is good and upright; therefore he teaches sinners the right way.
25.9: He guides the humble in justice and teaches the humble his way.
25.10: All the LORD's paths are steadfast love and faithfulness, for those who keep his covenant and his decrees.
25.11: For your name's sake, O LORD, forgive my guilt, for it is very great.
25.12: Who is the one who fears the LORD? The LORD will instruct him in the way he should take.
25.13: He will dwell in prosperity, and his children will inherit the land.
25.14: The LORD confides in those who fear him, and makes his covenant known to them.
25.15: My eyes are always toward the LORD, for he will free my feet from the trap.
25.16: Turn to me and be merciful to me, for I am alone and in distress.
25.17: The troubles of my heart have multiplied; bring me out of my anguish.
25.18: Look at my affliction and my trouble, and forgive all my sins.
25.19: Consider how many are my foes, and how violently they hate me.
25.20: Guard my soul and rescue me! Do not let me be put to shame, for I take shelter in you.
25.21: May integrity and uprightness protect me, as I wait for you.
25.22: Redeem Israel, O God, from all their troubles.

26.1: A Psalm of David. Vindicate me, O LORD, for I have walked in integrity and trusted in the LORD without faltering.
26.2: Test me, O LORD, and try me; examine my heart and my inmost self.
26.3: For your steadfast love is before my eyes, and I have conducted myself in your faithfulness.
26.4: I do not sit with deceitful men or associate with hypocrites.
26.5: I hate the company of evildoers and refuse to sit with the wicked.
26.6: I wash my hands in innocence and take my place at your altar, O LORD,
26.7: lifting my voice in thanksgiving and recounting all your wonderful works.
26.8: LORD, I love the dwelling place of your house and the place where your glory lives.
26.9: Do not take away my life with sinners or with those who shed blood,
26.10: in whose hands are evil schemes and whose right hands are full of bribes.
26.11: But as for me, I walk in my integrity; redeem me and show me your grace.
26.12: My foot stands on level ground; in the great congregation I will praise the LORD.

27.1: A Psalm of David. The LORD is my light and my salvation—whom shall I fear? The LORD is the fortress of my life—of whom shall I be afraid?
27.2: When evildoers came against me to devour my flesh—my adversaries and foes—they stumbled and fell.
27.3: Even if an army surrounds me, my heart will not fear; even if war breaks out against me, I will still be confident.
27.4: One thing I have asked of the LORD, and that I will pursue: to dwell in the house of the LORD all the days of my life, to gaze on the beauty of the LORD and to seek him in his temple.
27.5: For in the day of trouble he will hide me in his dwelling; he will conceal me under the shelter of his tent; he will set me securely on a rock.
27.6: And now my head will be raised above my enemies who surround me, and in his tent I will offer sacrifices with shouts of joy; I will sing and make music to the LORD.
27.7: Hear, O LORD, when I cry aloud; be merciful to me and answer me.
27.8: 'Seek my face'—so you have spoken. My heart answers you: 'Your face, LORD, I am seeking.'
27.9: Do not hide your face from me; do not turn your servant away in anger. You have been my help; do not abandon or forsake me, O God of my salvation.
27.10: Even if my father and mother abandon me, the LORD will receive me.
27.11: Teach me your way, O LORD, and lead me on a straight path because of my enemies.
27.12: Do not hand me over to the will of my adversaries, for false witnesses have arisen against me and breathe out cruelty.
27.13: I am certain I will see the goodness of the LORD in the land of the living.
27.14: Wait for the LORD! Be strong, and let your heart be courageous; wait for the LORD!

28.1: A Psalm of David. To you, O LORD, I call; my Rock, do not be deaf to me. If you remain silent, I will be like those who go down to the grave.
28.2: Hear my cry for mercy as I call to you for help, as I lift my hands toward your Most Holy Place.
28.3: Do not sweep me away with the wicked or with evildoers, who speak peace to their neighbors while plotting evil in their hearts.
28.4: Pay them back for what they have done, according to the evil of their deeds; repay them for the work of their hands; give them what they deserve.
28.5: Because they take no account of the LORD's works or of what his hands have done, he will pull them down and not rebuild them.
28.6: Praise the LORD! For he has heard my cry for mercy.
28.7: The LORD is my strength and my shield; my heart trusts in him and I am helped; my heart leaps for joy, and with my song I give thanks to him.
28.8: The LORD is the strength of his people; he is the saving fortress of his anointed one.
28.9: Save your people and bless your heritage; be their shepherd and carry them forever.

29.1: A Psalm of David. Ascribe to the LORD, O heavenly beings, ascribe to the LORD glory and strength.
29.2: Give the LORD the glory due his name; worship the LORD in the splendor of his holiness.
29.3: The voice of the LORD is over the waters; the God of glory thunders—the LORD, over mighty waters.
29.4: The voice of the LORD is powerful; the voice of the LORD is majestic.
29.5: The voice of the LORD shatters the cedars; the LORD shatters the cedars of Lebanon.
29.6: He makes Lebanon skip like a calf, and Sirion like a young wild ox.
29.7: The voice of the LORD strikes with flashes of lightning.
29.8: The voice of the LORD shakes the wilderness; the LORD shakes the wilderness of Kadesh.
29.9: The voice of the LORD causes the deer to writhe in labor and strips the forests bare; and in his temple all cry, 'Glory!'
29.10: The LORD sat enthroned over the Flood; the LORD sits enthroned as King forever.
29.11: The LORD gives strength to his people; the LORD blesses his people with peace.

30.1: A Psalm. A Song for the Dedication of the Temple. Of David. I exalt you, O LORD, for you have lifted me up and have not let my enemies gloat over me.
30.2: O LORD my God, I cried to you for help, and you healed me.
30.3: LORD, you brought my soul up from the realm of the dead; you restored my life from among those who descend to the pit.
30.4: Sing praises to the LORD, O you his faithful ones, and give thanks to his holy name.
30.5: For his anger lasts only a moment, but his favor lasts a lifetime; weeping may remain through the night, but joy arrives with the morning.
30.6: When I was prosperous, I said, 'I will never be shaken.'
30.7: By your favor, LORD, you had made my mountain stand firm; when you hid your face, I was terrified.
30.8: To you, LORD, I called out; to the Lord I made my appeal:
30.9: What is gained if I die and go down to the grave? Can the dust praise you? Can it proclaim your faithfulness?
30.10: Hear me, LORD, and have mercy on me! LORD, be my helper!
30.11: You have turned my mourning into dancing; you have removed my sackcloth and clothed me with joy,
30.12: so that my spirit may sing your praise and not be silent. O LORD my God, I will give you thanks forever.

34.1: A Psalm of David, when he pretended to be insane before Abimelech, who drove him away, and he left. I will bless the LORD at all times; his praise will always be on my lips.
34.2: My soul boasts in the LORD; the humble will hear and be glad.
34.3: Magnify the LORD with me, and let us exalt his name together.
34.4: I sought the LORD, and he answered me; he delivered me from all my fears.
34.5: Those who look to him are radiant with joy, and their faces will never be ashamed.
34.6: This poor man cried, and the LORD heard him; he saved him out of all his troubles.
34.7: The angel of the LORD encamps around those who fear him and rescues them.
34.8: Taste and see that the LORD is good! Blessed is the person who takes refuge in him!
34.9: Fear the LORD, you his holy people, for those who fear him lack nothing.
34.10: Young lions go hungry and lack food, but those who seek the LORD lack no good thing.
34.11: Come, children, listen to me; I will teach you the fear of the LORD.
34.12: Who is the person who desires life and loves long days to enjoy what is good?
34.13: Keep your tongue from evil and your lips from speaking lies.
34.14: Turn from evil and do good; seek peace and go after it.
34.15: The LORD's eyes are on the righteous and his ears are open to their cry.
34.16: The LORD turns his face against those who do evil, to erase all memory of them from the earth.
34.17: The righteous cry out, and the LORD hears; he delivers them from all their troubles.
34.18: The LORD is close to the brokenhearted and rescues those whose spirit is crushed.
34.19: The righteous face many afflictions, but the LORD delivers them from all of them.
34.20: He protects all his bones; not one of them will be broken.
34.21: Evil will slay the wicked, and those who hate the righteous will be condemned.
34.22: The LORD redeems the lives of his servants; none who take refuge in him will be condemned.

35.1: A Psalm of David. Contend, O LORD, against those who contend with me; fight against those who fight against me!
35.2: Take up your shield and armor and come to my aid!
35.3: Brandish the spear and block the path of those who pursue me; say to my soul, 'I am your deliverance!'
35.4: Let those who seek my life be put to shame and humiliated; let those who plot evil against me be turned back in confusion.
35.5: Let them be like chaff before the wind, with the angel of the LORD driving them off.
35.6: Make their path dark and treacherous, with the angel of the LORD pursuing them.
35.7: For without reason they concealed their net to trap me; without reason they dug a pit for my life.
35.8: May ruin overtake him when he least expects it; may the net he hid catch him; may he fall in it to his destruction.
35.9: Then my soul will rejoice in the LORD, exulting in his deliverance.
35.10: Every bone in my body will say, 'LORD, who is like you? Who rescues the poor from those who overpower them, the poor and needy from those who rob them?'
35.11: Malicious witnesses rise against me; they charge me with things I know nothing of.
35.12: They repay me evil for good, leaving my soul desolate.
35.13: But when they were sick, I wore sackcloth and humbled myself with fasting; my prayer returned to my own chest.
35.14: I acted like one grieving for a dear friend or brother; I bowed down in mourning as one who weeps for his mother.
35.15: But when I stumbled, they rejoiced and gathered together; attackers assembled against me without my knowing, and tore at me relentlessly.
35.16: Like godless mockers at a banquet, they gnashed their teeth at me.
35.17: How long, O Lord, will you keep watching? Rescue my life from their devastation, my precious self from these lions!
35.18: I will give you thanks in the great assembly; among the vast multitude I will praise you.
35.19: Do not let my enemies gloat over me without cause; do not let those who hate me without reason exchange knowing glances.
35.20: For they do not pursue peace; instead they invent slanders against those who live quietly in the land.
35.21: They open their mouths wide against me and say, 'Aha! Aha! We have seen it with our own eyes!'
35.22: You have seen this, LORD; do not stay silent. Lord, do not stay far from me.
35.23: Arise and wake yourself to my defense, for my cause, my God and my Lord!
35.24: Vindicate me, LORD my God, according to your righteousness, and do not let them triumph over me.
35.25: Do not let them say in their hearts, 'Aha, just what we wanted!' Do not let them say, 'We have devoured him!'
35.26: Let all who rejoice at my misfortune be put to shame and humiliated together; let those who exalt themselves against me be clothed with shame and disgrace.
35.27: Let those who delight in my vindication shout for joy and be glad, and let them say always, 'Great is the LORD, who takes pleasure in the welfare of his servant!'
35.28: Then my tongue will proclaim your righteousness and your praise all day long.

36.1: To the choirmaster. A Psalm of David, the servant of the LORD. The voice of transgression speaks deep within the heart of the wicked: there is no fear of God before his eyes.
36.2: For he deceives himself into thinking his guilt cannot be discovered and condemned.
36.3: His mouth pours out wickedness and deception; he has stopped acting wisely or doing good.
36.4: On his bed he hatches schemes; he places himself on a path that is not good; he refuses to reject evil.
36.5: Your steadfast love, LORD, reaches to the heavens; your faithfulness extends to the clouds.
36.6: Your righteousness towers like the mighty mountains; your judgments plunge as deep as the ocean; you preserve both human and animal, O LORD.
36.7: How priceless is your steadfast love, O God! The children of humanity take refuge under the shadow of your wings.
36.8: They feast on the richness of your house, and you give them drink from the river of your pleasures.
36.9: For with you is the fountain of life; by your light we see light.
36.10: Extend your steadfast love to those who know you, and your righteousness to the upright in heart.
36.11: Let not the foot of arrogance trample me, nor the hand of the wicked drive me from my place.
36.12: There the evildoers lie fallen; they have been thrust down and cannot get up.

37.1: Of David. Do not let evildoers upset you; do not envy those who do wrong.
37.2: For like grass they will soon be cut down; like green plants they will wither away.
37.3: Trust in the LORD and do good; live in the land and enjoy secure provision.
37.4: Find your delight in the LORD, and he will give you the desires of your heart.
37.5: Commit your way to the LORD; trust in him, and he will bring it to pass.
37.6: He will bring your righteousness to light and your cause bright as noon.
37.7: Be still before the LORD and wait patiently for him; do not resent the one who prospers in his way, who carries out his evil plans.
37.8: Let go of anger and abandon wrath; do not fret—it leads only to evil.
37.9: For the wicked will be cut off, but those who wait for the LORD will inherit the earth.
37.10: In just a little while the wicked will be gone; you will search for his place, but it will not be found.
37.11: But the meek will inherit the earth and delight in abundant peace.
37.12: The wicked man schemes against the righteous and grinds his teeth at him.
37.13: The Lord laughs at the wicked, for he sees that their day is coming.
37.14: The wicked draw the sword and bend their bow to bring down the poor and helpless, to kill those who walk uprightly.
37.15: Their swords will pierce their own hearts, and their bows will be shattered.
37.16: Better is the little that the righteous has than the great wealth of many wicked.
37.17: For the strength of the wicked will be shattered, but the LORD supports the righteous.
37.18: The LORD knows the days of the upright, and their inheritance will last forever.
37.19: They will not be disgraced in days of trouble; in times of famine they will be satisfied.
37.20: The wicked will perish; the enemies of the LORD will fade like green pastures—they will dissolve, consumed like smoke.
37.21: The wicked borrows and does not repay, but the righteous is generous and gives.
37.22: Those blessed by the LORD will inherit the earth, but those he has cursed will be cut off.
37.23: The LORD establishes the steps of a man and delights in his way.
37.24: Though he stumbles, he will not fall headlong, for the LORD holds his hand.
37.25: I was young and now I am old, yet I have never seen the righteous abandoned or their children begging for food.
37.26: He is always generous and lending, and his children become a blessing.
37.27: Turn away from evil and do good, so you will live in the land permanently.
37.28: For the LORD loves justice; he does not abandon his faithful people. They are kept safe forever, but the offspring of the wicked will be cut off.
37.29: The righteous will inherit the earth and make their home in it forever.
37.30: The righteous speak wisdom, and their tongue talks of justice.
37.31: The law of his God is in his heart; his steps do not falter.
37.32: The wicked lie in wait for the righteous, looking to kill them.
37.33: The LORD will not leave the righteous at the mercy of the wicked or let them be condemned at trial.
37.34: Wait for the LORD and keep his way; he will exalt you to inherit the earth. You will be there to see the wicked cut off.
37.35: I have seen a ruthless wicked man towering like a flourishing tree in its native ground.
37.36: But he passed away—gone; I looked for him and he could not be found.
37.37: Take note of the blameless person and look at the upright; the future of such a person is peace.
37.38: But rebels will all be destroyed together; the future of the wicked will be cut off.
37.39: The salvation of the righteous comes from the LORD; he is their stronghold in times of trouble.
37.40: The LORD helps and delivers them; he rescues them from the wicked and saves them, because they take shelter in him.

38.1: Of David, as a memorial offering. LORD, do not rebuke me in your anger, or discipline me in your fury.
38.2: Your arrows have pierced me deeply, and your hand has come down hard on me.
38.3: My body has no soundness because of your anger; my bones have no rest because of my sin.
38.4: My iniquities have overwhelmed me—they are a burden too heavy to carry.
38.5: My wounds stink and fester because of my own foolishness.
38.6: I am bowed low and hunched over; I walk about mourning all day long.
38.7: My loins are on fire with disease, and there is no health anywhere in my body.
38.8: I am benumbed and utterly crushed; I cry out from the anguish of my heart.
38.9: Lord, all my longing is laid out before you; my groaning is not hidden from you.
38.10: My heart pounds; my strength has failed, and even the light of my eyes has deserted me.
38.11: My friends and companions keep away from my affliction, and even my relatives stand at a distance.
38.12: Those who want me dead set traps for me; those who seek to harm me speak of my destruction and scheme all day long.
38.13: But I am like someone who cannot hear—I do not listen; like someone mute who cannot speak.
38.14: I am like a man who does not hear, with no counter-arguments in his mouth.
38.15: But in you, LORD, I place my hope; you will answer, O Lord my God.
38.16: For I thought: Do not let them gloat over me—those who triumph over me when I stumble.
38.17: For I am about to stumble and my pain is always with me.
38.18: I confess my wrongdoing; I am distressed by my sin.
38.19: But my enemies are full of life and strong; those who hate me without cause have multiplied.
38.20: Those who repay good with evil oppose me, because I pursue what is right.
38.21: Do not abandon me, LORD; my God, do not stay far away.
38.22: Come quickly to help me, O Lord, my salvation.

39.1: To the director of music. To Jeduthun. A Psalm of David. I said, 'I will keep watch over my ways so I will not sin with my tongue; I will hold a muzzle over my mouth while the wicked are in front of me.'
39.2: I was mute and utterly silent, holding back even from what was good, and my pain intensified.
39.3: My heart grew hot within me; as I brooded, the fire blazed up; then I spoke with my tongue:
39.4: 'LORD, show me my end—how long I have to live; let me grasp how short-lived I am.'
39.5: You have made my days barely a handbreadth long; my lifetime is as nothing in your sight. Truly every person at their best is only a breath. Selah
39.6: Surely everyone moves about like a shadow; they are in commotion for nothing; they pile up wealth and do not know who will collect it.
39.7: And now, Lord, what do I wait for? My hope is in you.
39.8: Rescue me from all my transgressions; do not make me a disgrace before fools.
39.9: I am silent; I do not open my mouth, because this is your doing.
39.10: Take away your affliction from me; I am worn down by the blow of your hand.
39.11: When you correct someone for their iniquity with rebukes, you consume like a moth everything they prize; truly every person is only a breath. Selah
39.12: Hear my prayer, LORD, and listen to my cry; do not stay silent at my tears. I am a stranger in your presence, a temporary traveler, as all my ancestors were.
39.13: Turn your gaze from me, so that I may recover joy before I depart and am gone.

40.1: To the director of music. A Psalm of David. I waited patiently for the LORD; he turned to me and listened to my cry.
40.2: He lifted me out of the pit of destruction, from the muddy bog; he set my feet on a rock and made my steps firm.
40.3: He put a new song in my mouth, praise to our God; many will see and be filled with awe and will put their trust in the LORD.
40.4: Blessed is the one who makes the LORD his trust and does not turn to the proud or to those who follow falsehood.
40.5: You have multiplied your wonderful deeds and your thoughts toward us, O LORD my God; no one can compare to you. If I were to declare and speak them, they are too many to count.
40.6: Sacrifice and grain offering you did not want; but you have given me opened ears; burnt offering and sin offering you have not asked for.
40.7: Then I said, 'Here I am; in the scroll of the book it is written about me.'
40.8: I delight to do your will, O my God; your instruction is within my heart.
40.9: I have announced righteousness in the great assembly; I have not held my lips back—LORD, you know it.
40.10: I have not hidden your righteousness in my heart; I have declared your faithfulness and your salvation; I have not concealed your steadfast love or your truth from the great assembly.
40.11: As for you, LORD, do not withhold your compassion from me; let your steadfast love and truth continually protect me.
40.12: For countless evils have closed in around me; my iniquities have caught up with me until I cannot see clearly—they are more than the hairs of my head, and my courage fails.
40.13: Please, LORD, come to rescue me; LORD, hurry to help me.
40.14: Let those who seek to take my life be put to shame and confounded; let those who wish me harm be turned back in disgrace.
40.15: Let those who mock me with 'Aha! Aha!' be horrified by their own shame.
40.16: Let all who seek you rejoice and be glad in you; let those who love your salvation always say, 'Great is the LORD!'
40.17: As for me, I am poor and needy; yet the Lord thinks of me. You are my help and my deliverer—do not be slow, my God.

41.1: To the director of music. A Psalm of David. Blessed is the one who cares for the vulnerable; the LORD will deliver that person in the day of trouble.
41.2: The LORD protects and preserves his life; he is regarded as blessed in the land; you will not hand him over to his enemies' desire.
41.3: The LORD supports him on his bed of illness; in his sickness you bring him back to health.
41.4: As for me, I said, 'LORD, have mercy on me; heal my soul, for I have sinned against you.'
41.5: My enemies speak wickedly against me: 'When will he die and his name vanish?'
41.6: When one of them comes to visit me, he mouths empty words while inwardly filing away material against me; then he walks out and spreads it everywhere.
41.7: All who hate me whisper together against me; they are plotting disaster for me.
41.8: They say, 'Something incurable has seized him; he will never get up from where he is lying.'
41.9: Even my closest friend, whom I trusted, who shared my bread, has turned against me.
41.10: But you, LORD, have mercy on me and restore me, so that I may give them what they deserve.
41.11: By this I know that you are pleased with me—my enemy has not triumphed over me.
41.12: As for me, you hold me up in my integrity and place me before your face forever.
41.13: Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel, from everlasting to everlasting. Amen and Amen.

42.1: To the director of music. A Maskil of the Sons of Korah. As a deer longs for flowing streams, so my soul longs for you, O God.
42.2: My soul thirsts for God, for the living God. When will I come and stand in God's presence?
42.3: My tears have been my food day and night, while people keep saying to me all day long, 'Where is your God?'
42.4: These things I remember as I pour out my soul: how I used to walk with the crowd, leading them in procession to the house of God, with shouts of joy and thanksgiving—a multitude keeping festival.
42.5: Why are you downcast, O my soul? Why so disturbed within me? Put your hope in God, for I will yet praise him—my Savior and my God.
42.6: My soul is cast down within me; therefore I remember you from the land of Jordan, the heights of Hermon, and Mount Mizar.
42.7: Deep calls to deep at the roar of your waterfalls; all your waves and breakers have rolled over me.
42.8: By day the LORD directs his steadfast love, and at night his song is with me, a prayer to the God of my life.
42.9: I say to God my rock, 'Why have you forgotten me? Why do I walk in mourning under the oppression of the enemy?'
42.10: As if a sword were cutting through my bones, my enemies taunt me, saying to me all day, 'Where is your God?'
42.11: Why are you downcast, O my soul? Why so disturbed within me? Put your hope in God, for I will yet praise him—my Savior and my God.

43.1: Vindicate me, O God, and plead my cause against an ungodly nation; from the deceitful and unjust man deliver me.
43.2: For you are the God who is my stronghold; why have you rejected me? Why do I go about mourning under the oppression of the enemy?
43.3: Send out your light and your truth; let them lead me, let them bring me to your holy mountain and to your dwelling place.
43.4: Then I will approach the altar of God, to God who is my greatest joy, and I will praise you with the harp, O God my God.
43.5: Why are you downcast, O my soul? Why are you in turmoil within me? Put your hope in God; for I will yet praise him, the salvation of my face and my God.

44.1: To the choirmaster, a Maskil of the sons of Korah. O God, we have heard with our own ears; our ancestors have told us what you accomplished in their time, in the days long ago.
44.2: With your own hand you drove out the nations and planted our ancestors; you crushed the peoples and expelled them.
44.3: For they did not win the land by their own sword, and their own arm did not deliver them; it was your right hand and your arm and the light of your face, because you were favorable toward them.
44.4: You are my King, O God; decree victories for Jacob.
44.5: Through you we push back our enemies; in your name we trample those who rise against us.
44.6: For I do not trust in my bow, and my sword cannot bring me victory.
44.7: But you have saved us from our enemies and disgraced those who hate us.
44.8: In God we have boasted all day long, and we will praise your name forever. Selah
44.9: But you have cast us off and brought us to disgrace, and have not gone out with our armies.
44.10: You made us retreat before the enemy, and our enemies plundered us.
44.11: You handed us over like sheep for slaughter and scattered us among the nations.
44.12: You sold your people for a pittance and made no profit from the sale.
44.13: You have made us the object of mockery to our neighbors, a laughingstock and a source of scorn to those around us.
44.14: You have made us a byword among the nations, a wagging of heads among the peoples.
44.15: All day long my disgrace is before me, and shame has covered my face.
44.16: On account of the taunting voice of the one who reviles and blasphemes, because of the enemy who seeks revenge.
44.17: All this has come upon us, yet we have not forgotten you, and we have not been unfaithful to your covenant.
44.18: Our heart has not turned away, and our steps have not strayed from your path.
44.19: Yet you have crushed us in the haunt of jackals and covered us with the shadow of death.
44.20: If we had forgotten the name of our God or spread our hands in prayer toward a foreign god —
44.21: Would God not discover this? For he knows the hidden things of the heart.
44.22: Yet for your sake we face death all day long; we are counted as sheep destined for slaughter.
44.23: Wake up! Why are you sleeping, O Lord? Rise up! Do not reject us forever!
44.24: Why do you hide your face? Why do you ignore our affliction and our oppression?
44.25: For our soul is brought down to the dust; our body clings to the ground.
44.26: Rise up and help us! Deliver us for the sake of your steadfast love.

45.1: To the choirmaster. On Shoshannim. For the sons of Korah. A Maskil, a Song of Loves. My heart is overflowing with a beautiful theme; I address my poem to the king; my tongue is the pen of a skilled scribe.
45.2: You are more handsome than any other man; grace is poured into your lips; therefore God has blessed you forever.
45.3: Strap your sword on your thigh, O champion, in your glory and splendor.
45.4: Ride out victoriously in your splendor for the sake of truth, humility, and righteousness; let your right hand achieve awesome deeds.
45.5: Your arrows are sharp, bringing peoples down beneath you; they pierce into the hearts of the king's enemies.
45.6: Your throne, O God, is forever and ever; the scepter of your kingdom is a righteous scepter.
45.7: You have loved righteousness and hated wickedness; therefore God, your God, has anointed you with the oil of gladness beyond all your companions.
45.8: All your robes are fragrant with myrrh, aloes, and cassia; from ivory palaces the music of strings has brought you joy.
45.9: Daughters of kings are among your ladies of honor; at your right hand stands the queen arrayed in gold of Ophir.
45.10: Listen, daughter, and pay attention; lean in with your ear: forget your own people and your father's house.
45.11: Then the king will desire your beauty; because he is your lord, bow down to him.
45.12: The daughter of Tyre comes bearing a gift; the wealthy among the nations will seek your favor.
45.13: The king's daughter is altogether beautiful within; her garment is interwoven with gold.
45.14: In embroidered garments she will be led to the king; the maidens, her companions, will follow her and be brought to you.
45.15: With joy and celebration they will be led in; they will enter the king's palace.
45.16: In place of your ancestors will be your sons; you will appoint them as princes throughout all the earth.
45.17: I will cause your name to be remembered in all generations; therefore the peoples will praise you forever and ever.

46.1: To the choirmaster, for the sons of Korah, a Song on Alamoth. God is our refuge and strength, a help that is always present in times of trouble.
46.2: Therefore we will not fear, even if the earth gives way, even if the mountains topple into the heart of the sea.
46.3: Though the waters roar and churn, though the mountains quake at the surging sea. Selah
46.4: There is a river whose streams bring joy to the city of God, the sacred dwelling of the Most High.
46.5: God is within her; she will not be shaken; God will help her when morning breaks.
46.6: Nations raged and kingdoms shook; he lifted his voice, and the earth dissolved.
46.7: The LORD of hosts is with us; the God of Jacob is our fortress. Selah
46.8: Come and see what the LORD has done, what desolations he has brought upon the earth.
46.9: He puts an end to wars across the whole earth; he breaks bows and cuts spears apart; he burns the war-chariots with fire.
46.10: Be still, and know that I am God; I will be exalted among the nations; I will be exalted throughout the earth.
46.11: The LORD of hosts is with us; the God of Jacob is our fortress. Selah

47.1: To the choirmaster. A Psalm of the sons of Korah. Clap your hands, all you peoples; shout to God with a voice of joy.
47.2: For the LORD Most High is to be feared; he is a great King over the whole earth.
47.3: He subdues peoples under us and nations beneath our feet.
47.4: He chose our inheritance for us, the glory of Jacob whom he loved. Selah
47.5: God has ascended with shouts of joy, the LORD with the sound of a ram's horn.
47.6: Sing praises to God, sing praises! Sing praises to our King, sing praises!
47.7: For God is the King of the whole earth; sing praises with understanding.
47.8: God reigns over the nations; God is seated on his holy throne.
47.9: The nobles of the nations have gathered as the people of the God of Abraham; for the rulers of the earth belong to God; he is highly exalted.

48.1: A Song, a Psalm of the sons of Korah. Great is the LORD and greatly to be praised in the city of our God, on his holy mountain.
48.2: Mount Zion, beautiful in its height, is the joy of all the earth; on the heights of the north it rises, the city of the great King.
48.3: Within her citadels God has shown himself to be a fortress.
48.4: For look — the kings assembled; they came against her together.
48.5: They saw it and were bewildered; they were struck with terror and fled.
48.6: Trembling took hold of them there, pain like that of a woman giving birth.
48.7: With the east wind you shatter the ships of Tarshish.
48.8: As we have heard, so we have seen in the city of the LORD of hosts, in the city of our God; God establishes it forever. Selah
48.9: We have meditated on your steadfast love, O God, within your temple.
48.10: As your name reaches, O God, so does your praise, to the ends of the earth; your right hand is full of righteousness.
48.11: Let Mount Zion rejoice; let the towns of Judah be glad because of your righteous judgments.
48.12: Walk all around Zion, circle her, count her towers.
48.13: Mark her walls carefully, tour her citadels, that you may tell the next generation.
48.14: For this God is our God forever and ever; he will guide us until the very end.

55.1: To the director of music. With stringed instruments. A Maskil of David. Give ear to my prayer, O God; do not hide yourself from my plea for mercy.
55.2: Pay attention to me and answer me; I am troubled in my lament and I am agitated—
55.3: because of the threatening voice of the enemy, the oppressive presence of the wicked; they bring evil upon me, and in anger they press hard against me.
55.4: My heart is writhing in anguish within me; the terrors of death have fallen on me.
55.5: Fear and trembling have overtaken me, and shuddering has covered me over.
55.6: And I said, 'If only I had wings like a dove! I would fly away and find rest.'
55.7: Yes, I would flee far away; I would make my dwelling in the wilderness. Selah
55.8: I would hurry to my refuge from the driving wind and the storm.
55.9: Confuse them, O Lord, split their tongues apart; for I see violence and contention in the city.
55.10: Day and night they patrol its walls; iniquity and trouble are entrenched within it.
55.11: Ruin is entrenched at its center; oppression and fraud never leave its public spaces.
55.12: For it is not an enemy who insults me—that I could endure; nor is it someone who hates me who has risen up against me—from that I could hide.
55.13: But it is you—a person like me, my peer, my companion, the one I confided in.
55.14: We shared deep, sweet conversation together; together we walked in God's house among the festival crowd.
55.15: Let death ambush them; let them go down to Sheol alive; for wickedness lives in their dwellings and among them.
55.16: As for me, I will call upon God, and the LORD will rescue me.
55.17: Evening and morning and at noon I cry out my lament and sigh; and he hears my voice.
55.18: He ransoms me safely from the battle I am in, for they are many who oppose me.
55.19: God will hear and afflict them, the one who is enthroned from of old—Selah—because they show no sign of change and have no fear of God.
55.20: He raised his hand against those who were at peace with him; he broke his covenant.
55.21: His words were smoother than butter, yet war was in his heart; his speech was softer than oil, but it was all drawn swords.
55.22: Throw your burden onto the LORD, and he will hold you up; he will never let the righteous be shaken.
55.23: But you, O God, will bring them down into the pit of ruin; men of bloodshed and treachery will not reach half their days. But I—I will trust in you.

56.1: To the director of music. To the tune 'Dove of the Far-off Oaks.' A Michtam of David, when the Philistines had seized him in Gath. Be merciful to me, O God, for men are trampling on me; all day long they fight and press against me.
56.2: My enemies trample on me all day long, for many are arrayed against me proudly.
56.3: When I am afraid, I will put my trust in you.
56.4: In God—whose word I praise—in God I trust; I will not be afraid. What can a mere human being do to me?
56.5: All day long they distort my words; all their scheming is against me for evil.
56.6: They gather themselves to stir up trouble, they lie in ambush, they track my steps as they wait to take my life.
56.7: For their crime, should they escape? In wrath cast down the peoples, O God!
56.8: You have recorded my restless wandering; store my tears in your wineskin—are they not written in your book?
56.9: On the day I call out, my enemies will turn back; this I know: God is on my side.
56.10: In God—whose word I praise—in the LORD—whose word I praise—
56.11: in God I trust; I will not be afraid. What can human beings do to me?
56.12: I have made vows to you, O God, and I will fulfill them; I will offer you my thanksgiving.
56.13: For you have saved my life from death, and my feet from stumbling, so that I may walk before God in the light that belongs to the living.

57.1: To the director of music. 'Do Not Destroy.' A Michtam of David, when he fled from Saul in the cave. Be merciful to me, O God, be merciful to me, for my soul takes refuge in you; in the shadow of your wings I will take shelter until the storms of destruction pass.
57.2: I cry out to God Most High, to God who carries out his purpose for me.
57.3: He will send from heaven and rescue me; he will rebuke the one who tramples me. Selah. God will send his steadfast love and his faithfulness.
57.4: My soul is surrounded by lions; I must lie down among those who breathe fire—human beings whose teeth are spears and arrows and whose tongue is a sharp sword.
57.5: Be exalted above the heavens, O God; let your glory be over all the earth!
57.6: They spread a net for my feet; my soul was beaten down; they dug a pit in my path, but they themselves have fallen into it. Selah
57.7: My heart is fixed, O God, my heart is firm; I will sing and make melody!
57.8: Awake, my honor! Awake, harp and lyre! I will rouse the dawn!
57.9: I will give you thanks, O Lord, among the peoples; I will sing your praise among the nations.
57.10: For your steadfast love reaches to the heavens, your faithfulness to the skies.
57.11: Be exalted above the heavens, O God; let your glory be over all the earth!

58.1: To the director of music. 'Do Not Destroy.' A Michtam of David. Do you indeed decree what is right, you rulers? Do you judge human beings with justice?
58.2: No—in your heart you devise wrongs; your hands administer violence throughout the land.
58.3: The wicked are alienated from the womb; from their very birth they go astray, speaking lies.
58.4: Their venom is like the venom of a snake; they are like the deaf cobra that blocks its ear,
58.5: that will not listen to the voice of those who charm, no matter how skillfully they do it.
58.6: Smash their teeth in their mouths, O God; tear out the fangs of the young lions, O LORD!
58.7: Let them drain away like water flowing downhill; when they draw their bows, let their arrows fall harmless.
58.8: Let them be like a snail that dissolves as it goes, like a stillborn child that never sees the light of the sun.
58.9: Before your cooking pots have felt the heat of the thornwood—both green and dry—he will whirl them away.
58.10: The righteous will rejoice when they see justice carried out; they will wash their feet in the blood of the wicked.
58.11: Then people will say, 'There really is a reward for the righteous; truly there is a God who judges the earth.'

59.1: To the director of music. 'Do Not Destroy.' A Michtam of David, when Saul sent men to watch the house to kill him. Rescue me from my enemies, O my God; set me beyond the reach of those who rise up against me.
59.2: Rescue me from those who do evil, and save me from men who want my blood.
59.3: For see—they lie in ambush for my life; fierce men are gathering against me—not for any offense or sin of mine, LORD.
59.4: For no fault of mine they have mobilized against me. Arouse yourself, come to meet me, and look!
59.5: You are the LORD God of hosts, the God of Israel—rouse yourself to punish all the nations; show no mercy to those who commit treason with evil intent. Selah
59.6: They return each evening, howling like dogs and circling around the city.
59.7: Listen—they pour out venom with their mouths; their lips are full of swords, for they think, 'Who is going to hear?'
59.8: But you, LORD, laugh at them; you scoff at all the nations.
59.9: O my Strength, I will wait on you; for God is my fortress.
59.10: The God of my steadfast love will come ahead of me; God will let me look in triumph on my enemies.
59.11: Do not kill them, so my people will not forget; bring them down by your power and humble them, O Lord our shield!
59.12: For the sin of their mouths and the words of their lips, let them be ensnared in their own pride. For the cursing and deception that they utter,
59.13: Consume them in your wrath; consume them until they are no more, so that people may know that God rules over Jacob to the ends of the earth. Selah
59.14: They return each evening, howling like dogs and circling the city.
59.15: They roam around in search of food; they growl when they are not satisfied.
