59.16: But I will sing of your strength; I will sing aloud of your steadfast love in the morning; for you have been my fortress and my place of refuge in my day of distress.
59.17: O my Strength, I will sing praise to you; for God is my fortress, the God who shows me steadfast love.

60.1: To the director of music. 'Lily of the Testimony.' A teaching Michtam of David; from when he fought Aram-naharaim and Aram-zobah, while Joab returned and struck down twelve thousand Edomites in the Valley of Salt. O God, you have rejected us and broken our defenses; you have been angry—restore us!
60.2: You have made the earth shake; you have cracked it open; heal its fractures, for it is collapsing.
60.3: You have put your people through severe trials; you have made us drink disorienting wine.
60.4: You have set up a banner for those who fear you, for them to rally to in the face of the enemy's bow. Selah
60.5: Save your dear ones—rescue them by your right hand, and answer our prayer.
60.6: God has spoken in his holiness: 'I will triumph; I will divide up Shechem and portion out the Valley of Succoth.'
60.7: 'Gilead is mine, Manasseh is mine; Ephraim is my helmet; Judah is my scepter.'
60.8: 'Moab is my washbasin; over Edom I throw my sandal; over Philistia I shout in triumph.'
60.9: Who will bring me into the fortified city? Who will guide me all the way to Edom?
60.10: Have you not rejected us, O God? You no longer go out, God, with our armies.
60.11: Give us help against the enemy, for human deliverance is worthless.
60.12: Through God we will do great deeds; he himself will crush our enemies.

49.1: To the director of music. Of the Sons of Korah. A Psalm. Hear this, all peoples! Listen, all who live in the world—
49.2: the humble and the great alike, the rich and the poor together.
49.3: My mouth will speak wisdom, and the reflection of my heart will be understanding.
49.4: I bend my ear to a proverb; I will open my riddle with the harp.
49.5: Why should I be afraid in evil days, when the guilt of my accusers closes in around me?
49.6: those who trust in their wealth and boast of their great riches—
49.7: truly no one can ransom another person, or give God a payment for their life—
49.8: for the price of their life is too high; the payment would never be enough—
49.9: no sum could keep him alive forever, safe from seeing Sheol.
49.10: He sees that even the wise die—the fool and the senseless alike perish and leave their wealth behind for others.
49.11: In their hearts they imagined their homes would last forever, their estates from generation to generation; they stamped their own names on their lands.
49.12: Man in all his honor will not last; he is like the animals that die.
49.13: This is the fate of those who are confident in themselves, and after them people applaud their talk. Selah
49.14: Like sheep they are headed for Sheol; Death will be their shepherd, and the upright will have dominion over them in the morning. Their bodies will decay in Sheol, with no home left for them.
49.15: But God will ransom my life from the grip of Sheol, for he will take me to himself. Selah
49.16: Do not be frightened when someone becomes rich, when the splendor of their household grows—
49.17: for when they die they take nothing with them; their glory will not follow them down.
49.18: Though in their lifetime they counted themselves fortunate—and people praised them for prospering—
49.19: but they will go to join their ancestors and never again see the light.
49.20: Man in all his honor, but without understanding, is like the animals that die.

50.1: A Psalm of Asaph. The Mighty One, God, the LORD, speaks and summons the earth from east to west.
50.2: Out of Zion, perfect in beauty, God blazes out.
50.3: Our God comes and will not stay silent; fire devours before him, and a fierce storm rages around him.
50.4: He calls out to the heavens above and to the earth, to judge his people:
50.5: 'Bring together my faithful people, those who sealed covenant with me by sacrifice.'
50.6: The heavens declare his righteousness, for God himself is the judge. Selah
50.7: 'Listen, my people, and I will speak; I will testify against you, O Israel. I am God, your God.'
50.8: 'My rebuke is not over your sacrifices; your burnt offerings are always before me.'
50.9: 'I do not need a bull from your household or goats from your pens.'
50.10: 'Every animal of the forest is mine—the cattle on a thousand hills.'
50.11: 'I know every bird in the hills; everything that moves in the open fields is mine.'
50.12: 'If I were hungry, I would not come to you—the world and everything in it is mine.'
50.13: 'Do I eat bull meat? Do I drink the blood of goats?'
50.14: 'Bring God a thank-offering as your sacrifice, and keep your vows to the Most High.'
50.15: 'Call on me when trouble comes; I will rescue you, and you will honor me.'
50.16: But to the wicked, God says: 'What right do you have to quote my laws, or to take my covenant on your lips?'
50.17: 'You despise correction and fling my words aside.'
50.18: 'When you see a thief you go along with him, and you make yourself at home with adulterers.'
50.19: 'You let your mouth run loose into evil, and your tongue is busy weaving lies.'
50.20: 'You sit and talk against your own brother; you slander your mother's son.'
50.21: 'You did all these things, and I said nothing. You concluded I must be just like you. But now I rebuke you and lay everything out before your eyes.'
50.22: 'Take this seriously, you who have forgotten God, or I will tear you apart, and no one will rescue you.'
50.23: 'Whoever brings thanksgiving as their sacrifice glorifies me; to the one who walks a right path I will show the salvation of God.'

51.1: To the director of music. A Psalm of David, when the prophet Nathan came to him after David had slept with Bathsheba. Have mercy on me, O God, in keeping with your steadfast love; in your great compassion, blot out my transgressions.
51.2: Scrub away my guilt completely, and purify me from my sin.
51.3: For I know my rebellions, and my sin is before me at every moment.
51.4: Against you—you alone—have I sinned and done what is evil in your sight, so that you are proved right when you speak and blameless when you judge.
51.5: Surely I was brought forth into guilt, and in sin my mother conceived me.
51.6: You desire truth deep inside a person; in the hidden place you are teaching me wisdom.
51.7: Cleanse me with hyssop, and I will be clean; wash me, and I will be whiter than snow.
51.8: Let me hear the sounds of joy and gladness; let the bones you have crushed be glad.
51.9: Look away from my sins; wipe out all my guilt.
51.10: Create a pure heart in me, O God, and renew a steadfast spirit within me.
51.11: Do not banish me from your presence; do not take your Holy Spirit from me.
51.12: Give back to me the joy of your rescue, and sustain me with a generous, willing spirit.
51.13: Then I will show other rebels your ways, and sinners will come back to you.
51.14: Rescue me from the guilt of bloodshed, O God—God of my salvation—and my tongue will ring out your righteousness.
51.15: O Lord, open my lips, and my mouth will declare your praise.
51.16: You do not want sacrifice—otherwise I would bring it; you take no delight in burnt offerings.
51.17: What God wants as sacrifice is a broken spirit; a broken and crushed heart, O God, you will not reject.
51.18: In your favor, do good to Zion; build up the walls of Jerusalem.
51.19: Then you will be pleased with right sacrifices, with burnt offerings and whole offerings; then bulls will go up on your altar.

52.1: To the director of music. A Maskil of David. When Doeg the Edomite came and told Saul: 'David has gone to the house of Ahimelech.' Why do you boast of evil, O powerful man? The steadfast love of God holds all the day.
52.2: Your tongue plots harm, sharp as a razor blade—you craftsman of deceit.
52.3: You love evil rather than good, and lies rather than truth. Selah
52.4: You love every word that destroys, you treacherous tongue.
52.5: But God will pull you down permanently; he will grab and rip you from your home; he will tear you out of the land of the living. Selah
52.6: The righteous will see and be filled with awe, and they will laugh:
52.7: 'Here is the man who would not take refuge in God, but relied on his great wealth and found his strength in destruction.'
52.8: But I am like a flourishing olive tree in the house of God; I trust in God's steadfast love forever and ever.
52.9: I will give you thanks forever, because of what you have done; I will hope in your name, for it is good, before your faithful people.

53.1: To the director of music. On Mahalath. A Maskil of David. The fool has said in his heart, 'There is no God.' They are corrupt and have done vile deeds; not one of them does good.
53.2: God looked down from heaven on all humanity to see if anyone understands, if anyone seeks after God.
53.3: Every single one has turned aside; together they have become rotten; there is no one who does good—not a single person.
53.4: Don't those who work evil know anything—those who devour my people the way they eat bread and never call on God?
53.5: But there they are, overwhelmed with terror where there was no terror, for God scattered the bones of those who attacked you; you put them to shame, for God has rejected them.
53.6: Oh, that Israel's rescue would come out of Zion! When God restores the fortunes of his people, Jacob will rejoice and Israel will be glad.

54.1: To the director of music. With stringed instruments. A Maskil of David. When the Ziphites came to Saul and said, 'Is not David hiding among us?' O God, save me by your name; vindicate me by your strength.
54.2: Hear my prayer, O God; listen to what I am saying.
54.3: For outsiders are threatening me; violent men are hunting my life—people who have no thought of God. Selah
54.4: But God is my helper; the Lord is the one who sustains my life.
54.5: He will bring the evil back on my enemies; in your faithfulness, silence them.
54.6: With a generous, willing heart I will offer sacrifice to you; I will give thanks to your name, LORD, for it is good.
54.7: For he has rescued me from every distress, and my eye has seen the downfall of my enemies.

61.1: To the choirmaster, on stringed instruments. A Psalm of David. Hear my cry, O God; listen to my prayer.
61.2: From the ends of the earth I call out to you when my heart grows faint; lead me to the rock that is higher than I.
61.3: For you have been a shelter for me, a strong tower against the enemy.
61.4: I will dwell in your tent forever; I will take refuge under the shelter of your wings. Selah.
61.5: For you, O God, have heard my vows; you have given me the inheritance of those who fear your name.
61.6: You will extend the king's days; his years shall span many generations.
61.7: May he dwell before God forever; appoint steadfast love and faithfulness to guard him.
61.8: So I will sing praise to your name forever, as I fulfill my vows day after day.

62.1: To the choirmaster, according to Jeduthun. A Psalm of David. Truly my soul waits in silence for God alone; from him comes my salvation.
62.2: He alone is my rock and my salvation; he is my fortress; I will not be greatly shaken.
62.3: How long will all of you assault a man? You might as well be killing him. You are like a leaning wall, like a tottering fence.
62.4: They only plan to bring him down from his position; they delight in falsehood; with their mouths they bless, but inwardly they curse. Selah.
62.5: Be still before God alone, my soul; for my hope comes from him.
62.6: He alone is my rock and my salvation; he is my fortress; I will not be shaken.
62.7: In God rests my salvation and my honor; God is the rock of my strength, my refuge.
62.8: Trust in him at all times, O people; pour out your hearts before him; God is a refuge for us. Selah.
62.9: Surely common people are only a breath, and people of rank are a deception; weighed in the scales they rise — they are lighter than a breath.
62.10: Do not trust in extortion; do not take false pride in robbery; if wealth increases, do not set your heart on it.
62.11: One thing God has spoken; two things I have heard: that power belongs to God.
62.12: And to you, O Lord, belongs steadfast love; for you repay each person according to their deeds.

63.1: A Psalm of David, when he was in the wilderness of Judah. O God, you are my God; earnestly I seek you; my soul thirsts for you; my flesh longs for you in a dry and parched land where there is no water.
63.2: So I have gazed upon you in the sanctuary, beholding your power and your glory.
63.3: Because your steadfast love is better than life itself, my lips will praise you.
63.4: So I will bless you as long as I live; in your name I will lift up my hands.
63.5: My soul is satisfied as with the richest of food; my mouth praises you with joyful lips.
63.6: When I remember you on my bed, I meditate on you through the watches of the night.
63.7: For you have been my help; and in the shadow of your wings I will rejoice.
63.8: My soul clings close to you; your right hand holds me fast.
63.9: But those who seek to destroy my life shall descend into the depths of the earth.
63.10: They will be delivered over to the sword; they will become prey for jackals.
63.11: But the king will rejoice in God; all who swear by him will boast in him; for the mouths of liars will be silenced.

64.1: To the choirmaster. A Psalm of David. Hear my voice, O God, in my complaint; guard my life from the terror of the enemy.
64.2: Shelter me from the conspiracy of the wicked, from the scheming mob of evildoers.
64.3: They sharpen their tongues like swords and aim bitter words like arrows from a bow.
64.4: Shooting from hiding places at the innocent; they strike suddenly and without fear.
64.5: They scheme their evil plan together; they talk of setting traps in secret; they say, Who will see us?
64.6: They plan iniquities: 'We have devised a perfect scheme!' But the inward thought and heart of a person are unfathomable.
64.7: But God will shoot his arrow at them; suddenly they will be struck down.
64.8: God makes their own tongues their undoing; all who see them shake their heads and flee.
64.9: Then all people will stand in fear; they will proclaim what God has done and ponder his deeds.
64.10: The righteous will rejoice in the LORD and take refuge in him; all the upright in heart shall boast.

65.1: To the choirmaster. A Psalm. A Song of David. Praise awaits you in silence, O God, in Zion; and to you the vow shall be fulfilled.
65.2: O you who hear prayer, to you all flesh shall come.
65.3: When our sins overwhelm us, you atone for our transgressions.
65.4: Blessed is the one you choose and bring near, to dwell in your courts; we shall be satisfied with the goodness of your house, your holy temple.
65.5: By awesome deeds in righteousness you answer us, O God of our salvation; you are the hope of all the ends of the earth and of the most distant seas.
65.6: The one who by his strength sets the mountains in place, who is clothed with power.
65.7: You still the roaring of the seas, the roaring of their waves, and the uproar of the nations.
65.8: Those who live at earth's farthest bounds stand in awe of your signs; you make the sunrise and the sunset ring with joy.
65.9: You visit the earth and water it abundantly; you enrich it with the river of God, full of water; you provide grain for them, for so you have prepared it.
65.10: You soak its furrows abundantly, you level its ridges, you soften it with showers, and you bless what springs up from it.
65.11: You crown the year with your bounty; your wagon tracks overflow with abundance.
65.12: The pastures of the wilderness overflow; the hills are wrapped in rejoicing.
65.13: The meadows are dressed with flocks; the valleys are blanketed with grain — they shout for joy, they break into song.

66.1: To the choirmaster. A Song. A Psalm. Shout joyfully to God, all the earth.
66.2: Sing the glory of his name; make his praise resplendent.
66.3: Say to God, 'How awesome are your deeds! Through the greatness of your power your enemies cower before you.'
66.4: All the earth bows down before you; they sing to you, they sing praises to your name. Selah.
66.5: Come and see what God has done; he is awesome in his acts toward humanity.
66.6: He turned the sea into dry land; they crossed the river on foot; there we rejoiced in him.
66.7: He rules by his power forever; his eyes watch over the nations — let the rebellious not exalt themselves. Selah.
66.8: Bless our God, O peoples; let the sound of his praise be heard.
66.9: He keeps our lives from death and does not allow our feet to stumble.
66.10: For you, O God, have tested us; you have refined us as silver is refined.
66.11: You brought us into the net; you laid a crushing burden on our backs.
66.12: You let men ride over our heads; we went through fire and through water; yet you brought us out to a wide and spacious place.
66.13: I will come into your house with burnt offerings; I will fulfill my vows to you.
66.14: The vows that my lips uttered and my mouth spoke in my time of trouble.
66.15: I will offer you burnt offerings of fattened animals, with the rising smoke of rams; I will sacrifice bulls and goats. Selah.
66.16: Come and hear, all you who fear God, and I will tell what he has done for me.
66.17: I cried out to him with my mouth, and his praise was on my tongue.
66.18: If I had cherished sin in my heart, the Lord would not have listened.
66.19: But truly God has heard; he has attended to the voice of my prayer.
66.20: Blessed be God, who has not turned away my prayer or withheld his steadfast love from me.

67.1: To the director of music. On stringed instruments. A Psalm. A Song. May God be gracious to us and bless us, and may he cause his face to shine upon us. Selah
67.2: so that your way may be known on earth, your saving power among all the nations.
67.3: Let the peoples praise you, O God; let all the peoples give you praise.
67.4: Let the nations be glad and shout for joy; for you will judge the peoples with fairness, and guide the nations of the earth. Selah
67.5: Let the peoples praise you, O God; let all the peoples give you praise.
67.6: The earth has given its harvest; God, our own God, has blessed us.
67.7: God blesses us, and all the ends of the earth will stand in awe of him.

68.1: To the director of music. A Psalm of David. A Song. Let God arise and let his enemies be scattered; let those who hate him flee from before him.
68.2: As smoke is dispersed by the wind, may you disperse them; as wax melts before fire, so let the wicked vanish from before God.
68.3: But let the righteous be glad and rejoice before God; let them celebrate with great joy.
68.4: Sing to God, sing praises to his name; exalt him who rides upon the heavens — the LORD is his name — and rejoice before him.
68.5: God in his holy dwelling is a father to the fatherless and a defender of widows.
68.6: God gives the isolated a home to belong to; he brings prisoners out into freedom; but the rebellious must live in a barren land.
68.7: O God, when you went out before your people, when you marched through the wilderness — Selah —
68.8: the earth shook and the heavens poured down rain at the presence of God — even Sinai itself trembled — at the presence of God, the God of Israel.
68.9: You sent an abundant rain, O God; you restored your weary inheritance.
68.10: Your congregation settled in the land; in your goodness, O God, you provided for the needy.
68.11: The Lord gave the word; great was the army of women who announced the victory.
68.12: The kings of armies fled in haste; she who stayed at home shared in the plunder.
68.13: Even if you once lay among the sheepfolds, you shall shine like the wings of a dove, silver-covered, its feathers gleaming with gold.
68.14: When the Almighty scattered kings throughout the land, snow fell on Zalmon.
68.15: The mountain of God is like the mountain of Bashan — a high, many-peaked mountain, like the mountain of Bashan.
68.16: Why do you look with envy, O many-peaked mountains? This is the mountain that God chose for his dwelling place — yes, the LORD will dwell there forever.
68.17: The chariots of God number in the tens of thousands, thousands upon thousands; the Lord is among them — as at Sinai — in his holy place.
68.18: You have ascended to the heights, leading a train of captives; you have received tribute from men — even from those who rebelled — so that the LORD God might make his dwelling there.
68.19: Blessed be the Lord, who day after day carries our burdens for us — the God of our salvation. Selah
68.20: Our God is the God of deliverance; and to the LORD, the Lord, belong the ways of escape from death.
68.21: But God will crush the head of his enemies, the proud skull of those who persist in their guilt.
68.22: The Lord declared, 'I will bring them back from Bashan; I will bring my people back from the depths of the sea—'
68.23: so that your foot will be stained in the blood of your enemies, and the tongues of your dogs will lap it up.
68.24: People have witnessed your procession, O God — the procession of my God, my King, into the holy place.
68.25: The singers lead the procession; behind them come the musicians; among them, young women playing tambourines.
68.26: Bless God in the great assembly; praise the LORD, all you who come from the fountain of Israel.
68.27: There is Benjamin, the youngest, leading the way; the princes of Judah in their bright company; the princes of Zebulun and the princes of Naphtali.
68.28: Your God has established your power; show your strength, O God, in what you have already begun for us.
68.29: Because of your temple in Jerusalem, kings will bring their gifts to you.
68.30: Rebuke the beast that lurks in the reeds, the herd of bulls among the peoples' young, until they submit and bring silver tribute; scatter the peoples who delight in war.
68.31: Envoys will come from Egypt; Ethiopia will quickly extend her hands to God.
68.32: Sing to God, O kingdoms of the earth; sing praises to the Lord. Selah
68.33: to him who rides upon the ancient heavens — listen, he thunders with his mighty voice.
68.34: Ascribe power to God; his splendor is over Israel, and his strength is in the heavens.
68.35: God is awe-inspiring from his sanctuary — the God of Israel is the one who gives strength and power to his people. Blessed be God!

69.1: To the director of music. To the tune of 'The Lilies.' A Psalm of David. Save me, O God, for the waters have risen to my neck.
69.2: I am sinking in the deep mud — no place to stand; I have come into the depths of the waters, and the current sweeps over me.
69.3: I am exhausted from calling out; my throat is raw; my eyes have given out while waiting for my God.
69.4: Those who hate me without cause outnumber the hairs of my head; those who are my enemies for no reason and who would destroy me are powerful; am I to restore what I never took?
69.5: O God, you know my foolishness; and my sins are not concealed from you.
69.6: Let not those who hope in you be disgraced because of me, O Lord GOD of hosts; let not those who seek you be humiliated on my account, O God of Israel.
69.7: For your sake I have borne disgrace; humiliation has covered my face.
69.8: I have become a stranger to my brothers, an outsider to my own mother's children.
69.9: Zeal for your house has consumed me; and the insults directed at you have landed on me.
69.10: When I wept and humbled my soul with fasting, that became a source of shame for me.
69.11: I dressed in sackcloth, and I became a laughingstock to them.
69.12: Those who sit at the city gate gossip about me; and I have become the subject of the drunkards' songs.
69.13: But as for me, my prayer is directed to you, LORD, at a time of your favor; O God, in your great steadfast love, answer me in the faithfulness of your salvation.
69.14: Rescue me from the mire and do not let me sink; let me be saved from those who hate me and from the deep water.
69.15: Let not the flood sweep me away; let not the depths swallow me; let not the pit close its mouth on me.
69.16: Hear me, LORD, for your steadfast love is good; in your great compassion, turn toward me.
69.17: Do not turn your face away from your servant; I am in great distress — answer me quickly.
69.18: Come near to my soul and rescue it; set me free because of my enemies.
69.19: You know my disgrace, my shame, and my humiliation; all my enemies are in your sight.
69.20: Disgrace has broken my heart so that I am in anguish; I looked for someone to show compassion, but there was none; and for comforters, but I found none.
69.21: They put bitter gall in my food; and when I was thirsty, they offered me vinegar to drink.
69.22: Let their table be set as a snare before them; and what was meant for their prosperity become a trap.
69.23: Let their eyes be darkened so they cannot see; and let their bodies tremble without ceasing.
69.24: Pour out your wrath upon them; let your fierce anger seize them.
69.25: Let their camp become desolate; let no one live in their dwelling.
69.26: For they have harassed the one whom you have afflicted; they have gossiped about the suffering of those you wounded.
69.27: Pile their guilt on top of their guilt; and do not let them share in your vindication.
69.28: Let them be erased from the scroll of the living; let them not be listed among the righteous.
69.29: But I am afflicted and in pain; may your salvation, O God, lift me up.
69.30: I will praise the name of God with a song of praise; I will honor him with thanksgiving.
69.31: This will please the LORD more than an ox or a bull with its horns and hoofs.
69.32: The afflicted will see it and rejoice; those of you who seek God, may your hearts be renewed.
69.33: For the LORD listens to the needy; he does not look down on those he has bound.
69.34: Let heaven and earth give him praise, the seas and every living thing in them.
69.35: For God will save Zion and rebuild the cities of Judah; people will live there and take possession of it.
69.36: The children of his servants will inherit it; and those who love his name will live there.

70.1: To the director of music. A Psalm of David, to bring to remembrance. O God, hurry to deliver me! O LORD, make haste to help me!
70.2: Let those who seek my life be put to shame and confusion; let those who delight in my harm be turned back and brought to dishonor.
70.3: Let them be turned back in their shame—those who say, 'Aha, aha!'
70.4: But let all who seek you rejoice and be glad in you; let those who love your salvation say always, 'God is great!'
70.5: But I am poor and needy; hurry to me, O God! You are my help and my deliverer; O LORD, do not tarry.

71.1: In you, O LORD, I take refuge; let me never be put to shame.
71.2: In your righteousness deliver me and rescue me; incline your ear to me and save me.
71.3: Be for me a sheltering rock, a place I can always come to; you have commanded my salvation, for you are my rock and my fortress.
71.4: Rescue me, O my God, from the hand of the wicked, from the grip of the unjust and cruel.
71.5: For you, O Lord GOD, are my hope, my trust from my earliest years.
71.6: I have depended on you from before I was born; you drew me safely out of my mother's womb. My praise is forever of you.
71.7: I have been like an astonishing sign to many, but you are my strong shelter.
71.8: My mouth is full of your praise and your glory all the day long.
71.9: Do not reject me when old age comes; do not abandon me when my strength gives out.
71.10: For my enemies speak about me; those who watch for my life conspire together.
71.11: They say, 'God has abandoned him; run him down and take him, for there is no one to rescue him.'
71.12: O God, do not be far from me; O my God, come quickly to my aid.
71.13: Let those who attack my life be put to shame and consumed; let those who seek my harm be wrapped in reproach and dishonor.
71.14: But I will hope continually and will praise you more and more.
71.15: My mouth will proclaim your righteousness and your saving acts all the day long, for I cannot count their number.
71.16: I will come in the strength of the Lord GOD; I will declare your righteousness—yours and no one else's.
71.17: O God, you have taught me from my youth, and to this day I am still declaring your wonderful deeds.
71.18: And even now in old age, with gray hair, O God, do not forsake me until I have proclaimed your strength to this generation and your power to all who are yet to come.
71.19: Your righteousness, O God, reaches the high heavens; you who have done such great things, O God—who is like you?
71.20: You who have made me experience many bitter troubles will revive me again; from the depths of the earth you will bring me up once more.
71.21: You will increase my honor and surround me with comfort.
71.22: I will praise you with the lyre for your faithfulness, O my God; I will sing to you with the harp, O Holy One of Israel.
71.23: My lips will shout for joy when I sing praises to you; and my soul, which you have redeemed, will also rejoice.
71.24: And my tongue will speak of your righteous deeds all day long, for those who sought to harm me have been put to shame and humiliated.

72.1: A Psalm of Solomon. O God, give the king your justice, and your righteousness to the king's son.
72.2: May he judge your people with righteousness and your poor with justice.
72.3: May the mountains bring peace to the people, and the hills, through righteousness.
72.4: May he defend the cause of the poor of the people, give deliverance to the children of the needy, and crush the oppressor.
72.5: May they fear you as long as the sun and moon endure, through all generations.
72.6: May he be like rain falling on newly mown grass, like showers that water the earth.
72.7: In his days may the righteous flourish, and peace abound, until the moon is no more.
72.8: May he have dominion from sea to sea, from the River to the ends of the earth.
72.9: May desert peoples bow before him, and his enemies lick the dust.
72.10: May the kings of Tarshish and the coastlands bring tribute; may the kings of Sheba and Seba offer gifts.
72.11: Yes, may all kings bow down before him; may all nations serve him.
72.12: For he delivers the needy when they cry for help, the poor and those who have no one to help them.
72.13: He has compassion on the weak and the needy, and saves the lives of the poor.
72.14: He redeems their life from oppression and violence, and their blood is precious in his eyes.
72.15: Long may he live! May gold from Sheba be given to him! May prayer be made for him continually and blessings pronounced for him all day long.
72.16: May grain be abundant throughout the land; may it wave on the mountaintops; may its yield be like Lebanon, and may the people of the cities flourish like the grass of the fields.
72.17: May his name endure forever; may it continue as long as the sun endures! May all peoples find blessing through him, and all nations call him blessed.
72.18: Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel, who alone does wondrous things.
72.19: Blessed be his glorious name forever; may the whole earth be filled with his glory! Amen and Amen.
72.20: The prayers of David, the son of Jesse, are ended.

73.1: A Psalm of Asaph. Truly God is good to Israel, to those who are pure in heart.
73.2: But as for me, my feet nearly slipped; my steps almost gave way.
73.3: For I was envious of the arrogant when I saw how the wicked prosper.
73.4: For they have no anguish in their death; their bodies are sleek and strong.
73.5: They are not in trouble like other people; they are not plagued like the rest of mankind.
73.6: Therefore pride adorns them like a necklace; violence wraps them like a garment.
73.7: Their eyes bulge with prosperity; the desires of their heart exceed all bounds.
73.8: They scoff and speak with malice; they talk of oppression with arrogance.
73.9: They direct their mouths against the heavens, and their tongue ranges across the earth.
73.10: Therefore people keep returning to them, and a full cup of water is drained by them.
73.11: They say, 'How can God know? Does the Most High have knowledge?'
73.12: See — these are the wicked, always at ease, growing in wealth.
73.13: Surely I have kept my heart clean for nothing, and washed my hands in innocence in vain.
73.14: For all day long I have been afflicted, and punishment comes every morning.
73.15: If I had said, 'I will speak this way,' I would have betrayed the generation of your children.
73.16: When I tried to make sense of all this, it was a torment before my eyes —
73.17: until I entered the sanctuary of God; then I grasped their end.
73.18: Surely you have placed them on slippery ground; you cast them down to desolation.
73.19: How suddenly they are destroyed, completely swept away by terrors!
73.20: Like a dream that vanishes when one awakes, so when you arise, O Lord, you despise their fleeting form.
73.21: When my heart was embittered and I was pierced in my inner being —
73.22: I was brutish and ignorant; I was no better than an animal before you.
73.23: Yet I am always with you; you hold me by my right hand.
73.24: You guide me with your counsel, and afterward you will receive me into glory.
73.25: Whom in heaven do I have but you? And on earth there is nothing I desire besides you.
73.26: My flesh and my heart may fail, but God is the strength of my heart and my portion forever.
73.27: For behold, those who are far from you will perish; you destroy all who are unfaithful to you.
73.28: But as for me, it is good to be near God; I have made the Lord GOD my refuge, that I may declare all your works.

74.1: A Maskil of Asaph. O God, why have you rejected us forever? Why does your anger smolder against the sheep of your pasture?
74.2: Remember your congregation, which you acquired long ago, the tribe of your inheritance, which you redeemed — Mount Zion, where you have made your dwelling.
74.3: Make your way to the perpetual ruins; the enemy has wrought complete destruction in the sanctuary.
74.4: Your enemies have roared in the midst of your assembly; they have set up their own banners as signs.
74.5: They were like men wielding axes to fell a dense forest.
74.6: All its carved paneling they smashed apart with axes and hammers.
74.7: They set your sanctuary on fire; they defiled the dwelling place of your name by pulling it down to the ground.
74.8: They said in their hearts, 'We will crush them all together'; they burned down every meeting place of God throughout the land.
74.9: We see none of our signs; there is no longer a prophet; and none of us knows how long this will last.
74.10: How long, O God, will the enemy mock you? Is the adversary to revile your name forever?
74.11: Why do you hold back your hand, your right hand? Draw it out from under your garment and act!
74.12: Yet God my King is from of old, bringing salvation in the midst of the earth.
74.13: You split the sea apart by your power; you shattered the heads of the sea monsters on the waters.
74.14: You crushed the many heads of Leviathan; you gave his carcass as food for the creatures of the wilderness.
74.15: You split open springs and streams; you dried up the ever-flowing rivers.
74.16: The day belongs to you; the night also is yours; you established the light and the sun.
74.17: You have fixed all the boundaries of the earth; you have made both summer and winter.
74.18: Remember this, O LORD: the enemy has mocked, and a foolish people has reviled your name.
74.19: Do not hand over your dove to the pack of enemies; do not forget the community of your poor forever.
74.20: Look to your covenant, for the dark corners of the land are filled with dens of violence.
74.21: Do not let the oppressed be turned away in shame; let the poor and needy praise your name.
74.22: Rise up, O God, and defend your cause; remember how fools mock you every single day.
74.23: Do not forget the clamor of your enemies; the uproar of those who defy you rises without ceasing.

75.1: To the choirmaster. According to 'Do Not Destroy.' A Psalm. A Song of Asaph. We give thanks to you, O God; we give thanks, for your name is near; we declare your wondrous deeds.
75.2: When I choose the appointed time, I will judge with perfect equity.
75.3: When the earth totters and all its people with it, it is I who hold its pillars firm. Selah.
75.4: I say to the arrogant, 'Stop your boasting,' and to the wicked, 'Do not raise your horn.'
75.5: Do not raise your horn on high; do not speak with an arrogant neck.
75.6: For advancement comes neither from east nor west, nor from the wilderness of the south.
75.7: but God is the judge; he brings one person down and raises another up.
75.8: For in the hand of the LORD there is a cup of foaming wine, well mixed; he pours it out, and all the wicked of the earth shall drain it to the very dregs.
75.9: But as for me, I will declare this forever; I will sing praises to the God of Jacob.
75.10: All the horns of the wicked I will cut down, but the horns of the righteous shall be lifted high.

76.1: To the choirmaster, with stringed instruments. A Psalm. A Song of Asaph. God is known in Judah; his name is great in Israel.
76.2: His tabernacle is in Salem; his dwelling place is in Zion.
76.3: There he shattered the flashing arrows, the shield, the sword, and all the weapons of war. Selah.
76.4: You are glorious and majestic, more than the everlasting mountains of plunder.
76.5: The valiant warriors have been stripped of their plunder; they have fallen into the sleep of death; not one soldier could lift a hand.
76.6: At your rebuke, O God of Jacob, both chariot and horse fell into a dead sleep.
76.7: But you — you alone are to be feared. Who can stand before you when your anger is kindled?
76.8: From heaven you pronounced judgment; the earth feared and stood still —
76.9: when God arose to judge, to save all the humble of the earth. Selah.
76.10: Surely human fury will bring you praise; whatever wrath remains you will restrain.
76.11: Make vows to the LORD your God and keep them; let all the surrounding nations bring tribute to the one who is to be feared.
76.12: He cuts off the spirit of princes; he is fearsome to the kings of the earth.

77.1: To the director of music. To Jeduthun. A Psalm of Asaph. I called out to God with my full voice; I cried to God, and he listened.
77.2: In the day of my trouble I sought the Lord; through the night my hand was outstretched without ceasing; my soul refused to be comforted.
77.3: I thought of God and I groaned; I poured out my grief until my spirit grew faint. Selah
77.4: You kept my eyes wide open through the night; I was too troubled to speak.
77.5: I think about the days of old, the years of long ago.
77.6: I recall my song in the night; I ponder in my heart, and my spirit searches deeply.
77.7: Will the Lord reject us forever? Will he never again be pleased with us?
77.8: Has his steadfast love ceased once and for all? Has his promise failed for every generation to come?
77.9: Has God forgotten to show grace? Has he locked away his compassion in his anger? Selah
77.10: Then I said, 'This is the sickness that is killing me — that the right hand of the Most High seems to have changed.' So I will remember what that right hand once did.
77.11: I will call to mind the works of the LORD; yes, I will remember your wonders from of old.
77.12: I will meditate on all your works and think carefully about everything you have done.
77.13: O God, your path runs through what is holy; what god anywhere is as great as our God?
77.14: You are the God who performs wonders; you have declared your power among the nations.
77.15: With your strong arm you redeemed your people, the descendants of Jacob and Joseph. Selah
77.16: The waters saw you, O God; the waters saw you and recoiled; the very depths were shaken.
77.17: The clouds poured down rain; the sky resounded; your arrows flashed out in every direction.
77.18: Your thunder rolled through the churning heavens; your lightning illuminated the world; the earth trembled and quaked.
77.19: Your path went through the sea, your way through the great deep; but your footprints could not be found.
77.20: You guided your people like a flock, through the leadership of Moses and Aaron.

78.1: A contemplative poem of Asaph. Listen, O my people, to my instruction; pay close attention to what I am about to say.
78.2: I will open my mouth with a parable; I will speak of things hidden from ages past.
78.3: What we have heard and come to know, what our ancestors passed down to us—
78.4: we will not conceal from their children. We will tell the next generation of the LORD's praiseworthy deeds, his power, and the wonderful things he has done.
78.5: For he established a formal testimony in Jacob and ordained a law in Israel, which he commanded our ancestors to teach their children,
78.6: so that the generation to come would know these things — even children not yet born — and when they grew up, would tell them to their own children,
78.7: so they would place their confident hope in God, and would not forget what he has done, but would keep his commands,
78.8: and would not be like their ancestors — a stubborn and rebellious generation that did not fix its heart on God and whose spirit was not faithful to him.
78.9: The men of Ephraim, though armed with bows, turned back when the day of battle came.
78.10: They failed to keep God's covenant and refused to live by his instruction.
78.11: They forgot what he had done and the wonders he had shown them.
78.12: He performed remarkable deeds in plain sight of their ancestors — in the land of Egypt, in the region of Zoan.
78.13: He split the sea and brought them through; he made the water stand in walls.
78.14: In the daytime he led them with a cloud, and all night long with a column of fire.
78.15: He split open rocks in the wilderness and gave them water to drink as from the great deep.
78.16: He brought streams out of the rock and made the water flow down in rivers.
78.17: Yet they kept on sinning against him, provoking the Most High in the wilderness.
78.18: They deliberately tested God in their hearts, demanding food to satisfy their cravings.
78.19: They spoke against God, saying, 'Can God really lay out a meal here in the wilderness?'
78.20: 'Look, he hit a rock and water poured out, streams overflowed — but can he also supply bread? Can he provide meat for his whole people?'
78.21: Therefore the LORD heard what they said and became furious; a fire broke out against Jacob, and his anger rose up against Israel,
78.22: because they refused to believe in God and put no trust in his power to save.
78.23: Yet he had already commanded the clouds above and opened the doors of heaven;
78.24: He had rained down manna for them to eat and had given them grain from heaven.
78.25: Human beings ate the food of angels; he sent them abundant provisions.
78.26: He caused an east wind to blow through the sky; and by his power he drove the south wind.
78.27: He rained meat on them as thick as dust and birds with wings as numerous as grains of sea sand.
78.28: He dropped it right in the middle of their camp, all around where they lived.
78.29: They ate and were completely satisfied; he had given them exactly what they wanted.
78.30: But they had not turned from their craving; the food was still in their mouths
78.31: when God's wrath came down on them and slew the strongest among them and struck down the young men of Israel.
78.32: Despite all of this, they went on sinning and refused to believe even his miracles.
78.33: So he made their days dissolve into emptiness and their years end in sudden fright.
78.34: Whenever he struck them down, they would search for him; they would turn around and sincerely seek God.
78.35: They recalled that God was their rock, and the Most High God was their redeemer.
78.36: But they only said what he wanted to hear; they spoke to him with lying tongues.
78.37: Their hearts were not truly directed toward him; they were not faithful to his covenant.
78.38: Yet he, being full of compassion, covered over their guilt and did not destroy them; again and again he turned back his anger and did not unleash all his fury.
78.39: He remembered that they were only flesh — a passing wind that does not come back.
78.40: How many times they defied him in the wilderness and caused him grief in the wasteland!
78.41: They kept turning back and testing God, pressing against the Holy One of Israel.
78.42: They forgot what his hand had done, and the day he rescued them from their oppressor—
78.43: how he had displayed his miraculous signs in Egypt and his wonders in the region of Zoan.
78.44: He turned their rivers into blood so that they were unable to drink from their streams.
78.45: He sent swarms of flies to devour them, and frogs that brought ruin upon them.
78.46: He handed their harvest over to the locust and their labor to the swarming caterpillar.
78.47: He struck their vines down with hail and their sycamore trees with killing frost.
78.48: He surrendered their cattle to the hail and their flocks to lightning strikes.
78.49: He unleashed his fierce anger upon them — his wrath, indignation, and distress — a company of destroying messengers.
78.50: He made a clear way for his wrath; he did not withhold their lives from death, but surrendered them to the plague.
78.51: He killed all the firstborn in Egypt, the prime of their strength in the dwellings of Ham.
78.52: But he brought his own people out like sheep and led them through the wilderness like a flock.
78.53: He guided them in safety so that they had nothing to fear; the sea swallowed their enemies.
78.54: He brought them to his sacred territory, to the mountain that his right hand had won.
78.55: He expelled the nations from before them, assigned their land as an inheritance by lot, and made the tribes of Israel to dwell in their territory.
78.56: Nevertheless they tested and defied the Most High God and refused to keep his commandments.
78.57: They turned back and acted faithlessly, just as their ancestors had; they bent away like a defective bow.
78.58: They made him angry with their hilltop shrines and roused his jealousy with their carved idols.
78.59: When God heard all this, he was incensed; he thoroughly rejected Israel.
78.60: He abandoned the tabernacle at Shiloh, the tent he had established among his people.
78.61: He allowed his strength to go into exile and his glory into enemy hands.
78.62: He surrendered his people to the sword and turned against his inheritance in anger.
78.63: Fire devoured their young men, and their young women received no marriage praise.
78.64: Their priests were cut down by the sword; their widows could not even weep.
78.65: Then the Lord awoke as if from sleep, like a warrior who rises vigorous from wine.
78.66: He drove his enemies back and put them to shame that will not end.
78.67: He refused the tent of Joseph and did not choose the tribe of Ephraim;
78.68: Instead he chose the tribe of Judah — Mount Zion, which he loved.
78.69: He built his sanctuary like the heights of heaven, like the earth that he has set in place forever.
78.70: He chose David his servant and took him from the sheep pens.
78.71: From tending the nursing ewes he brought him to shepherd Jacob his people, and Israel his inheritance.
78.72: He tended them with the integrity of his heart and guided them with the skill of his hands.

79.1: A Psalm of Asaph. O God, the nations have invaded your inheritance; they have defiled your holy temple; they have laid Jerusalem in ruins.
79.2: They have given the corpses of your servants as food to the birds of the heavens, the flesh of your godly ones to the beasts of the earth.
79.3: They have poured out their blood like water all around Jerusalem, and there was no one to bury the dead.
79.4: We have become a reproach to our neighbors, a mockery and derision to those around us.
79.5: How long, O LORD? Will you be angry forever? Will your jealousy burn like fire?
79.6: Pour out your wrath on the nations that do not know you, and on the kingdoms that do not call on your name!
79.7: For they have devoured Jacob and laid waste his homeland.
79.8: Do not hold our former iniquities against us; let your compassion come quickly to meet us, for we are utterly brought low.
79.9: Help us, O God of our salvation, for the glory of your name; deliver us and forgive our sins, for your name's sake!
79.10: Why should the nations say, 'Where is their God?' Before our eyes, let the avenging of your servants' shed blood be known among the nations.
79.11: Let the groaning of the prisoner come before you; according to your great power, preserve those condemned to die!
79.12: Return to our neighbors sevenfold into their bosom the reproach with which they have taunted you, O Lord.
79.13: Then we your people, the sheep of your pasture, will give you thanks forever; from generation to generation we will recount your praise.

80.1: To the choirmaster. To the tune 'Lilies of the Covenant.' A Psalm of Asaph. Give ear, O Shepherd of Israel, you who lead Joseph like a flock! You who are enthroned above the cherubim, shine forth!
80.2: Before Ephraim and Benjamin and Manasseh, stir up your might and come to save us!
80.3: Restore us, O God; let your face shine upon us, that we may be saved!
80.4: O LORD God of hosts, how long will you be angry with your people's prayers?
80.5: You have fed them with the bread of tears and given them tears to drink in full measure.
80.6: You make us a bone of contention for our neighbors, and our enemies mock us.
80.7: Restore us, O God of hosts; let your face shine upon us, that we may be saved!
80.8: You brought a vine out of Egypt; you drove out the nations and planted it.
80.9: You cleared the ground before it; it took deep root and filled all the land.
80.10: The mountains were covered with its shade, and its branches were like towering cedars.
80.11: It sent its branches to the sea and its shoots to the River.
80.12: Why then have you broken down its walls, so that all who pass along the road pluck its fruit?
80.13: The boar from the forest ravages it, and all the wild creatures of the field feed on it.
80.14: Return, we pray, O God of hosts! Look down from heaven, see, and tend this vine!
80.15: Look at the stock your right hand planted, and the son you made strong for yourself.
80.16: It is burned with fire and cut down; may they perish at the rebuke of your face!
80.17: Let your hand be on the man of your right hand, the son of man whom you have made strong for yourself.
80.18: Then we will not turn back from you; give us life, and we will call on your name!
80.19: Restore us, O LORD God of hosts; let your face shine upon us, that we may be saved!

81.1: To the choirmaster. To the tune of the Gittith. A Psalm of Asaph. Sing aloud to God our strength; shout for joy to the God of Jacob!
81.2: Raise a song; sound the tambourine, the sweet lyre with the harp.
81.3: Blow the trumpet at the new moon, at the full moon, on our feast day.
81.4: For it is a statute for Israel, a rule given by the God of Jacob.
81.5: He established it as a decree in Joseph when he went out over the land of Egypt. I hear a voice I had not known:
81.6: 'I removed the burden from his shoulder; his hands were freed from the basket.'
81.7: 'In your distress you called, and I rescued you; I answered you from the hidden place of thunder; I tested you at the waters of Meribah. Selah.'
81.8: 'Listen, O my people, while I admonish you! O Israel, if you would only listen to me!'
81.9: 'There must be no foreign god among you; you must not bow down to any alien god.'
81.10: 'I am the LORD your God, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt. Open your mouth wide, and I will fill it.'
81.11: 'But my people did not listen to my voice; Israel would not submit to me.'
81.12: 'So I gave them over to their stubborn hearts, to follow their own devices.'
81.13: 'If only my people would listen to me, if only Israel would walk in my ways!'
81.14: 'I would quickly have subdued their enemies and turned my hand against their foes.'
81.15: 'Those who hate the LORD would have cringed before him, and Israel's time of peace would have endured forever.'
81.16: 'I would have fed you with the finest of the wheat, and with honey from the rock I would have satisfied you.'

82.1: A Psalm of Asaph. God has taken his place in the divine assembly; in the midst of the gods he holds judgment:
82.2: 'How long will you go on judging unjustly and showing partiality to the wicked? Selah.'
82.3: 'Defend the poor and fatherless; maintain the right of the afflicted and the destitute.'
82.4: 'Rescue the poor and needy; save them from the grip of the wicked.'
82.5: 'They have no knowledge and no understanding; they walk about in darkness, and all the foundations of the earth are shaken.'
82.6: 'I declared, "You are gods, sons of the Most High, every one of you."'
82.7: 'Nevertheless, you will die like mere mortals and fall like any fallen ruler.'
82.8: Rise up, O God, judge the earth; for all the nations belong to you!

83.1: A Song. A Psalm of Asaph. O God, do not remain silent; do not hold your peace or be still, O God!
83.2: For behold, your enemies are in an uproar; those who hate you have raised their heads.
83.3: They devise cunning plans against your people and scheme against your treasured ones.
83.4: They say, 'Come, let us wipe them out as a nation; let the name of Israel be remembered no more!'
83.5: For they conspire with one accord; they have made a covenant against you —
83.6: the tents of Edom and the Ishmaelites, Moab and the Hagrites,
83.7: Gebal and Ammon and Amalek, Philistia with the inhabitants of Tyre,
83.8: Assyria too has joined them; they lend their strength to the children of Lot. Selah.
83.9: Do to them as you did to Midian, as to Sisera and Jabin at the river Kishon —
83.10: who were destroyed at En-dor and became dung for the ground.
83.11: Make their nobles like Oreb and Zeeb; make all their princes like Zebah and Zalmunna —
83.12: who said, 'Let us seize for ourselves the pastures of God.'
83.13: O my God, make them like whirling dust, like chaff before the wind.
83.14: As fire consumes the forest, as the flame sets the mountains ablaze —
83.15: so pursue them with your storm and terrify them with your tempest.
83.16: Fill their faces with shame, that they may seek your name, O LORD.
83.17: Let them be put to shame and dismayed forever; let them be disgraced and perish!
83.18: Let them know that you alone, whose name is the LORD, are the Most High over all the earth.

84.1: To the choirmaster. To the tune of the Gittith. A Psalm for the sons of Korah. How lovely is your dwelling place, O LORD of hosts!
84.2: My soul longs, yes, faints for the courts of the LORD; my heart and my flesh cry out for the living God.
84.3: Even the sparrow has found a home, and the swallow a nest for herself where she may lay her young — even your altars, O LORD of hosts, my King and my God!
84.4: Blessed are those who dwell in your house; they are ever singing your praise! Selah.
84.5: Blessed is the man whose strength is in you, in whose heart are the pilgrim roads.
84.6: As they pass through the valley of Baca they make it a place of springs; the early rain also covers it with pools.
84.7: They go from strength to strength; each one appears before God in Zion.
84.8: O LORD God of hosts, hear my prayer; give ear, O God of Jacob! Selah.
84.9: Behold our shield, O God; look on the face of your anointed!
84.10: For a single day in your courts is better than a thousand elsewhere. I would rather stand at the threshold of the house of my God than dwell comfortably in the tents of the wicked.
84.11: For the LORD God is a sun and shield; the LORD bestows favor and honor. No good thing does he withhold from those who walk with integrity.
84.12: O LORD of hosts, blessed is the one who trusts in you!

85.1: A Psalm of the Sons of Korah. For the choir director. O LORD, you showed favor to your land; you restored the fortunes of Jacob.
85.2: You forgave the iniquity of your people; you covered over all their sin. Selah.
85.3: You withdrew all your wrath; you turned away from your fierce anger.
85.4: Restore us again, O God of our salvation, and set aside your displeasure against us.
85.5: Will you be angry with us forever? Will you extend your anger through every generation?
85.6: Will you not revive us again, so that your people may rejoice in you?
85.7: Show us your steadfast love, O LORD, and grant us your salvation.
85.8: I will listen to what God the LORD says; for he will speak peace to his people and to his faithful ones — only let them not return to folly.
85.9: Surely his salvation is near to those who fear him, so that glory may dwell in our land.
85.10: Steadfast love and faithfulness meet; righteousness and peace kiss.
85.11: Faithfulness springs up from the earth, and righteousness looks down from heaven.
85.12: Yes, the LORD will give what is good, and our land will yield its harvest.
85.13: Righteousness will march before him and prepare a path for his steps.

86.1: A Prayer of David. Turn your ear toward me, O LORD, and answer me, for I am poor and needy.
86.2: Preserve my soul, for I am devoted to you; save your servant who trusts in you — O my God.
86.3: Be gracious to me, O Lord, for I cry out to you all day long.
86.4: Gladden the soul of your servant, for to you, O Lord, I lift up my soul.
86.5: For you, O Lord, are good and forgiving, abounding in steadfast love for all who call on you.
86.6: Give ear to my prayer, O LORD; listen to the voice of my plea for mercy.
86.7: On the day of my trouble I call to you, for you answer me.
86.8: There is no one like you among the gods, O Lord, and no works compare to yours.
86.9: All the nations you have made will come and bow before you, O Lord, and they will glorify your name.
86.10: For you are great and you do wonders; you alone are God.
86.11: Teach me your way, O LORD, that I may walk in your truth; give me an undivided heart to reverence your name.
86.12: I will thank you, O Lord my God, with my whole heart; I will glorify your name forever.
86.13: For your steadfast love toward me is great; you have rescued my soul from the lowest depths of Sheol.
86.14: O God, arrogant men have risen up against me; a band of ruthless men seeks my life — they do not keep you before them.
86.15: But you, O Lord, are a compassionate and gracious God, slow to anger, abounding in steadfast love and faithfulness.
86.16: Turn to me and be gracious to me; give your strength to your servant, and save the son of your maidservant.
86.17: Show me a sign of your favor, so that those who hate me will see and be ashamed, because you, O LORD, have helped me and comforted me.

87.1: A Psalm. A Song of the Sons of Korah. His foundation is on the holy mountains.
87.2: The LORD loves the gates of Zion more than all the dwelling places of Jacob.
87.3: Glorious things are spoken of you, O city of God. Selah.
87.4: Among those who acknowledge me I will name Rahab and Babylon; and behold — Philistia, Tyre, and Ethiopia: 'This one was born there.'
87.5: Of Zion it will be said, 'This one and that one were born in her,' and the Most High himself will establish her.
87.6: When the LORD records the enrollment of the peoples, he will write, 'This one was born there.' Selah.
87.7: Singers and dancers alike say, 'All my springs of life are in you.'

88.1: A Song. A Psalm of the Sons of Korah. For the choir director. According to Mahalath Leannoth. A Maskil of Heman the Ezrahite. O LORD, God of my salvation, I cry out to you day and night.
88.2: Let my prayer come before you; turn your ear toward my cry.
88.3: For my soul is saturated with troubles, and my life draws near to Sheol.
88.4: I am reckoned among those who go down to the pit; I have become like a man with no strength at all.
88.5: Like one cast loose among the dead — like the slain who lie in the grave — those you no longer remember, cut off from your hand.
88.6: You have placed me in the lowest pit, in utter darkness, in the depths below.
88.7: Your wrath presses down hard on me, and you have battered me with all your waves. Selah.
88.8: You have pushed my companions far away from me; you have made me repulsive to them. I am sealed in and cannot get out.
88.9: My eye wastes away with grief; O LORD, I call to you every day; I spread out my hands toward you.
88.10: Do you perform wonders for the dead? Can the departed arise and praise you? Selah.
88.11: Is your steadfast love announced in the grave? Is your faithfulness spoken of in Abaddon?
88.12: Will your wonders be known in the darkness? Will your righteousness be heard of in the land of forgetting?
88.13: But I, O LORD, cry out to you; in the morning my prayer comes before you.
88.14: O LORD, why do you reject my soul? Why do you hide your face from me?
88.15: I have been afflicted and near death since my youth; I suffer your terrors; I am overcome.
88.16: Your fierce wrath has swept over me; your terrors have destroyed me.
88.17: They surround me like water all day long; they close in on me together.
88.18: You have put far from me those who love me and my friends; my companions are in darkness.

89.1: A contemplative poem of Ethan the Ezrahite. I will sing of the LORD's steadfast love forever; with my mouth I will proclaim your faithfulness to every generation.
89.2: For I declared, 'Your steadfast love is built to stand forever; your faithfulness is established in the heavens themselves.'
89.3: 'I have made a covenant with my chosen one; I have sworn an oath to David my servant:'
89.4: 'I will make your line endure forever and build your throne for every generation to come.' Selah
89.5: The heavens praise your wonders, O LORD; your faithfulness is declared in the congregation of the holy ones.
89.6: For who in all the heavens can be compared with the LORD? Who among the heavenly beings is like the LORD?
89.7: A God greatly feared in the assembly of the holy ones and awesome above all who surround him.
89.8: LORD God of hosts, who is as powerful as you, LORD? Your faithfulness wraps around you on every side.
89.9: You govern the surging sea; when its waves rear up, you calm them.
89.10: You crushed Rahab like a slaughtered carcass; with your mighty arm you scattered your enemies.
89.11: The heavens belong to you; the earth also is yours; you founded the world and everything in it.
89.12: North and south — you created them; Tabor and Hermon sing for joy in your name.
89.13: Your arm is mighty; your hand is strong; your right hand is raised high.
89.14: Righteousness and justice are the foundation of your throne; steadfast love and faithfulness lead the way before you.
89.15: Blessed is the people who know the joyful shout; they walk, LORD, in the light of your face.
89.16: They rejoice in your name all day long and are lifted up by your righteousness.
89.17: For you are the splendor of their strength; by your favor our strength is raised high.
89.18: For our shield belongs to the LORD; our king is the Holy One of Israel.
89.19: You spoke in a vision to your faithful servants and said: 'I have given help to a warrior; I have raised up a young man chosen from among the people.'
89.20: 'I have found David my servant; with my holy oil I have anointed him,'
89.21: 'My hand will uphold him steadily; my arm will give him strength.'
89.22: 'No enemy will overpower him; no wicked person will oppress him.'
89.23: 'I will shatter his enemies before his eyes and defeat all who hate him.'
89.24: 'My faithfulness and steadfast love will be with him; through my name his strength will be raised high.'
89.25: 'I will extend his dominion to the sea and his right hand over the rivers.'
89.26: 'He will call out to me, "You are my Father, my God, and the Rock who saves me!"'
89.27: 'I will appoint him as my firstborn, the highest of all the kings of the earth.'
89.28: 'I will maintain my steadfast love for him forever, and my covenant with him will be secure.'
89.29: 'His line will continue forever, and his throne will last as long as the heavens above.'
89.30: 'If his descendants abandon my instruction and refuse to live by my rulings,'
89.31: 'if they break my decrees and fail to keep my commandments,'
89.32: 'then I will punish their rebellion with a rod and their sin with blows.'
89.33: 'But I will not withdraw my steadfast love from him or betray my faithfulness.'
89.34: 'I will not break my covenant or change what I have spoken.'
89.35: 'I have sworn by my own holiness once and for all; I will not deceive David.'
89.36: 'His descendants will endure forever; his throne will last as long as the sun shines before me.'
89.37: 'Like the moon it will be established forever, that faithful witness set in the heavens.' Selah
89.38: But now you have cast off and rejected him; you are enraged against your anointed king.
89.39: You have repudiated the covenant with your servant; you have thrown his crown into the dust.
89.40: You have broken down all his walls and reduced his fortifications to rubble.
89.41: All who pass by strip him of what he has; he has become a mockery to his neighbors.
89.42: You have strengthened the hand of his enemies; you have let all who hate him celebrate.
89.43: You have blunted his sword in battle and failed to hold him up when he fought.
89.44: You have stripped away his glory and hurled his throne down to the ground.
89.45: You have shortened the days of his prime and wrapped him in shame. Selah
89.46: How long, O LORD? Will you hide yourself away forever? How long will your anger blaze like fire?
89.47: Remember how short my span of life is! For what emptiness you have made all the children of man!
89.48: What man can go on living and never see death? Who can rescue his life from the grip of the grave? Selah
89.49: Lord, where is your steadfast love of old, which in your faithfulness you swore to David?
89.50: Remember, Lord, the mocking your servants endure — how I bear in my heart the taunts of all those nations,
89.51: with which your enemies, LORD, have taunted, mocking every step of your anointed king.
89.52: Blessed be the LORD forever! Amen and Amen.

90.1: A Prayer of Moses the man of God. Lord, you have been our home in every generation.
90.2: Before the mountains were born, or you had shaped the earth and the world — from age to age you are God.
90.3: You turn human beings back to dust and say, 'Return, O children of Adam!'
90.4: For a thousand years in your sight are like yesterday after it has passed — like a single watch in the night.
90.5: You sweep them away like a sudden flood; they are like a dream, like grass that sprouts in the morning.
90.6: In the morning it blossoms and shoots up; by evening it is mown down and dry.
90.7: We are brought to an end by your anger; by your fury we are overwhelmed.
90.8: You have placed our sins before you and our hidden faults in the full light of your presence.
90.9: All our days slip away under your wrath; we bring our years to an end like a breath.
90.10: The span of our years is seventy — or if we are strong, eighty — but even then they contain only toil and grief; they pass quickly and we are gone.
90.11: Who truly grasps the force of your anger? Who fears your wrath as it deserves?
90.12: Teach us, then, to count our days aright, that we may acquire a wise heart.
90.13: Turn back, LORD! How long will this go on? Have mercy on your servants!
90.14: Fill us in the morning with your steadfast love, so that we may celebrate and rejoice through all our days.
90.15: Give us gladness to match the days you have made us suffer — joy equal to the years we have known trouble.
90.16: Let your work appear to your servants and your splendor shine on their children.
90.17: May the favor of the LORD our God rest upon us; establish the work of our hands — yes, establish the work of our hands!

91.1: Whoever lives in the shelter of the Most High will rest in the shadow of the Almighty.
91.2: I will say to the LORD, 'You are my refuge and my fortress, my God, in whom I trust.'
91.3: He will rescue you from the hunter's trap and from every deadly disease.
91.4: He will shelter you under his feathers; you will find refuge beneath his wings; his faithfulness is your shield and armor.
91.5: You will not fear the terror that comes in the night or the arrow that flies in the daylight,
91.6: nor the disease that moves through the dark or the plague that strikes at noon.
91.7: A thousand may fall beside you, ten thousand at your right hand — but it will not reach you.
91.8: You will only watch with your own eyes and see the fate of the wicked.
91.9: Because you have made the LORD your refuge, the Most High your dwelling place,
91.10: no evil will happen to you, and no plague will come close to your home.
91.11: He will command his angels to guard you in all your ways.
91.12: They will hold you up in their arms so your foot does not strike against a stone.
91.13: You will walk on the lion and the cobra; you will trample the young lion and the serpent underfoot.
91.14: 'Because he clings to me in love,' declares the LORD, 'I will rescue him; I will set him beyond reach, because he truly knows who I am.'
91.15: 'When he calls on me I will answer; I will be with him in his trouble; I will rescue him and give him honor.'
91.16: 'I will give him a full life and let him see my saving power.'

92.1: A Psalm. A Song for the Sabbath day. It is good to give thanks to the LORD and to sing praises to your name, O Most High,
92.2: to declare your steadfast love each morning and your faithfulness through every night,
92.3: to the music of the ten-stringed lute and the melody of the harp, with the rippling of the lyre.
92.4: You have made me glad, LORD, by what you have done; I sing for joy over all that your hands have made.
92.5: How great are your works, LORD! How utterly deep are your thoughts!
92.6: A senseless person cannot grasp it, and a fool does not understand:
92.7: the wicked may spring up like grass and every evildoer may flourish, but they are headed for eternal ruin —
92.8: while you, LORD, are exalted on high forever.
92.9: Yes, your enemies, LORD — your enemies will certainly perish; all who do evil will be scattered.
92.10: But you have raised my strength high like the horn of a wild bull; you have anointed me with fresh oil.
92.11: My eyes have seen the downfall of my enemies; my ears have heard the ruin of those who rose against me.
92.12: The righteous person flourishes like the palm tree and grows tall like a cedar of Lebanon.
92.13: Planted in the house of the LORD, they will flourish in the courts of our God.
92.14: In old age they still produce fruit; they remain vigorous and full of life,
92.15: bearing witness that the LORD is just — he is my rock, and there is nothing wrong in him.

93.1: The LORD reigns! He is clothed with majesty; the LORD is dressed in strength and has girded himself. The world is firmly established — it cannot be shaken.
93.2: Your throne has been established from ancient times; you yourself are from everlasting.
93.3: The great waters have lifted up their voice, LORD; the rushing floods have lifted up their voice and their crashing waves.
93.4: Mightier than the roar of many waters, mightier than the waves of the sea — the LORD on high is mighty.
93.5: Your decrees are completely trustworthy; holiness adorns your house, LORD, forevermore.

94.1: O LORD, God who avenges — God of justice, arise and shine forth!
94.2: Rise up, Judge of the earth; pay back to the proud what they deserve!
94.3: How long, LORD, will the wicked — how long will the wicked rejoice in triumph?
94.4: They spew proud words without end; all who do evil brag without restraint.
94.5: They crush your people, LORD, and oppress those who belong to you.
94.6: They murder the widow and the foreigner and kill the fatherless.
94.7: And they say, 'The LORD never sees it; the God of Jacob pays no attention.'
94.8: Grasp this, you most senseless among the people — fools, when will you become wise?
94.9: The one who made the ear — does he not hear? The one who formed the eye — does he not see?
94.10: The one who corrects the nations — will he not call them to account? The one who teaches all people —
94.11: the LORD knows what human beings think — that their thoughts are nothing but vapor.
94.12: Blessed is the one you discipline, LORD, and instruct from your teaching,
94.13: giving him rest in days of trouble until a pit is dug for the wicked.
94.14: The LORD will not abandon his people or desert his inheritance.
94.15: Surely justice will return to the righteous, and all who are upright in heart will pursue it.
94.16: Who will stand up for me against the wicked? Who will take my side against evildoers?
94.17: If the LORD had not been my help, I would very soon have lain in the silence of death.
94.18: When I felt my foot giving way, your steadfast love, LORD, held me steady.
94.19: When anxious thoughts multiply inside me, your comfort brings delight to my soul.
94.20: Can a corrupt throne have any fellowship with you — one that enacts harm through its own laws?
94.21: They join forces against the lives of the righteous and condemn the innocent to death.
94.22: But the LORD is my strong fortress, and my God is the rock where I take shelter.
94.23: He will pay them back for their sin and destroy them for their evil; the LORD our God will wipe them out.

95.1: Come, let us sing joyfully to the LORD; let us shout aloud to the Rock who saves us!
95.2: Let us come before him with thanksgiving and celebrate him with songs of praise.
95.3: For the LORD is a great God, a great King greater than all other gods.
95.4: The deepest places of the earth are in his hand, and the mountain peaks belong to him.
95.5: The sea is his — he made it; his own hands shaped the dry land.
95.6: Come, let us bow down and worship; let us kneel before the LORD who made us.
95.7: For he is our God, and we are the people of his pasture, the flock he tends. Today — if only you would hear his voice:
95.8: 'Do not make your hearts stubborn, as at Meribah — as on that day at Massah in the wilderness,'
95.9: 'Your ancestors put me to the test and challenged me, even after seeing all I had done.'
95.10: 'For forty years I was provoked by that generation and declared, "This people's heart keeps wandering; they have never learned my ways."'
95.11: 'So in my anger I swore: "They will never enter my rest."'

96.1: Sing a new song to the LORD; let all the earth sing to the LORD!
96.2: Sing to the LORD; praise his name; announce his salvation day after day.
96.3: Announce his glory among the nations and his wonderful deeds among all peoples.
96.4: For the LORD is great and most worthy of praise; he is to be held in awe above every other god.
96.5: All the gods of the nations are nothing — mere idols — but the LORD made the heavens.
96.6: Honor and majesty surround him; strength and beauty fill his sanctuary.
96.7: Give to the LORD, all you families of the nations — give to the LORD glory and strength.
96.8: Give to the LORD the honor his name deserves; bring your offering and come into his courts.
96.9: Worship the LORD in the splendor of his holiness; let all the earth tremble before him.
96.10: Declare among the nations: 'The LORD reigns! The world is set in place and cannot be shaken; he will judge the peoples with justice.'
96.11: Let the heavens rejoice and the earth be glad; let the sea and everything in it roar out.
96.12: Let the fields exult and everything in them; let all the trees of the forest shout for joy
96.13: before the LORD, for he is coming — coming to judge the earth. He will judge the world in righteousness and the peoples in his faithfulness.

103.1: A Psalm of David. Bless the LORD, O my soul; let every part of me bless his holy name!
103.2: Bless the LORD, O my soul, and forget not a single good thing he has done—
103.3: who forgives every one of your sins, who heals every one of your illnesses,
103.4: who redeems your life from destruction, who crowns you with steadfast love and compassion,
103.5: who satisfies you with good things so that your strength is renewed like the eagle's.
103.6: The LORD acts with righteousness and justice for all who are oppressed.
103.7: He revealed his ways to Moses and his mighty deeds to the people of Israel.
103.8: The LORD is compassionate and gracious, patient in anger and overflowing with steadfast love.
103.9: He does not bring charges forever, nor does he hold on to his anger without end.
103.10: He has not treated us as our sins deserve, nor paid us back measure for measure for our iniquities.
103.11: For as high as the heavens are above the earth, so great is his steadfast love for those who fear him;
103.12: As far as east is from west, that is how far he has removed our transgressions from us.
103.13: Just as a father is tender toward his children, so the LORD is tender toward those who fear him.
103.14: He knows exactly what we are made of; he has not forgotten that we are dust.
103.15: As for mortals, their days are like grass; they bloom like a wildflower of the field;
103.16: the wind sweeps over it and it vanishes, and the place where it stood no longer knows it.
103.17: But the steadfast love of the LORD is from age to age over those who fear him, and his righteousness reaches to their grandchildren,
103.18: to those who hold to his covenant and keep his commands in mind to live by them.
103.19: The LORD has set up his throne in the heavens; his kingdom governs everything that exists.
103.20: Bless the LORD, you his angels — his mighty warriors who carry out his commands, swift to obey his voice!
103.21: Bless the LORD, all his armies — all who serve him and carry out his purposes!
103.22: Bless the LORD, everything he has made, in every place his reign extends. Bless the LORD, O my soul!

104.1: Bless the LORD, O my soul! O LORD my God, how great you are — robed in honor and majesty,
104.2: wrapped in light as in a robe, spreading out the heavens like a tent curtain.
104.3: He sets the beams of his upper rooms on the waters; he mounts the clouds as his chariot; he travels on the wings of the wind;
104.4: He makes the winds his messengers and flashing fire his servants.
104.5: He fixed the earth on its foundations so that it will never be shaken.
104.6: You covered it with the deep like a cloak; the waters towered above the mountains.
104.7: At your rebuke they retreated; at the sound of your thunder they fled in haste.
104.8: The mountains rose and the valleys sank to the places you had set for them.
104.9: You drew a boundary they cannot cross, so the waters will never again engulf the earth.
104.10: He sends springs into the valleys; they run between the hills;
104.11: giving water to every wild animal; the wild donkeys slake their thirst there.
104.12: Along their banks the birds of the sky make their homes and sing among the branches.
104.13: From his upper chambers he sends rain down the mountains; the earth is filled by what he provides.
104.14: He makes grass grow for the cattle and plants for people to tend so they can bring food out of the ground —
104.15: wine that lifts the human heart, oil that makes the face glow, and bread that gives strength to the body.
104.16: The trees of the LORD drink deeply — the great cedars of Lebanon that he himself planted.
104.17: There the birds build their nests; the stork finds her home in the cypress trees.
104.18: The rugged mountains belong to the wild goats; the rocky crags shelter the rock badgers.
104.19: He appointed the moon to mark the seasons; the sun knows exactly when to set.
104.20: You bring on darkness and it becomes night, when all the forest creatures stir.
