104.21: The young lions roar as they hunt, looking to God for their food.
104.22: When the sun comes up, they slip away and stretch out in their lairs.
104.23: Then people go out to their work and labor on until the evening.
104.24: LORD, what a multitude of works you have made! You crafted them all in wisdom; the earth is full of what belongs to you.
104.25: And here is the sea — vast and wide — teeming with creatures beyond counting, great and small alike.
104.26: The ships sail there, and there is Leviathan, which you formed to frolic in the deep.
104.27: All of these look to you to give them their food when they need it.
104.28: When you give, they gather it in; when you open your hand, they are satisfied with good.
104.29: If you hide your face, they are terrified; if you take back their breath, they die and return to dust.
104.30: When you send out your Spirit, they are created, and you renew the surface of the earth.
104.31: May the glory of the LORD last forever; may the LORD take delight in what he has made!
104.32: He looks at the earth and it shakes; he touches the mountains and they pour out smoke.
104.33: I will sing to the LORD as long as I live; I will sing praise to my God for as long as I draw breath.
104.34: May my thoughts about him be sweet to him, as I find my joy in the LORD.
104.35: Let sinners vanish from the earth and let the wicked exist no longer! Bless the LORD, O my soul! Hallelujah!

97.1: The LORD reigns; let the earth rejoice! Let all the distant coastlands be glad!
97.2: Clouds and thick darkness surround him; righteousness and justice are the foundation of his throne.
97.3: Fire goes out before him and consumes his enemies on every side.
97.4: His lightnings illuminate the world; the earth sees and shakes with fear.
97.5: The mountains melt like wax before the LORD, before the Lord of all the earth.
97.6: The heavens proclaim his righteousness, and all the peoples behold his glory.
97.7: All who worship carved images are put to shame — those who boast in worthless idols. All gods bow down before him.
97.8: Zion heard and was glad, and the towns of Judah rejoiced because of your judgments, O LORD.
97.9: For you, LORD, are most high over all the earth; you are raised far above all gods.
97.10: You who love the LORD, hate evil! He guards the lives of his faithful and rescues them from the power of the wicked.
97.11: Light is sown for the righteous and gladness for the upright in heart.
97.12: Rejoice in the LORD, you who are righteous, and give thanks to his holy name!

98.1: A Psalm. Sing to the LORD a new song, for he has done wondrous things! His own right hand and his holy arm have gained him the victory.
98.2: The LORD has made his salvation known and has displayed his righteousness before the nations.
98.3: He has remembered his steadfast love and faithfulness to the house of Israel. All the ends of the earth have witnessed the salvation of our God.
98.4: Shout for joy to the LORD, all the earth! Burst into song and sing praises!
98.5: Sing praises to the LORD with the harp, with the harp and the sound of singing!
98.6: With trumpets and the blast of the ram's horn shout for joy before the King, the LORD!
98.7: Let the sea thunder with all that is in it, the world and all who live in it!
98.8: Let the rivers clap their hands; let the mountains sing together for joy!
98.9: Before the LORD, for he is coming to judge the earth! He will rule the world with righteousness and the peoples with fairness.

99.1: The LORD reigns; let the peoples tremble! He is enthroned above the cherubim; let the earth shake!
99.2: The LORD is great in Zion; he is raised high above all the peoples.
99.3: Let them praise your great and fearsome name — holy is he!
99.4: The mighty king loves justice. You have established fairness; you have carried out justice and righteousness in Jacob.
99.5: Exalt the LORD our God and bow down at his footstool — holy is he!
99.6: Moses and Aaron were among his priests; Samuel was among those who called on his name. They called out to the LORD, and he answered them.
99.7: He spoke to them in the pillar of cloud; they kept his testimonies and the law he gave them.
99.8: LORD our God, you answered them; you were a God who forgave them, yet you held them accountable for their offenses.
99.9: Exalt the LORD our God and worship at his holy mountain, for the LORD our God is holy!

100.1: A Psalm of thanksgiving. Shout for joy to the LORD, all the earth!
100.2: Serve the LORD with joy! Come into his presence with songs of praise!
100.3: Know that the LORD is God! He made us, and we belong to him; we are his people, the flock he tends.
100.4: Enter his gates with thanksgiving and his courts with praise! Thank him and bless his name!
100.5: For the LORD is good; his steadfast love endures forever, and his faithfulness reaches to every generation.

101.1: A Psalm of David. I will sing of steadfast love and justice; to you, LORD, I will make music.
101.2: I will conduct myself with wisdom and integrity. O when will you come to me? I will walk with a blameless heart within my own house.
101.3: I will not place anything vile before my eyes. I despise the conduct of those who fall away; it will have no hold on me.
101.4: I will have nothing to do with a crooked heart; I will have no part in evil.
101.5: Whoever slanders his neighbor in private, him I will silence. The proud-eyed and the arrogant-hearted I will not tolerate.
101.6: My eyes will be on the faithful people of the land, that they may live with me; only those who walk blamelessly will serve in my presence.
101.7: No one who practices deceit will live in my house; no one who tells lies will remain in my presence.
101.8: Each morning I will silence all the wicked in the land, cutting off every evildoer from the city of the LORD.

102.1: A Prayer of an afflicted man when he is faint and pours out his anguish before the LORD. Hear my prayer, O LORD; let my cry reach you!
102.2: Do not hide your face from me when I am in trouble! Lean down and listen; answer me quickly when I call!
102.3: For my days are vanishing like smoke, and my bones burn like a blazing hearth.
102.4: My heart is crushed like grass and has dried up; I have even forgotten to eat.
102.5: From my constant groaning I am reduced to skin and bones.
102.6: I am like a pelican of the desert, like an owl haunting the ruins.
102.7: I cannot sleep; I am like a solitary bird stranded on a rooftop.
102.8: My enemies insult me all day long; those who deride me invoke my name as a curse.
102.9: For I eat ashes instead of bread and mix my tears into what I drink,
102.10: because of your fury and wrath; for you have lifted me up only to hurl me down.
102.11: My days are like a fading shadow at evening; I am withering like grass.
102.12: But you, LORD, are enthroned forever; your name endures throughout every generation.
102.13: You will rise up and have mercy on Zion; the time to show her favor has come — the appointed hour is here.
102.14: For your servants hold her very stones dear and take pity even on her dust.
102.15: The nations will stand in awe of the name of the LORD, and all the kings of the earth will reverence your glory.
102.16: For the LORD will rebuild Zion and reveal himself in his glory.
102.17: He listens to the prayer of the helpless and does not ignore their cry.
102.18: Let this be written down for a future generation, so that a people not yet born will praise the LORD.
102.19: That the LORD looked down from his holy height; from heaven he gazed upon the earth,
102.20: to hear the groaning of the prisoner and release those condemned to death,
102.21: so that the name of the LORD would be proclaimed in Zion and his praise sung in Jerusalem,
102.22: when peoples and kingdoms assemble together to worship the LORD.
102.23: He has sapped my strength in midcourse; he has shortened my days.
102.24: I said, 'O my God, do not take me away in the middle of my life — you whose years go on through all generations!'
102.25: Long ago you laid the earth's foundation, and the heavens are the handiwork you shaped.
102.26: They will perish, but you will endure; they will all wear out like old clothes. You will roll them up like a garment and they will be changed,
102.27: but you are ever the same, and your years will never end.
102.28: The children of your servants will live in safety; their descendants will be established in your presence.

105.1: Give thanks to the LORD; call upon his name; make his deeds known among the nations!
105.2: Sing to him, sing praise to him; reflect on all his wonderful deeds!
105.3: Boast in his holy name; let the hearts of those who seek the LORD be glad!
105.4: Seek the LORD and his power; seek his presence at all times!
105.5: Remember the wonderful things he has done — his signs and wonders, the judgments he has spoken —
105.6: you descendants of Abraham his servant, you children of Jacob, his chosen people!
105.7: He is the LORD our God; his justice extends throughout all the earth.
105.8: He keeps his covenant in memory forever, the promise he declared for a thousand generations —
105.9: the covenant he made with Abraham and the oath he swore to Isaac,
105.10: which he established as a binding decree for Jacob, an everlasting covenant for Israel,
105.11: He said, 'I will give you the land of Canaan as your inherited portion.'
105.12: When they were just a handful of people — almost no one — living as foreigners there,
105.13: moving from one nation to another, from one kingdom to the next,
105.14: he allowed no one to oppress them; he rebuked kings for their sake:
105.15: 'Do not lay a hand on my anointed ones; do not harm my prophets!'
105.16: Then he summoned a famine over the land and cut off every supply of food.
105.17: He sent a man ahead of them — Joseph, who had been sold as a slave.
105.18: They bound his feet with shackles and an iron chain was laid upon him.
105.19: This lasted until what he had foretold came true — all the while the word of the LORD was testing him.
105.20: The king sent word and freed him; the ruler of the peoples set him at liberty.
105.21: He made him master of his household and ruler over everything he owned,
105.22: with authority to direct his princes as he saw fit and to instruct his elders in wisdom.
105.23: So Israel came down to Egypt, and Jacob took up residence in the land of Ham.
105.24: There God made his people extremely fruitful and stronger than those who opposed them.
105.25: He turned the Egyptians' hearts to hate his people and to scheme treacherously against his servants.
105.26: He sent Moses, his servant, and Aaron, the man he had chosen.
105.27: They carried out his signs among the Egyptians and performed wonders in the land of Ham.
105.28: He sent darkness and plunged the land into blackness; Moses and Aaron did not defy his command.
105.29: He turned their waters to blood and killed all their fish.
105.30: Frogs overran their land — even the private rooms of their kings.
105.31: He spoke and flies and gnats swarmed across all their territory.
105.32: He gave them hail instead of rain, and flashing fire across their land.
105.33: He struck down their vines and fig trees and splintered all the trees in their land.
105.34: He spoke and locusts arrived — grasshoppers beyond all counting —
105.35: eating up every plant in their land and consuming all the fruit of their soil.
105.36: He struck down every firstborn in their land, the firstfruits of all their vigor.
105.37: He brought his people out loaded with silver and gold; not one person in all their tribes was weak.
105.38: Egypt was relieved when they left, for the dread of Israel had settled over them.
105.39: He spread out a cloud as a shelter and gave fire to light the way through the night.
105.40: They asked for food and he brought them quail, satisfying them with bread from heaven.
105.41: He split open the rock and water poured out; it flowed like a river through the wilderness.
105.42: For he had called to mind his holy promise to Abraham his servant.
105.43: And so he led his people out with rejoicing, his chosen ones with shouts of joy.
105.44: He gave them the lands of other nations, and they took possession of what others had worked for,
105.45: so that they would keep his decrees and obey his teachings. Praise the LORD!

107.1: Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good; his steadfast love endures forever!
107.2: Let those the LORD has redeemed declare it — those he bought back from the grip of the enemy,
107.3: and gathered in from every land — from east and west, from north and south.
107.4: Some wandered in the desert waste, finding no road to a city where they could live.
107.5: Hungry and thirsty, their soul grew faint within them.
107.6: Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble, and he rescued them from their distresses.
107.7: He guided them on a straight path to a city where they could settle.
107.8: Let them give thanks to the LORD for his steadfast love and for his wonders done for the children of man!
107.9: For he gives what the hungry soul longs for and fills the starving soul with good things.
107.10: Some sat in darkness and in the shadow of death, bound in misery and iron chains —
107.11: for they had defied the commands of God and rejected the plan of the Most High.
107.12: So he wore them down with grinding toil; they stumbled and fell, with no one to help.
107.13: Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble, and he saved them from their distresses.
107.14: He led them out of the darkness and the shadow of death and broke their chains apart.
107.15: Let them give thanks to the LORD for his steadfast love and for his wonders done for the children of man!
107.16: For he has smashed the gates of bronze and snapped the iron bars.
107.17: Some were fools who made themselves sick by the path they chose, suffering because of their own sins.
107.18: Their soul could not bear any food, and they were approaching the very gates of death.
107.19: Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble, and he saved them from their distresses.
107.20: He sent his word and healed them and pulled them back from the pit of destruction.
107.21: Let them give thanks to the LORD for his steadfast love and for his wonders done for the children of man!
107.22: Let them bring offerings of thanksgiving and tell of his deeds with shouts of joy.
107.23: Some went down to the sea in ships, plying their trade on the wide and open sea —
107.24: they witnessed what the LORD does — his wonders in the depths.
107.25: He spoke and a storm-wind rose, whipping the sea into waves.
107.26: They heaved up to the sky and plunged back down to the depths; they were terrified out of their senses.
107.27: They lurched and stumbled like drunken men, and every bit of their seamanship was consumed.
107.28: Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble, and he brought them out of their distresses.
107.29: He calmed the storm to a stillness, and the waves went quiet.
107.30: They were overjoyed as the sea grew calm, and he guided them into the harbor they had longed for.
107.31: Let them give thanks to the LORD for his steadfast love and for his wonders done for the children of man!
107.32: Let them lift up his name in the great assembly of the people and honor him in the council of the elders.
107.33: He can turn rivers into desert and springs of water into parched ground,
107.34: fertile land into a barren salt flat — for the wickedness of those who live there.
107.35: But he can also turn the desert into pools of water and dry ground into flowing springs.
107.36: He brings the hungry to live there, and they build a city to call home.
107.37: They plant fields and vineyards that bring in an abundant crop.
107.38: He blesses them and they grow in number, and he keeps their herds from shrinking.
107.39: But when they are cut down and humbled through oppression, calamity, and grief,
107.40: he pours contempt on rulers and sends them wandering in pathless deserts —
107.41: but he raises the poor person up from their misery and makes their family grow like a flock.
107.42: The righteous see all this and rejoice, while every voice of wickedness is silenced.
107.43: Let those who are wise take note of all this and reflect carefully on the steadfast love of the LORD.

108.1: A Song. A Psalm of David. My heart is ready, O God; I will sing and praise you with all my soul.
108.2: Awake, harp and lyre! I will be the one to rouse the dawn.
108.3: I will praise you, O LORD, among all peoples and sing your praises among the nations.
108.4: For your steadfast love towers above the heavens, and your faithfulness reaches to the skies.
108.5: Be lifted high above the heavens, O God! Let your glory fill the whole earth!
108.6: Save your beloved ones — rescue us by your mighty hand and answer our prayer.
108.7: God has spoken from his holy place: 'I will celebrate; I will apportion Shechem and measure out the valley of Succoth.'
108.8: Gilead belongs to me; Manasseh belongs to me; Ephraim is the helmet on my head; Judah is my royal scepter.
108.9: Moab is my washbasin; over Edom I toss my sandal; over Philistia I raise a victory shout.
108.10: Who will take me into the strong, walled city? Who will guide me all the way to Edom?
108.11: God, have you not turned your back on us? Why do you no longer march out with our armies?
108.12: Come to our aid against the enemy — human help amounts to nothing.
108.13: With God we will fight with courage, for he is the one who will crush our enemies underfoot.

106.1: Praise the LORD! Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good; his steadfast love endures forever!
106.2: Who is able to speak all the LORD's mighty deeds, or make known all his praise?
106.3: Blessed are those who uphold justice and do what is right at every moment!
106.4: Remember me, O LORD, when you show favor to your people; come to me with your saving help,
106.5: so that I may see the well-being of your chosen ones, share the joy of your nation, and exult with your own inheritance.
106.6: We have sinned, just as our fathers did; we have committed iniquity and acted wickedly.
106.7: When our fathers were in Egypt they took no account of your wonders; they did not remember your great steadfast love, and they rebelled at the sea — at the Red Sea.
106.8: But he saved them for the sake of his own name, to demonstrate his mighty power.
106.9: He rebuked the Red Sea, and it dried up; he led them through the depths as if through a dry wasteland.
106.10: He rescued them from those who hated them and redeemed them from the power of their enemy.
106.11: The waters swept over their adversaries; not a single one remained.
106.12: Then they trusted his word and broke into songs of praise.
106.13: But they quickly forgot what he had done; they did not wait for his guidance.
106.14: They gave way to intense craving in the wilderness and put God to the test in the desert.
106.15: He gave them exactly what they wanted, but sent a wasting sickness into their bodies.
106.16: When jealousy of Moses broke out in the camp — and of Aaron, the LORD's holy one —
106.17: the earth split open and swallowed Dathan, and closed over the company of Abiram.
106.18: Fire blazed through their party; flame consumed the wicked.
106.19: At Horeb they made a calf and bowed down to a metal idol.
106.20: They traded the glory of God for the image of a grass-eating ox.
106.21: They forgot God, their Savior, who had done those great deeds in Egypt —
106.22: marvels in the land of Ham and fearsome acts at the Red Sea.
106.23: So he declared he would destroy them — but Moses, his chosen one, stood in the gap before him and turned his anger away from destroying them.
106.24: Then they scorned the desirable land and refused to trust what he had promised.
106.25: They grumbled in their tents and refused to listen to the LORD's voice.
106.26: So he swore with uplifted hand that he would strike them down in the wilderness,
106.27: and would scatter their descendants among the nations, spreading them across distant lands.
106.28: Then they attached themselves to Baal of Peor and partook of sacrifices offered to lifeless gods.
106.29: They stirred up the LORD's anger by what they were doing, and a plague broke out in their midst.
106.30: Then Phinehas stepped forward and executed judgment, and the plague was stopped.
106.31: And it was reckoned to him as righteousness for all generations, forever.
106.32: They angered the LORD at the waters of Meribah, and because of them it went badly for Moses —
106.33: for they had embittered Moses's spirit, and he spoke rashly with his mouth.
106.34: They did not drive out the peoples, as the LORD had commanded them.
106.35: Instead they mixed in with the nations and picked up their practices.
106.36: They served the idols of those peoples, and they became a trap for them.
106.37: They sacrificed their sons and their daughters to demonic powers —
106.38: they poured out the innocent blood of their sons and daughters, offering them to the idols of Canaan; the land was defiled by their bloodshed.
106.39: They defiled themselves by all they did and played the harlot in their ways.
106.40: Then the LORD's anger blazed against his own people; he came to loathe his own inheritance.
106.41: He handed them over to the nations, and people who hated them came to rule over them.
106.42: Their enemies crushed them, and they were ground down under their power.
106.43: He rescued them again and again, but they kept resisting his purposes and were laid low by their own sin.
106.44: Still, he took notice of their affliction and listened when they cried out.
106.45: For their sake he called his covenant to mind and relented, moved by the greatness of his steadfast love.
106.46: He made all those who had taken them captive treat them with compassion.
106.47: Save us, O LORD our God, and bring us back from among the nations, that we may give thanks to your holy name and take pride in your praise.
106.48: Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel, from age to age! And let all the people say, 'Amen!' Praise the LORD!

115.1: Not to us, O LORD — not to us — but to your name give glory, because of your steadfast love and your faithfulness.
115.2: Why should the nations say, 'Where is their God?'
115.3: But our God is enthroned in the heavens; he does whatever he pleases.
115.4: Their idols are silver and gold — nothing more than what human hands have made.
115.5: They have mouths but cannot speak; they have eyes but cannot see.
115.6: They have ears but cannot hear; they have noses but cannot smell.
115.7: They have hands but cannot feel anything; they have feet but cannot walk; they produce no sound from their throat.
115.8: Those who make them will end up like them — and so will everyone who puts their trust in them.
115.9: Israel, put your trust in the LORD! He is their help and their shield.
115.10: House of Aaron, put your trust in the LORD! He is their help and their shield.
115.11: All who fear the LORD, put your trust in the LORD! He is their help and their shield.
115.12: The LORD has remembered us and he will bless us; he will bless the house of Israel; he will bless the house of Aaron;
115.13: He will bless all who fear the LORD, the small as well as the great.
115.14: May the LORD grant you increase — you and your children!
115.15: May you be blessed by the LORD — he who made both heaven and earth!
115.16: The highest heavens belong to the LORD, but the earth he has given to the children of mankind.
115.17: The dead cannot praise the LORD, nor can any who descend into the silence of the grave.
115.18: But we will praise the LORD from this moment on, now and forever. Praise the LORD!

116.1: I love the LORD because he has heard my voice and my pleas for mercy.
116.2: Because he bent his ear down to listen to me, I will call on him for as long as I live.
116.3: Death's ropes wrapped around me; the grip of Sheol closed in; I was trapped in distress and anguish.
116.4: Then I cried out the name of the LORD: 'Please, LORD — rescue my life!'
116.5: The LORD is gracious and righteous; our God is full of compassion.
116.6: The LORD protects those who are simple-hearted; when I was at my lowest, he came to my rescue.
116.7: Come to your rest again, my soul, for the LORD has been good to you.
116.8: You have rescued my soul from death, my eyes from tears, my feet from stumbling.
116.9: I will walk in the presence of the LORD in the land of the living.
116.10: I trusted, even while I cried out: 'I am in great distress.'
116.11: In my panic I declared, 'Every human being is a liar.'
116.12: What can I give back to the LORD for all the good he has done for me?
116.13: I will take up the cup of salvation and call on the name of the LORD.
116.14: I will fulfill my vows to the LORD in the presence of all his people.
116.15: The LORD takes the death of his faithful ones very seriously.
116.16: LORD, I am your servant — your servant, the son born of your maidservant. You have set me free from my bonds.
116.17: I will bring you a thanksgiving offering and call out the name of the LORD.
116.18: I will fulfill my vows to the LORD in the presence of all his people,
116.19: right in the courtyards of the LORD's house, in your very midst, O Jerusalem. Praise the LORD!

117.1: Praise the LORD, all nations! Praise him, all peoples of the earth!
117.2: For his steadfast love toward us is great, and the faithfulness of the LORD endures forever. Praise the LORD!

118.1: Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good; his steadfast love endures forever.
118.2: Let Israel declare: 'His steadfast love endures forever.'
118.3: Let the house of Aaron declare: 'His steadfast love endures forever.'
118.4: Let all who fear the LORD declare: 'His steadfast love endures forever.'
118.5: From the depths of my distress I called to the LORD; the LORD answered me and gave me room to breathe.
118.6: The LORD is on my side; I will not be afraid. What can any person do to me?
118.7: The LORD stands on my side as my helper; I will see my enemies brought to shame.
118.8: It is better to take shelter in the LORD than to put your confidence in any person.
118.9: It is better to take shelter in the LORD than to put your confidence in rulers.
118.10: All the nations closed in around me, but in the name of the LORD I routed them.
118.11: They surrounded me — closed in from every side — but in the name of the LORD I cut them off.
118.12: They swarmed around me like bees, but blazed out as quickly as a brush fire; in the name of the LORD I cut them off.
118.13: I was shoved to the point of falling, but the LORD was there to help me.
118.14: The LORD is my strength and my song; he has become my salvation.
118.15: Shouts of joy and victory echo in the tents of the righteous: 'The right hand of the LORD does mighty things!'
118.16: The right hand of the LORD is raised high; the right hand of the LORD does mighty things!
118.17: I will not die — I will live, and I will tell of what the LORD has done.
118.18: The LORD has disciplined me hard, but he has not let death have me.
118.19: Open the righteous gates for me so I can go in and give thanks to the LORD.
118.20: This is the LORD's own gate; only the righteous may enter through it.
118.21: I praise you because you answered me and have become my salvation.
118.22: The stone that the builders threw aside has become the cornerstone.
118.23: This is what the LORD has done; it is wonderful in our sight.
118.24: This is the day the LORD has made; let us celebrate and be glad in it.
118.25: LORD, save us, we pray! LORD, grant us success, we pray!
118.26: May God bless the one who comes in the name of the LORD! We bless you from the LORD's house.
118.27: The LORD is God, and his light has shone on us. Bind the sacrifice with cords and bring it all the way to the horns of the altar.
118.28: You are my God, and I will give you thanks; you are my God, and I will praise you.
118.29: Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good; his steadfast love endures forever.

109.1: A Psalm of David. O God — the one I praise — do not be silent!
109.2: The mouths of the wicked and the treacherous are open wide against me; they have spoken against me with false words.
109.3: They encircle me with hateful words and fight against me for no reason.
109.4: In exchange for my love they have become my adversaries, though I give myself to prayer.
109.5: They have paid me back evil for good and hatred in place of my love.
109.6: Set a wicked man to judge him; let an accuser take his place at his right hand.
109.7: When he faces judgment, let him be condemned; let even his prayer be turned against him.
109.8: Let his life be cut short; let someone else take his position.
109.9: Let his children be left without a father and his wife become a widow.
109.10: Let his children wander and beg, searching for bread far from their ruined homes.
109.11: Let creditors take everything he owns; let foreigners carry off all he has worked for.
109.12: Let no one show him loyal kindness or take pity on the children he left behind.
109.13: Let his line come to an end; let his family name be wiped out in the next generation.
109.14: Let the LORD keep his fathers' guilt on record and never wipe out his mother's sin.
109.15: Let all those sins stand always before the LORD, until he wipes out their very memory from the earth.
109.16: For he never thought to show loyal kindness, but hounded the poor, the needy, and the brokenhearted, intent on killing them.
109.17: He loved to curse others — let curses fall on him! He took no pleasure in blessing — let blessing stay far away from him!
109.18: He wrapped himself in curses like a coat; let that curse seep into his body like water and into his bones like oil.
109.19: Let it cling to him like the clothing he wears, like a belt strapped on every single day.
109.20: Let the LORD repay my accusers with all of this — those who speak evil against me.
109.21: But you, O Lord GOD, deal with me for the sake of your own name; your steadfast love is good — deliver me!
109.22: For I am poor and in need, and my heart is deeply wounded within me.
109.23: I am fading like an evening shadow, brushed away like a locust.
109.24: My knees buckle from fasting; my body has wasted away, lean and gaunt.
109.25: I am the object of their scorn; when they catch sight of me, they shake their heads.
109.26: Help me, O LORD my God; save me in keeping with your steadfast love.
109.27: Let them see that your hand is behind this — that you, LORD, are the one who has acted.
109.28: Let them curse — you will bless. When they rise against me, let them be shamed; but let your servant be glad.
109.29: Let my accusers be dressed in disgrace and wrapped in their own shame like a mantle.
109.30: With my voice I will give the LORD great thanks; I will praise him among the gathered crowd.
109.31: For he stands at the right hand of the poor person, to rescue him from all who would condemn him.

110.1: A Psalm of David. The LORD declared to my Lord: 'Sit at my right hand until I make your enemies a stool for your feet.'
110.2: The LORD extends your powerful scepter from Zion: rule over your enemies!
110.3: On the day you march to war, your people volunteer freely, arrayed in holy splendor. Like dew born from the womb of the dawn, your young warriors come to you.
110.4: The LORD has taken an oath he will not revoke: 'You are a priest forever, of the same order as Melchizedek.'
110.5: The Lord stands at your right hand; he will crush kings on the day of his wrath.
110.6: He will bring judgment on the nations, filling the land with the dead; he will crush rulers across the wide earth.
110.7: He drinks from the stream along the road, and because of this he will hold his head high.

111.1: Praise the LORD! I will thank the LORD with my whole heart, in the gathering of the upright, in the full congregation.
111.2: The works of the LORD are great, studied carefully by all who find pleasure in them.
111.3: All he does is glorious and majestic, and his righteousness endures forever.
111.4: He has made his wonders unforgettable; the LORD is gracious and full of compassion.
111.5: He provides food for all who fear him; he is always mindful of his covenant.
111.6: He showed his people what his works can do, giving them the nations' land as their possession.
111.7: Everything he does is faithful and just; all his instructions are reliable.
111.8: They hold firm forever and ever, carried out with complete faithfulness and integrity.
111.9: He sent redemption to his people and established his covenant forever. His name is holy and full of awe.
111.10: The fear of the LORD is where wisdom begins; those who live by it gain deep understanding. His praise endures forever.

112.1: Praise the LORD! How blessed is the one who fears the LORD and finds great joy in his commands!
112.2: His children will be a powerful presence in the land; the descendants of the upright will be blessed.
112.3: Prosperity and riches fill his home, and his righteousness endures forever.
112.4: Even in the dark, light breaks for the upright; he is gracious, compassionate, and righteous.
112.5: It goes well with the one who is generous in lending and manages his business with justice.
112.6: The righteous will never be shaken; they will be remembered forever.
112.7: He does not fear bad news; his heart is steady, trusting in the LORD.
112.8: His heart is steady; he will not fear. In the end he will see his enemies get what they deserve.
112.9: He gives freely to the poor; his righteousness endures forever; his honor is lifted high.
112.10: The wicked see all this and seethe; they grind their teeth and waste away. What the wicked crave will come to nothing.

113.1: Praise the LORD! Let those who serve the LORD give praise — praise the name of the LORD!
113.2: Let the name of the LORD be praised from now and for all time to come!
113.3: From the rising of the sun to where it sets, the name of the LORD deserves all praise!
113.4: The LORD reigns above all nations; his glory soars above the very heavens.
113.5: Who can compare with the LORD our God, who has his throne so high above —
113.6: yet bends down to look at both heaven and earth?
113.7: He lifts the poor out of the dust and raises the needy from the garbage heap,
113.8: to seat them alongside princes — the princes of his own people.
113.9: He gives the childless woman a home, turning her into a glad mother of children. Praise the LORD!

114.1: When Israel came out of Egypt, when the family of Jacob left a people of foreign speech,
114.2: Judah became God's holy place, Israel his kingdom.
114.3: The sea saw and fled; the Jordan reversed its course.
114.4: The mountains leaped like rams and the hills like young lambs.
114.5: What happened to you, sea, that you ran? And you, Jordan — that you reversed your course?
114.6: You mountains — why did you leap like rams? You hills, like young lambs?
114.7: Tremble before the Lord, O earth — tremble before the God of Jacob,
114.8: who turns solid rock into a pool of water and hard flint into a flowing spring.

120.1: A Song of Ascents. In my distress I called out to the LORD, and he answered me.
120.2: LORD, rescue me from lying lips and from a deceitful tongue.
120.3: What will come to you, and what more will be done to you, O deceitful tongue?
120.4: A warrior's sharp arrows, fired with glowing coals of broom wood!
120.5: Woe to me, that I must live as a foreigner in Meshech and make my dwelling among the tents of Kedar!
120.6: My soul has lived too long among those who hate peace.
120.7: I am for peace — but the moment I open my mouth, they want war.

121.1: A Song of Ascents. I lift my eyes toward the hills — but where does my help come from?
121.2: My help comes from the LORD, the maker of heaven and earth.
121.3: He will not let your foot slip; your keeper will not slumber.
121.4: See — he who guards Israel will neither slumber nor sleep.
121.5: The LORD is your keeper; the LORD is your shade at your right hand.
121.6: The sun will not harm you by day, nor the moon by night.
121.7: The LORD will protect you from every kind of evil; he will guard your very life.
121.8: The LORD will watch over your coming and going, now and forever.

122.1: A Song of Ascents. Of David. I was filled with joy when they said to me, 'Let us go to the house of the LORD!'
122.2: Our feet are standing inside your gates, O Jerusalem!
122.3: Jerusalem — built as a city that is closely joined together,
122.4: to which the tribes make their pilgrimage — the tribes of the LORD — as it was commanded of Israel, to give thanks to the name of the LORD.
122.5: There the thrones of judgment stand — the thrones belonging to the house of David.
122.6: Pray for the peace of Jerusalem! May all who love this city flourish.
122.7: May there be peace within your walls and safety within your strongholds.
122.8: For the sake of my brothers and friends I say, 'May peace be with you!'
122.9: For the sake of the house of the LORD our God I will work for your wellbeing.

123.1: A Song of Ascents. To you I lift my eyes, O you who sit enthroned in the heavens!
123.2: As the eyes of a servant watch the hand of his master, as a maidservant's eyes follow the hand of her mistress, so our eyes are fixed on the LORD our God until he has mercy on us.
123.3: Have mercy on us, O LORD, have mercy on us — for we have endured far more than our fill of contempt.
123.4: We have had more than our fill of the mockery of the comfortable and the contempt of the arrogant.

124.1: A Song of Ascents. Of David. If the LORD had not been on our side — let Israel say it now —
124.2: if the LORD had not been on our side when our enemies rose up against us,
124.3: they would have swallowed us whole when their fury blazed against us.
124.4: The rushing waters would have swept us away; the current would have poured over us.
124.5: The raging waves would have overwhelmed us completely.
124.6: Praise be to the LORD — he did not hand us over as prey to their teeth!
124.7: We have gotten away like a bird that slips out of the hunter's trap — the snare is broken, and we are free!
124.8: Our help comes from the name of the LORD, the maker of heaven and earth.

125.1: A Song of Ascents. Those who trust in the LORD are as firm as Mount Zion — unmovable, enduring forever.
125.2: Just as the mountains encircle Jerusalem, so the LORD encircles his people, now and forever.
125.3: The scepter of the wicked will not be allowed to remain over the territory given to the righteous, lest the righteous be tempted to do evil themselves.
125.4: Bless those who are good, O LORD, and all who are upright in heart.
125.5: But as for those who swerve onto crooked paths, the LORD will banish them along with those who do evil. Peace be upon Israel!

119.1: ALEPH. How blessed are those who live without fault, walking by the LORD's instruction!
119.2: Blessed are those who guard his decrees and pursue him with all their heart,
119.3: who do nothing wrong but walk in his ways.
119.4: You have given your precepts to be carefully obeyed.
119.5: How I wish my ways were truly set on keeping your statutes!
119.6: Then I would not be shamed whenever I consider all your commandments.
119.7: I will thank you with a sincere heart as I learn your righteous decrees.
119.8: I will keep your statutes — please, LORD, do not abandon me.
119.9: BETH. How can a young person keep their life pure? By living in line with your word.
119.10: With all my heart I have pursued you — do not let me drift from your commandments.
119.11: I have treasured your word in my heart so I will not sin against you.
119.12: Blessed are you, O LORD — teach me your statutes!
119.13: With my lips I have declared all the decrees that have come from your mouth.
119.14: I rejoice in the path of your decrees as much as in every kind of wealth.
119.15: I will reflect on your precepts and keep my eyes on your paths.
119.16: I will delight in your statutes — I will not forget your word.
119.17: GIMEL. Be generous with your servant so that I may live and obey your word.
119.18: Open my eyes to see the wonderful truths that lie in your instruction.
119.19: I am a foreigner on this earth — do not hide your commandments from me.
119.20: My soul is worn out with longing for your ordinances at every moment.
119.21: You rebuke the arrogant — those who are cursed — who drift from your commandments.
119.22: Take away the scorn and contempt that fall on me, for I have kept your decrees.
119.23: Even when rulers sit together plotting against me, your servant reflects on your statutes.
119.24: Your decrees are my delight — they are my counselors.
119.25: DALETH. My soul is brought low to the dust — give me life according to your word.
119.26: I laid out my ways before you and you listened — now teach me your statutes.
119.27: Help me understand the path of your precepts, and I will ponder your wonderful deeds.
119.28: My soul dissolves in grief — lift me up according to your word.
119.29: Keep me from every path of falsehood and mercifully give me your instruction.
119.30: I have chosen the path of truth and set your decrees before me.
119.31: I have clung to your decrees, O LORD — do not let me be put to shame.
119.32: I will run the path of your commandments when you set my heart free.
119.33: HE. Teach me, LORD, the path of your statutes, and I will follow it to the very end.
119.34: Grant me understanding to keep your law and obey it with all my heart.
119.35: Guide me along the path of your commandments — that is where my delight is.
119.36: Turn my heart toward your decrees and away from selfish gain.
119.37: Turn my eyes away from worthless things and give me life by your ways.
119.38: Fulfill your promise to your servant — the promise given to those who revere you.
119.39: Take away the shame I dread, for your decrees are good.
119.40: See how I long for your precepts — give me life through your righteousness.
119.41: VAU. Let your steadfast love come to me, LORD — your salvation just as you have promised,
119.42: so I will have a reply for those who mock me, because I trust in your word.
119.43: Do not let me lose the power to speak truth, for my hope is in your decrees.
119.44: I will keep your law at all times, forever and ever.
119.45: I will move freely, for I have sought out your precepts.
119.46: I will speak of your decrees before kings and will not be ashamed.
119.47: I will find my delight in your commandments, which I love.
119.48: I will reach out toward your commandments, which I love, and reflect on your statutes.
119.49: ZAIN. Remember the word you gave to your servant — the word that gave me hope.
119.50: This is what comforts me when I suffer: your word has kept me alive.
119.51: The proud have made fun of me at every turn, yet I have not swerved from your law.
119.52: I call to mind your ancient decrees, O LORD, and find comfort in them.
119.53: Rage and anguish grip me when I see the wicked forsaking your law.
119.54: Your statutes have been the songs I sing in this place where I am passing through.
119.55: I call your name to mind in the night, O LORD, and keep your law.
119.56: This is what has been mine because I kept your precepts.
119.57: CHETH. The LORD is my portion — this I have declared. I commit to keeping your words.
119.58: I have asked for your favor with all my heart — be gracious to me as you have promised.
119.59: When I stopped to think about my life, I turned my steps back to your decrees.
119.60: I was quick and did not hesitate to obey your commandments.
119.61: The wicked have tried to trap me, but I have not forgotten your law.
119.62: I rise at midnight to thank you for your righteous decrees.
119.63: I am a friend to all who reverence you and to all who keep your precepts.
119.64: The earth is full of your steadfast love, O LORD — teach me your statutes.
119.65: TETH. You have been good to your servant, LORD — just as you promised.
119.66: Teach me discernment and understanding, for I trust in your commandments.
119.67: Before I suffered I used to wander; but now I keep your word.
119.68: You are good, and you do what is good — teach me your statutes.
119.69: The arrogant have spread lies about me, but I will keep your precepts with all my heart.
119.70: Their hearts are hardened and insensible, but I delight in your law.
119.71: My suffering was good for me, because it taught me your statutes.
119.72: The instruction that comes from your mouth is worth more to me than thousands of gold and silver coins.
119.73: JOD. Your hands made me and formed me — give me understanding to learn your commandments.
119.74: Those who revere you will see me and be glad, because I have put my hope in your word.
119.75: I know, O LORD, that your decrees are right and that you disciplined me out of faithfulness.
119.76: Let your faithful love be a comfort to me, just as you promised your servant.
119.77: Let your compassion come to me so I may live — your law is my delight.
119.78: May the proud be shamed — without reason they have twisted things against me; I will reflect on your precepts.
119.79: Let those who fear you gather around me — those who know your decrees.
119.80: May my heart be wholly devoted to your statutes, so I will not be put to shame.
119.81: CAPH. My soul is worn out waiting for your salvation, but I hope in your word.
119.82: My eyes are strained looking for your word, asking, 'When will you comfort me?'
119.83: I am like a wineskin dried out in the smoke, yet I do not forget your statutes.
119.84: How many days of my life are left? When will you bring judgment on those who persecute me?
119.85: The proud have set traps for me — people who do not follow your law.
119.86: All your commands are trustworthy; they are hounding me without cause — help me!
119.87: They nearly destroyed me from the earth, but I have not abandoned your precepts.
119.88: Give me life according to your steadfast love, so that I will obey the decrees that come from your mouth.
119.89: LAMED. Your word, O LORD, stands firm forever — fixed in the heavens.
119.90: Your faithfulness lasts through every generation — you set the earth in place and it holds firm.
119.91: Everything continues to this day according to your decrees, for all things serve you.
119.92: If your law had not been my joy, I would have died in my suffering.
119.93: I will never forget your precepts, for through them you have kept me alive.
119.94: I belong to you — rescue me, for I have devoted myself to your precepts.
119.95: The wicked lurk to destroy me, but my attention stays fixed on your decrees.
119.96: I have seen that every human achievement has its limits, but your commandment has no bounds.
119.97: MEM. How I love your law! I think about it all day long.
119.98: Your commandments make me wiser than my enemies, for they are always with me.
119.99: I understand more than all my teachers do, for your decrees fill my thoughts.
119.100: I discern more than those who are older, because I observe your precepts.
119.101: I have kept my feet from every wrong path so that I could obey your word.
119.102: I have not turned away from your decrees, for you yourself have been my teacher.
119.103: How sweet your words are to me — sweeter than honey in my mouth!
119.104: Your precepts give me discernment; that is why I hate every path of falsehood.
119.105: NUN. Your word is a lamp that lights my feet and a light for my path.
119.106: I have taken an oath and will hold to it: I will follow your righteous decrees.
119.107: I am deeply afflicted — give me life again, LORD, as you have promised!
119.108: O LORD, please accept the willing praise of my lips and teach me your decrees.
119.109: I hold my life in my hands at every moment, yet I do not forget your law.
119.110: The wicked laid a trap for me, but I have not wandered from your precepts.
119.111: Your decrees are mine as an inheritance forever — they are the joy of my heart.
119.112: I have set my heart on keeping your statutes always — right to the end.
119.113: SAMECH. I hate those who are half-hearted, but your law I love.
119.114: You are my shelter and my shield — I hope in your word.
119.115: Get away from me, you who do evil — I intend to keep the commands of my God.
119.116: Sustain me as you have promised so I may live — do not let my hope put me to shame.
119.117: Support me and I will be safe, and I will keep my gaze fixed on your statutes always.
119.118: You reject all who drift away from your statutes — their deceptions are empty lies.
119.119: You sweep away all the wicked of the earth like impure dross — therefore I love your decrees.
119.120: I tremble in awe of you — your judgments fill me with holy dread.
119.121: AIN. I have acted with justice and integrity — do not hand me over to those who oppress me.
119.122: Guarantee good things for your servant — do not let the proud trample me.
119.123: My eyes are worn out watching for your salvation and for the fulfillment of your righteous promise.
119.124: Treat your servant according to your steadfast love and teach me your statutes.
119.125: I am your servant — give me discernment to understand your decrees.
119.126: It is time for you to act, LORD — they have rendered your law void.
119.127: Therefore I love your commandments more than gold — more than the finest gold.
119.128: For that reason I consider all your precepts to be right in every area, and I hate every path of falsehood.
119.129: PE. Your decrees are wonderful — that is why I treasure them.
119.130: When your words are opened up they shed light; they give understanding to the untrained mind.
119.131: I opened my mouth and panted in longing — I craved your commandments.
119.132: Look on me and be merciful, as is your custom toward those who love your name.
119.133: Keep my steps firm in your word and do not let any sin take control of me.
119.134: Set me free from human cruelty, and I will keep your precepts.
119.135: Let your face shine on your servant and teach me your statutes.
119.136: My eyes pour down rivers of tears because people do not keep your law.
119.137: TZADDI. You are righteous, O LORD, and your decrees are just.
119.138: The decrees you have commanded are righteous and completely trustworthy.
119.139: A burning zeal has consumed me, because my enemies disregard your words.
119.140: Your word is completely pure — that is why your servant loves it.
119.141: I am lowly and scorned, but I have not forgotten your precepts.
119.142: Your righteousness stands forever, and your law is truth.
119.143: Distress and trouble have overtaken me, yet your commandments are my delight.
119.144: Your decrees are right for all time — give me understanding so I may live.
119.145: KOPH. I called out with all my heart — answer me, LORD! I will keep your statutes.
119.146: I called out to you — save me, so I can keep your decrees.
119.147: I am awake before daybreak to cry out — I put my hope in your word.
119.148: I am awake through the night watches to reflect on your word.
119.149: Listen to me, LORD, in your steadfast love — give me life according to your justice.
119.150: Those who chase after wickedness are closing in on me — they are far from your law.
119.151: But you are near, O LORD — all your commandments are truth.
119.152: I have known from ancient times about your decrees — you established them to last forever.
119.153: RESH. Look at my suffering and rescue me, for I have not forgotten your law.
119.154: Take up my case and rescue me — give me life as you have promised.
119.155: Rescue is far from the wicked — they have no interest in your statutes.
119.156: Your compassion is vast, O LORD — give me life according to your decrees.
119.157: I have many persecutors and enemies, yet I have not turned from your decrees.
119.158: I saw those who were disloyal and it grieved me, because they do not keep your word.
119.159: See how much I love your precepts! Give me life, LORD, in your steadfast love.
119.160: The whole of your word is truth, and every one of your righteous decrees stands forever.
119.161: SHIN. Rulers have hounded me without any reason, but my heart trembles in reverence before your word.
119.162: I rejoice over your word like someone finding a great treasure.
119.163: I deeply hate falsehood and deception, but I love your law.
119.164: Seven times each day I praise you because your decrees are just.
119.165: Those who love your law enjoy great peace — nothing will trip them up.
119.166: I put my hope in your salvation, O LORD, and I have carried out your commandments.
119.167: I have kept your decrees with my whole being and I love them deeply.
119.168: I have obeyed your precepts and your decrees — all my ways are open before you.
119.169: TAV. Let my cry reach you, O LORD — grant me understanding as you have promised.
119.170: Let my prayer reach you — rescue me as you have promised.
119.171: My lips will overflow with praise, because you have taught me your statutes.
119.172: My tongue will celebrate your word, for all your commandments are right.
119.173: Be ready to help me, for I have chosen your precepts.
119.174: I have longed for your salvation, O LORD — your law is my delight.
119.175: Let me live so that I may praise you — let your decrees sustain me.
119.176: I have wandered away like a lost sheep — come and find me, for I have not forgotten your commandments.

126.1: A Song of Ascents. When the LORD restored the fortunes of Zion, we were like people in a dream.
126.2: Then our mouths were full of laughter and our tongues rang out with shouts of joy; the nations were saying, 'The LORD has done great things for them!'
126.3: The LORD has done great things for us — and we are overjoyed.
126.4: Restore our fortunes, O LORD, like the streams that suddenly fill the desert wadis.
126.5: Those who plant in tears will harvest with shouts of joy.
126.6: The one who sets out weeping, carrying the seed bag, will come home with joyful shouts, loaded down with the harvest.

127.1: A Song of Ascents. Of Solomon. Unless the LORD builds the house, the builders work to no purpose; unless the LORD guards the city, the sentinels watch for nothing.
127.2: It is pointless to rise before dawn and work until dark, eating meals of anxious toil — for the LORD gives sleep as a gift to those he loves.
127.3: Yes, children are a gift from the LORD; they are a reward he gives.
127.4: Children born in a man's prime are like arrows in the hand of a soldier.
127.5: How blessed is the man who has his quiver full of them! He will not be put to shame when he faces his enemies at the city gate.

128.1: A Song of Ascents. How blessed is every person who fears the LORD and walks in his ways.
128.2: You will enjoy the fruit of your labor; you will be blessed, and things will go well for you.
128.3: Your wife will be like a fruitful vine flourishing within your home; your children like young olive trees gathered around your table.
128.4: This is the blessing that comes to the man who fears the LORD.
128.5: May the LORD bless you from Zion; may you see Jerusalem flourish all the days of your life.
128.6: May you live to see your grandchildren. Peace be upon Israel.

129.1: A Song of Ascents. 'Again and again they have attacked me since my earliest days' — let Israel say this now.
129.2: Again and again they have attacked me since my earliest days, and yet they have never overcome me.
129.3: They plowed across my back like farmers cutting their furrows; they made them long and deep.
129.4: But the LORD is righteous — he has cut through the ropes the wicked used to bind me.
129.5: May every one who hates Zion be put to shame and driven back.
129.6: May they be like grass growing on a flat rooftop, which withers before it even has a chance to grow.
129.7: No reaper ever fills his hands with it, and no one gathers it into sheaves —
129.8: and no one passing by will say, 'The LORD's blessing be on you! We pronounce blessing over you in the name of the LORD.'

130.1: A Song of Ascents. From the depths of despair I cry out to you, O LORD.
130.2: O Lord, hear my voice; let your ears be open to my pleading.
130.3: If you, O LORD, kept a record of sins, Lord, who could stand before you?
130.4: But forgiveness is found with you — and that is why you are held in awe.
130.5: I wait for the LORD — my whole being waits — and in his word I put my hope.
130.6: My soul waits for the Lord more than sentinels long for the morning light — yes, more than sentinels wait for morning.
130.7: O Israel, put your hope in the LORD, for with the LORD is steadfast love, and with him is abundant redemption.
130.8: He will redeem Israel from all its sins.

131.1: A Song of Ascents. Of David. LORD, my heart is not proud; my eyes are not raised in arrogance; I do not involve myself with things too grand or too marvellous for me.
131.2: No, I have settled and silenced my soul; like a weaned child resting contentedly with its mother — like a weaned child, my soul is at rest within me.
131.3: O Israel, put your hope in the LORD both now and forever.

132.1: A Song of Ascents. O LORD, remember David — remember all his hardships,
132.2: how he made a solemn oath to the LORD and vowed to the Mighty One of Jacob:
132.3: 'I will not enter my house, nor climb into my bed;'
132.4: 'I will allow no sleep to my eyes, no slumber to my eyelids,'
132.5: 'until I find a fitting place for the LORD — a dwelling for the Mighty One of Jacob.'
132.6: We heard of the ark at Ephrathah; we discovered it in the fields of Jaar.
132.7: Let us go to his dwelling place; let us bow down at his footstool.
132.8: Rise up, O LORD, to your resting place — you and the ark that holds your power.
132.9: Clothe your priests with righteousness, and let your devoted people shout for joy.
132.10: For your servant David's sake, do not reject your anointed king.
132.11: The LORD made a sworn, true covenant with David — he will never go back on it: 'I will place one of your own descendants upon your throne.'
132.12: 'If your sons keep my covenant and the decrees I teach them, their sons too shall sit upon your throne forever.'
132.13: The LORD has chosen Zion; he has made it his desired home.
132.14: 'This is my resting place forever; here I will live, for I have longed for it.'
132.15: 'I will richly bless her food supply; I will fill the poor with bread.'
132.16: 'I will dress her priests with salvation, and her devoted people will shout with joy.'
132.17: 'There I will make a shoot of strength grow for David; I have set a lamp ready for my anointed.'
132.18: 'I will dress his enemies in shame, but his crown will gleam upon his head.'

133.1: A Song of Ascents. Of David. How wonderfully good it is, how delightful, when brothers live together as one!
133.2: It is like costly anointing oil poured on the head, running down over the beard — Aaron's beard — flowing all the way down to the edge of his robes!
133.3: It is like the dew of Hermon descending on the mountains of Zion — for there the LORD has commanded the blessing: life that lasts forever.

134.1: A Song of Ascents. Come, bless the LORD, all you servants of the LORD who stand watch in his house through the night.
134.2: Raise your hands toward the sanctuary and bless the LORD.
134.3: May the LORD, maker of heaven and earth, bless you from Zion.

135.1: Praise the LORD! Praise the name of the LORD; praise him, all you servants of the LORD,
135.2: you who stand in the LORD's house and serve in the courts of our God.
135.3: Praise the LORD, for the LORD is good; sing praises to his name — it is a delight.
135.4: For the LORD chose Jacob as his own, Israel as his prized and treasured possession.
135.5: I know that the LORD is great — our Lord stands above every so-called god.
135.6: The LORD does whatever he pleases — in heaven and on earth, in the seas and every depth.
135.7: He draws up clouds from the far ends of the earth, makes lightning flash with the rain, and releases the wind from his storehouses.
135.8: He struck down the firstborn of Egypt — both people and animals alike.
135.9: He sent miraculous signs and wonders into Egypt — striking Pharaoh and all his servants.
135.10: He defeated great nations and put mighty kings to death —
135.11: Sihon king of the Amorites, Og king of Bashan, and all the kingdoms of Canaan —
135.12: and gave their land as an inheritance — an inheritance to his people Israel.
135.13: O LORD, your name endures forever; your memory is kept alive through every generation.
135.14: The LORD will uphold the cause of his people and will show compassion toward his servants.
135.15: The idols of the nations are silver and gold — nothing but the work of human hands.
135.16: They have mouths, but they cannot speak; they have eyes, but they cannot see;
135.17: they have ears but cannot hear; there is not a breath of air in their mouths.
135.18: Those who make them will become like them — and so will all who put their trust in them.
135.19: Praise the LORD, O house of Israel! Praise the LORD, O house of Aaron!
135.20: Praise the LORD, O house of Levi! All who fear the LORD, praise the LORD!
135.21: Praise be to the LORD from Zion — he who makes his home in Jerusalem. Praise the LORD!

136.1: Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.2: Give thanks to the God above all gods — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.3: Give thanks to the Lord who reigns above all lords — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.4: To him who alone performs great wonders — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.5: To him who made the heavens with wisdom — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.6: To him who laid the earth out over the waters — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.7: To him who made the great lights — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.8: The sun to govern the day — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.9: The moon and stars to govern the night — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.10: To him who struck down Egypt by killing their firstborn — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.11: Who led Israel out from among them — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.12: With a powerful hand and an arm stretched wide — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.13: To him who split the Red Sea apart — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.14: Who led Israel safely through its midst — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.15: And swept Pharaoh and his army into the Red Sea — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.16: To him who guided his people through the desert — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.17: To him who defeated great kings — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.18: Who slew renowned kings — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.19: Sihon, king of the Amorites — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.20: And Og, king of Bashan — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.21: Who gave their land as an inheritance — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.22: An inheritance given to Israel his servant — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.23: Who remembered us when we were brought low — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.24: And delivered us from our enemies — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.25: Who provides food for every living thing — for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.26: Give thanks to the God of heaven — for his steadfast love endures forever.

137.1: By the rivers of Babylon we sat down and wept when we remembered Zion.
137.2: We hung up our harps in the willow trees there.
137.3: Our captors demanded that we sing for them; those who had ruined us wanted entertainment: 'Sing us a song of Zion!'
137.4: But how could we sing the LORD's song in a foreign land?
137.5: If I forget you, O Jerusalem, may my right hand lose all skill.
137.6: If I do not hold you in memory, may my tongue stick to the roof of my mouth — if I do not place Jerusalem above my greatest delight.
137.7: Remember, O LORD, what Edom's sons did on the day Jerusalem fell — how they shouted, 'Tear it down! Tear it down to its very foundations!'
137.8: O Babylon, marked for destruction — how happy will be the one who pays you back for what you did to us!
137.9: Blessed is he who takes your little ones and smashes them against the rocks.

138.1: A Psalm of David. I will praise you, O LORD, with all my heart; in the presence of the heavenly assembly I will sing your praises.
138.2: I will bow down toward your holy temple and praise your name for your steadfast love and faithfulness; for you have exalted your promise above even your own name.
138.3: On the day I called out, you answered me; you filled my soul with bold strength.
138.4: All the kings of the earth will praise you, O LORD, when they hear the words you have spoken.
138.5: They will sing of the paths of the LORD, for the glory of the LORD is great.
138.6: Though the LORD is exalted, he pays attention to the humble; but the arrogant he perceives from a distance.
138.7: Even when I walk through terrible trouble, you keep me alive; you reach out your hand against the fury of my enemies, and your right hand rescues me.
138.8: The LORD will fulfill his purpose for me; your steadfast love, O LORD, endures forever — do not abandon what your hands have made.

139.1: For the director of music. A Psalm of David. O LORD, you have searched me and you know me.
139.2: You know when I sit and when I stand; you understand my thoughts before I even form them.
139.3: You trace every path I walk and every place I rest; you are familiar with all my ways.
139.4: Before a word is on my tongue, you know it completely, O LORD.
139.5: You surround me on every side — behind and before — and you have laid your hand upon me.
139.6: This knowledge is beyond me — too wonderful, too high for me to grasp.
139.7: Where can I go to escape your Spirit? Where can I flee from your presence?
139.8: If I rise to heaven, you are there; if I make my bed in the grave, you are there too.
139.9: If I fly away on the wings of the dawn and settle at the farthest edge of the sea,
139.10: even there your hand will guide me, and your right hand will hold me fast.
139.11: If I say, 'The darkness will surely hide me, and the light around me turn to night' —
139.12: even the darkness is not dark to you; the night shines like the day, for darkness and light are the same to you.
139.13: For you formed my inward parts; you wove me together in my mother's womb.
139.14: I praise you because I am fearfully and wonderfully made — your works are marvellous, and my whole being knows it.
139.15: My very frame was not hidden from you when I was made in secret, skillfully crafted in the depths of the earth.
139.16: Your eyes saw my unformed body; all my days were written in your book before any of them had come to be.
139.17: How priceless are your thoughts toward me, O God! How immeasurable their total!
139.18: If I tried to count them, they would be more than the grains of sand; and when I wake, I am still with you.
139.19: O God, if only you would put an end to the wicked! Get away from me, you murderers!
139.20: They speak against you with evil intent; your adversaries invoke your name for nothing.
139.21: LORD, don't I hate those who hate you? Don't I despise those who rise up against you?
139.22: I hate them with an absolute hatred; I number them among my enemies.
139.23: Search me, O God, and know my heart; put me to the test and see what I am thinking.
139.24: See if there is any harmful path in me, and lead me in the way that lasts forever.

140.1: For the director of music. A Psalm of David. Rescue me, O LORD, from evil men; protect me from violent men —
140.2: who scheme wickedness in their hearts and stir up conflict every day.
140.3: Their tongues are as sharp as a serpent's; viper venom is under their lips. Selah.
140.4: Keep me, O LORD, from the clutches of the wicked; preserve me from violent men who intend to make me stumble.
140.5: The arrogant have laid a trap for me and stretched out cords; they have spread a net along my path and set snares. Selah.
140.6: I say to the LORD, 'You are my God' — hear me, O LORD, and listen to my pleas for mercy.
140.7: O Sovereign LORD, the strength of my salvation — you have shielded my head in the day of battle.
140.8: Do not give the wicked what they want, O LORD; do not let their evil plans succeed, or they will grow proud. Selah.
140.9: Let the trouble caused by the words of those who hem me in fall back upon their own heads.
140.10: Let burning coals rain down on them; let them be thrown into the fire — into pits so deep they never rise.
140.11: Let no slander take root in the land; may evil relentlessly pursue the violent man until he falls.
140.12: I know that the LORD will uphold the cause of the oppressed and defend the rights of the poor.
140.13: Surely the righteous will give thanks to your name; the upright will live in your presence.

141.1: A Psalm of David. O LORD, I call on you — come quickly! Hear my voice when I cry out to you.
141.2: May my prayer rise before you like incense, and the lifting up of my hands like the evening offering.
141.3: Post a guard at my mouth, O LORD; station a sentry at the door of my lips.
141.4: Do not let my heart be drawn to anything evil — to take part in the wicked acts of evildoers, or to taste any of their pleasures.
141.5: If a righteous man strikes me, I will take it as a kindness; if he rebukes me, it is like fine oil on my head — I will not refuse it. But my prayer remains against the deeds of the wicked.
141.6: When their leaders are hurled down from the cliffs, the people will find my words welcome.
141.7: Like clods of earth turned up by the plowshare, our bones lie scattered at the brink of the grave.
141.8: But my eyes are fixed on you, O Lord GOD — in you I shelter; do not leave me exposed.
141.9: Keep me from the trap they have set for me, and from the snares that evildoers have laid.
141.10: Let the wicked tumble into their own nets while I escape unharmed.

142.1: A Maskil of David, a prayer composed when he was in the cave. With full voice I cry out to the LORD; with full voice I plead with him for mercy.
142.2: I pour out my troubles before him; I lay my distress before him.
142.3: When my spirit grows faint within me, you know my way. In the path I must travel, they have hidden a trap for me.
142.4: I look to my right — no one acknowledges me; all escape is cut off from me; not a soul cares for me.
142.5: I cry to you, O LORD; I say, 'You are my shelter and my portion in this living world.'
142.6: Hear my cry, for I have been brought very low; rescue me from those who pursue me, for they are too strong for me.
142.7: Lead my soul out of this prison so I can praise your name. Then the righteous will gather around me, because you will have been generous to me.

143.1: A Psalm of David. O LORD, hear my prayer; listen to my pleas for mercy; in your faithfulness and righteousness, answer me.
143.2: Do not bring your servant into judgment, for no one alive can stand as righteous before you.
143.3: For the enemy has hunted me down and crushed my life to the ground; he has forced me to dwell in darkness like those who died long ago.
143.4: So my spirit grows faint within me; my heart within me is filled with dismay.
143.5: I call to mind the days of old; I meditate on all you have done; I reflect on the work of your hands.
143.6: I reach out my hands toward you; my whole being longs for you like a land parched for rain. Selah.
143.7: Answer me quickly, O LORD — my spirit is failing! Do not hide your face from me, or I will be like those who sink into the grave.
143.8: Let me hear of your steadfast love in the morning, for in you I put my trust; show me the path I should take, for I lift my soul to you.
143.9: Rescue me from my enemies, O LORD — I run to you for shelter.
143.10: Teach me to do your will, for you are my God; may your good Spirit lead me on a level path.
143.11: For the sake of your name, O LORD, keep me alive; in your righteousness lead my soul out of its distress.
143.12: And in your steadfast love silence my enemies and put an end to all who oppress me — for I am your servant.

150.1: Praise the LORD! Praise God in his holy sanctuary; praise him in the vault of his power.
150.2: Praise him for his mighty deeds; praise him for the fullness of his greatness.
150.3: Praise him with the blast of the trumpet; praise him with lyre and harp.
150.4: Praise him with tambourine and dancing; praise him with strings and flute.
150.5: Praise him with resounding cymbals; praise him with crashing cymbals.
150.6: Let everything that has breath praise the LORD! Praise the LORD!

144.1: Blessed is the LORD, my rock, who trains my hands for war and my fingers for battle.
144.2: He is my steadfast love and my fortress, my high tower and my deliverer, my shield and the one in whom I trust, who subdues peoples under me.
144.3: LORD, what is a human being that you take notice of him, or the son of man that you think of him?
144.4: A human being is like a breath; his days pass like a shadow.
144.5: Bow your heavens, LORD, and come down; touch the mountains so that they smoke.
144.6: Send out lightning and scatter them; shoot your arrows and put them to flight.
144.7: Reach down your hand from above; rescue me and deliver me from the overwhelming waters, from the hand of foreigners,
144.8: whose mouths speak emptiness and whose right hand is a hand of deception.
144.9: I will sing a new song to you, O God; on a ten-stringed instrument I will sing your praise,
144.10: who gives victory to kings and rescues his servant David from the deadly sword.
144.11: Deliver me and rescue me from the hand of foreigners, whose mouths speak emptiness and whose right hand is a hand of deception.
144.12: May our sons be like vigorous plants in their youth; may our daughters be like carved corner pillars of a palace;
144.13: may our storehouses be full with every kind of supply; may our flocks produce thousands and ten thousands in our open country;
144.14: may our cattle be well-fitted for labour; may there be no breach and no exile, no cry of distress in our streets;
144.15: Blessed are the people for whom it is so; blessed are the people whose God is the LORD.

145.1: I will exalt you, my God and King, and bless your name forever and ever.
145.2: Every day I will bless you and praise your name forever and ever.
145.3: The LORD is great and highly to be praised; his greatness no one can search out.
145.4: One generation will commend your works to the next and declare your mighty deeds.
145.5: I will speak of the splendor of your glorious majesty and of your wonderful deeds.
145.6: People will speak of the power of your awe-inspiring acts, and I will tell of your greatness.
145.7: They will overflow with praise for your great goodness and sing aloud of your righteousness.
145.8: The LORD is gracious and compassionate, slow to anger and rich in steadfast love.
145.9: The LORD is good to all; his compassion extends over all he has made.
145.10: All your works will praise you, LORD, and your devoted ones will bless you.
145.11: They will speak of the glory of your kingdom and tell of your power,
145.12: to make known to all humanity your mighty deeds and the majestic glory of your kingdom.
145.13: Your kingdom is an everlasting kingdom, and your dominion endures through all generations.
145.14: The LORD upholds all who are falling and lifts up all who are brought low.
145.15: The eyes of all look to you, and you give them their food in due season.
145.16: You open your hand and satisfy the desire of every living thing.
145.17: The LORD is righteous in all his ways and faithful in all his works.
145.18: The LORD is near to all who call on him, to all who call on him in sincerity.
145.19: He fulfills the desire of those who fear him; he hears their cry and saves them.
145.20: The LORD guards all who love him, but he will destroy all the wicked.
145.21: My mouth will speak the praise of the LORD, and let all living things bless his holy name forever and ever.

146.1: Praise the LORD! Praise the LORD, O my soul!
146.2: I will praise the LORD all my life; I will sing praises to my God as long as I live.
146.3: Do not put your trust in princes or in any human being, for there is no salvation in them.
146.4: When his breath departs, he returns to the earth; on that very day his plans come to nothing.
146.5: Blessed is the one whose help is the God of Jacob, whose hope is in the LORD his God,
146.6: who made heaven and earth, the sea and everything in them, and who keeps faith forever;
146.7: who secures justice for the oppressed and gives food to the hungry. The LORD sets prisoners free;
146.8: the LORD gives sight to the blind, the LORD lifts up those who are bowed down, the LORD loves the righteous.
146.9: The LORD watches over the alien resident; he supports the fatherless and the widow; but the way of the wicked he overturns.
146.10: The LORD will reign forever — your God, O Zion, for all generations. Praise the LORD!

147.1: Praise the LORD! For it is good to sing praises to our God; it is pleasant and fitting.
147.2: The LORD rebuilds Jerusalem and gathers together the exiles of Israel.
147.3: He heals the brokenhearted and bandages their wounds.
147.4: He counts the stars by number and calls each one by name.
147.5: Great is our Lord and mighty in power; his understanding has no limit.
147.6: The LORD lifts up the humble but brings the wicked down to the ground.
147.7: Sing to the LORD with thanksgiving; make music to our God on the harp.
147.8: He covers the sky with clouds, prepares rain for the earth, and makes grass grow on the hills.
147.9: He gives the animals their food and feeds the young ravens when they call.
147.10: He takes no delight in the strength of the horse, nor pleasure in the swiftness of a man.
147.11: but the LORD delights in those who fear him, in those who hope in his steadfast love.
147.12: Praise the LORD, O Jerusalem! Praise your God, O Zion!
147.13: For he has reinforced the bars of your gates and blessed your children within you.
147.14: He establishes peace within your borders and satisfies you with the finest wheat.
147.15: He sends his command to the earth; his word runs swiftly.
147.16: He spreads snow like a blanket of wool and scatters frost like ashes.
147.17: He hurls down hailstones like bread crumbs — who can withstand his cold?
147.18: He sends his word and melts them; he makes his wind blow and the waters flow.
147.19: He has declared his word to Jacob, his statutes and ordinances to Israel.
147.20: He has not done this for any other nation; they do not know his ordinances. Praise the LORD!

148.1: Praise the LORD! Praise the LORD from the heavens; praise him in the heights above!
148.2: Praise him, all his angels! Praise him, all his heavenly armies!
148.3: Praise him, sun and moon; praise him, all shining stars!
148.4: Praise him, you highest heavens, and you waters above the skies.
148.5: Let them praise the name of the LORD, for he commanded and they came into being.
148.6: He has fixed them in their places forever and ever; he has issued a decree that cannot be revoked.
148.7: Praise the LORD from the earth, you sea monsters and all ocean depths,
148.8: fire and hail, snow and mist, stormy winds that carry out his command,
148.9: mountains and all hills, fruit trees and all cedars,
148.10: wild beasts and all livestock, creeping things and flying birds,
148.11: kings of the earth and all peoples, princes and all rulers of the earth,
148.12: young men and young women together, the aged and children —
148.13: let them praise the name of the LORD, for his name alone is exalted; his glory is above earth and sky.
148.14: He has raised high the strength of his people, the praise of all his devoted ones, of the children of Israel, the people near to him. Praise the LORD!

149.1: Praise the LORD! Sing a new song to the LORD, his praise in the assembly of the faithful.
149.2: Let Israel rejoice in their Maker; let the people of Zion be joyful in their King.
149.3: Let them praise his name with dancing and make music to him with tambourine and lyre.
149.4: For the LORD takes pleasure in his people; he adorns the humble with victory.
149.5: Let the faithful exult in his glory; let them sing for joy even on their beds.
149.6: with the high praises of God in their mouths and a double-edged sword in their hands,
149.7: to execute vengeance on the nations and bring judgment on the peoples,
149.8: to bind their kings in chains and their nobles in iron fetters,
149.9: to carry out on them the judgment that is written — this is the honor of all his faithful ones. Praise the LORD!
